### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T04:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92069>

I've been experimenting recently with using additional magnets. I love my Magnetico, and I want to get a similar benefit during the day. So I purchased a lifting magnet, used a compass and labeled which end was North, and placed it just behind my computer chair, on the floor, north side up toward the ceiling.

[http://www.harborfreight.com/retrieving-magnet-150-lb-pull-36904.html](http://www.harborfreight.com/retrieving-magnet-150-lb-pull-36904.html)

Originally I used a magnet with a 250 lb pull, but it seemed to be too strong. Parts of my back, rear end, or elbow would get a buzzing/numb feeling, and I would feel stiff when I got out of the chair, like after a long car ride. I ended up moving the magnet further away from my chair, then back closer, etc. I haven't yet found a perfect spot for it. But still I feel more energetic during the day, and I tackled some jobs I had been putting off. Oh, and dark chocolate and pecans aren't calling to me anymore. Woot! It may also be helping with my back, because I can feel the same slight tingle in the small of my back that I feel when grounding. Love it.

Now I am using the 150 lb magnet in the link above. It may not be strong enough for this application. I may have to combine it with other magnets. I have no idea what gauss it is. I check it with the Trifield meter, but the reading varies wildly from 10-20 gauss to 0. I get about the same reading for the 250 lb magnet, oddly enough.

About a week ago I put several strips of magnets (trimmed from a too-large Magnetico pad) under my parent's sofa.

And I have just put a couple smaller, disc-shaped magnets under the seat of my car today. I think I need a couple more. This is fun.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T21:05:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92258>

Hi sjoshua, that's a really good question. I hadn't tried comparing the two like that.

I have just taken both magnets to the fridge door, but I wasn't able to tell much, I think the slick surface and thin metal was to blame. So I took them on the porch to an old outdoor couch with a metal frame made of rusty iron. The 250# magnet was able to lift the end of the couch a bit. The 150# magnet was able to tip up a corner of it. So, based on this highly scientific experiment, yeah, I'd say that one was about half as strong as the other. ;-)

I'm able to feel the difference in the two, actually, using my throat as a guide. Since I started taking iodine last year, my throat gets a "tight" or heavy feeling, and I've discovered that it does this when I'm in a higher EMF field. Weird, I know, but there you have it. The feeling goes completely away when I'm out in the country. And I discovered that the magnetico gets rid of it as well. Which has spurred further experimentation. And my back feels it also, giving a bit of a tingle when near the magnets. I don't feel anything in my back from regular EMF, however, so it is only positive feedback.

Currently I have the 150# right behind my chair, and the 250# is about 2 feet back from the chair. I have to trade off an uncomfortable throat if they are too far away, with discomfort in my hips if they are too close (and sleeplessness). Last night I had extreme difficulty sleeping, so I will move the 250# a bit further away. The 150 by itself isn't enough for me, according to my throat.

Oh, hey. I could get a second 150# magnet...
Like I said, fun with magnets.

If you want me to do something else to test the magnets, please let me know. Just don't ask me to lift 150#! I wish the magnetico needle would stay still and give a consistent result.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-14T00:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92418>

Oh this is Awesome!!! I found a way to reduce CT shivering!!!!! For me, at least. I took my 250# magnet into the bathroom, put it on it's side on a shelf about 3 ft from the tub, with North pointing toward me. It was about level with the top of the tub. During my bath (which started at about 35F and ended an hour later at about 51F) I noticed that I was shivering less than I expected. It must be the magnet?! I had some 'distant' tremors, but that's about it. This definitely warrants more research!

I was surprised to find that my throat felt a bit tight half way through the bath. But I was laying on my back, and I think the tub blocked the magnetism. It's a metal tub covered with porcelain. So next time I'll put it on top of the counter.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-15T03:37:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92584>

I've been using these magnets in my car:
[http://www.harborfreight.com/set-of-2-magnetic-decor-hooks-65528.html](http://www.harborfreight.com/set-of-2-magnetic-decor-hooks-65528.html)
Of the two 65 # magnets in each package, one has the shiny metal on the N side, the other has metal on the S side. I put two of them under my seat in my car, with N up. Then I drove around a bit (hey, how did you know I was taking another trip to Harbor Freight?) and noticed a large amount of relief from EMF. My throat barely noticed the high power lines, but it got upset in a downtown area. Today I added a third magnet of the same type, and it seemed to do the trick. Even driving near the airport. And I also didn't get the munchies.

Yesterday I had a small amount of stiffness with 2 magnets, but today, with 3, I was fine. Perhaps I'm getting used to it.

I have the magnets laying on the floor, in a haphazard placement, about a foot or two from the outside wall, N toward the ceiling. They are within a couple feet of the meter.

I was using two of the 150# magnets from Harbor Freight this morning when I noticed the effect (or lack of one). I later added a third magnet, a 250# magnet. The meter is almost between 2 rooms, so I put one magnet in one room, and 2 in the other.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-15T21:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92669>

Today I went to the chiropractor. It's been over two weeks since I saw him last. He said my lower back is doing very well today. He seemed a bit surprised. One manipulation that used to hurt badly is no issue now.

My back has been feeling quite good lately, but it's nice to get some confirmation that I haven't caused any harm with my experiments. I was a bit concerned about the temporary soreness in my hips/lower back when sitting near the magnets. But it looks like it may have helped.

And on the way to the chiro, I noticed a bit of a twinge in my throat, so took the small magnet from the passenger seat and added it to my collection under my seat. That did the trick for the way back. Now I have four 65# ones. It's nice, now that I'm back home, to walk around freely without discomfort.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-15T23:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92686>

I start to get the munchies, and sure enough, it is about 3pm. (I had removed the magnets from near my chair.) I did have lunch. So I lay down on my Magnetico bed for a bit, and bingo, not hungry. Sorry, I didn't check the time, but it was about 5 minutes. I couldn't stay there long, things to do, so I grabbed a medium-sized magnet (150#) and took it with me to do laundry (set it on the floor). Now it is by my chair. Not hungry a bit.

So perhaps, for now, I could put the magnets away from me until I get hungry in the afternoon, then use them a couple hours. And put away after dinner. I'm usually fine in the evenings.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-16T01:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-92700>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=92691)
>
> I am wondering about a DIY mini pillow or even a sleep pad. Anybody has any thoughts about it?

When you DIY, you have to be careful about pole orientation... you want the north poles of the magnets facing your body. However, even that may be problematic with a small mini pillow or pad, because if the magnet spacing is not optimal (or the size of the pad is smaller than your body), you will be exposed to the south return field. Exposure to the south return field, while stimulating in the short term, is not beneficial (and could be detrimental) in the medium/long run. This is explained in detail on the Magnetico website. I also recall Dr. K mentioning that he expended a lot of time, effort and ducats DIY'ing this in the beginning, and ended up going with Magnetico after all was said and done.

[http://www.themattresslady.com/purity\_magnetic\_mattress\_pads\_001203.html](http://www.themattresslady.com/purity_magnetic_mattress_pads_001203.html)**

Purity Magnetic Mattress Pads

Purity magnetic mattress pads tried to ride the magnetic-healing wave, and failed. Whether you believe in magnetic healing or not, Purity's magnetic mattress pads didn't succeed. Was it their approach? Check it out and decide for yourself.
When shopping for "purity" magnetic mattress pads, keep in mind that you are seeking magnetic mattress pads made by Purity Products. These magnetic mattress pads are hard to find because Purity Products no longer makes them.

Purity magnetic mattress pads enjoyed brief popularity during the 2000 to 2003 period. As magnetic mattress pads go, the Purity magnetic mattress pads were not the most powerful on the market. Purity Products advertised that its magnetic mattress pads contained "domino-sized, 3,950-gauss, magnetic north, negative north," and the magnet count was similar to other vendors. The Purity magnetic mattress pads weighed between 20 and 40 pounds depending on the size of the mattress. However, the "domino-sized" magnets were only one-third as thick as other magnets, greatly diminishing the penetration of the magnetic field.

But does it matter if the Purity magnetic mattress pads are inferior to others on the market? Not if all magnetic mattress pads fail to work. There is great skepticism about the efficacy of Purity magnetic mattress pads or any other brand.

The theory behind Purity magnetic mattress pads is that properly aligned magnetic fields provide a myriad of health benefits including reduced inflammation, increased blood circulation, pain relief, prevention and even reversal of infection, promotion of metabolic processing of toxins, support for the healing process, and general restoration of natural energy. Exactly how Purity magnetic mattress pads achieve these results is unclear.

Researchers identify three types of magnetic fields: a sedating, healing effect is stimulated by North pole magnetic fields; an energizing, activating effect is achieved with South pole magnetic fields; and bipolar fields stimulate blood circulation and control pain in conditions on or near the skin's surface. Note that the Purity magnetic mattress pads contain North pole field magnets. Indeed, magnetic mattress pads in general seek to envelope the sleeper in a North pole field.

The magnets in Purity magnetic mattress pads are not your ordinary refrigerator magnets. Instead, they are made of ceramics composes of clay and iron. These magnets are not as powerful as those made from rare earths such as samarium, cobalt, or neodymium, but they are not nearly as expensive either.

Purity magnetic mattress pads may contain up to 1,000 magnets and cost around $200. You may still be found on eBay, Craigslist, and other after-market or liquidation Web sites.

A few words of caution are in order for anyone buying Purity magnetic mattress pads, or any other brand. Persons with implanted medical devices, such as pacemakers or insulin pumps, should avoid magnetic mattress pads as they can disrupt the operation of these critical medical devices. Laptop computers, iPods, and other devices containing magnetic storage media should be kept away fro Purity magnetic mattress pads. Credit cardsâ€™ magnetic strips can be permanently damaged if they come close to Purity magnetic mattress pads.

If you decide to buy Purity magnetic mattress pads, try to find a seller who offers a money-back guarantee of at least 30 days. Purity magnetic mattress pads may or may not alleviate your health problem, and itâ€™s good to have a guarantee just in case.

### JanSz — 2013-08-24T04:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-93577>

> I am assuming that credit cards will/may get destroyed when put on top of bed with Magnetico pads, true/false?
> When bed have 20 gauss pads, what is the safe distance for credit cards, high, low, around the sides?
> Any other items, that may get damaged, computer, cell phone, else????

Hi Jan,
Thanks for the follow up questions. The magnetic field extends approx 4-5 feet from the edges of the sleep pads. We don't recommend having a computer any closer as we can not guarantee that the information on the computer will be safe. Technically the same goes with other electronic stored devices.
Having said that, I know my family personally has put their wallet, cell phone on top of the bed as well as on the night table beside the bed with no adverse effects however it is possible that the magnets in the sleep pad could damage to wipe out stored information. If you chose to place things on the bed you would do so at your own risk. What I have found is that if my cell phone is beside the bed, the magnetic field wears down the battery charge sooner.
I hope you find this information helpful

Kind regards,
Andrea

Andrea Morash
Customer Service
tel#800-265-1119
[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
Magnetico Sleep Pads

### nonchalant — 2013-08-26T17:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-93823>

My time out in the country had mixed results. I really, seriously missed my Magnetico bed. I tried placing a small (65#) magnet under the bed near my feet, but I had to get up after an hour and remove it. My lower legs slowly started to feel a bit like a weight was compressing them. And a bit of a twist as well. The sensation went away about 15 minutes after removing the magnet. DH didn't seem to notice the magnet at all. I didn't sleep well, and woke up HUNGRY! When the cat came inside in the morning, she loved laying on the bed, with the magnet back in place. She would lay a foot or two away from it and snooze the day away. And since the magnet was on the floor, she didn't object to walking near it.

During the day, I placed a larger magnet (150#) under my kitchen chair, and I placed a similar one on the floor near the sink. It seemed to make washing dishes and cooking less of a chore at the end of the day. The one under the chair helped, as usual, with hunger or tiredness.

I had a wonderful time, working in the sun, walking around barefoot, bird watching, going for spring water. But next time the single-layer magnetico is coming with us.

Edit: And the trip back was a lot less draining with some magnets under the car seat.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-29T04:23:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-94129>

My neighbor's smart meter is near our bedroom. I have been noticing that DH will occasionally twitch, or flinch, during his sleep. He will do it several times per night, but it doesn't wake him up. He is on the magnetico, but not the grounding cloth. I don't notice anything myself at the time he twitches. I was a bit hesitant to put magnets near that wall, because of interaction with the magnetico field, but finally I just did it. I spaced two 150# magnets about two feet from each other, plus another smaller magnet. All with north facing the ceiling, right up against the wall. The magnets are between the meter and our bed, as well as the furniture allows.

It has been three nights, and he hasn't been flinching. The magnets are staying. Perhaps some day I'll even tell him about it.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-30T05:07:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-94299>

Audrey, you could try it. I was ok if the magnet was a couple feet away from the bed. But I doubt it would have any effect at that distance, since it was a small magnet.
You would need the South side up, right? (She is in the southern hemisphere.) It's cheap, anyway.

Today, however, during CT I checked with a ball compass the effect of the large magnet. A couple feet away from the magnet, the compass went back to pointing to true north. However, when I was in the tub, I was a bit farther away. When I held the compass above the center of the tub, it did not point to the magnet. So the beneficial effect of the magnet went further than the compass could detect. I was surprised.

Perhaps you don't have to have the magnet so close?

### nonchalant — 2013-08-30T20:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-94364>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=92069)
>
> About a week ago I put several strips of magnets (trimmed from a too-large Magnetico pad) under my parent's sofa.

The Magnetico pads under my parents couch seems to be helping. My father lays on that couch every day, and he has been much more energetic. My mother, however, was complaining that she was always so tired (she had anemia last year). She usually sits on a different chair, a loveseat. So a couple weeks ago I put the remaining strip of Magnetico magnets (I had been storing water on top of it) under her chair. This week she was much more energetic. She had even done some sewing, which she loves. She hadn't touched any sewing in such a long time. What a relief! I'm going to put some more magnets that I have trimmed off a twin-sized Magnetico (so that I could put it on one layer on a queen-sized bed) under her chair, so she has more than one strip under her.

I was hesitant, at first, to put magnets under their furniture, because they both have Parkinson's. I figured they probably have lots of heavy metals to detox, and they might experience side effects. So I first put the magnets under a couch they use infrequently. Both of them did try out that sofa for an hour or so, with no issues. But that sofa got almost no use in over a month, so I ended up moving the magnets to their current location.

I am very pleased with their physical reactions. Of course, their new energy might be because of something else, but I suspect the magnets have a positive effect on their health. Maybe some day they will buy a Magnetico for their bed.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-06T18:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-95038>

Personally, I think that any strength Magnetico will have an effect even if the Magnetico is on the floor and the mattress is far above it. When I stretch up with a compass at the ceiling and it will point toward the bed instead of true north, I know it is affecting the entire room, not just 8-12 inches above it. I don't know if the 5 gauss will have a dramatic visible effect on your son, but I believe it will benefit him greatly.

These other magnets that I am using around the house... I can be out of range of them (at least the compass isn't aware of them) yet I still perceive a benefit from them. I feel less tired, get less hungry, feel less cold.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-06T21:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-95072>

Cantweight, I must tell you, however about the twin-sized magnetico I recently bought off craigslist. The woman has an autistic child. I really wanted the pad, but I felt obligated to ask if she was sure about selling it. She said it didn't work for her child, though others in her group had marvelous success with the magnetico for their autistic children. I asked if she had tried a ketogenic diet, grounding, or other approaches, and she said yes. She was selling the pad to get money for other therapies her Naturalist is recommending.

I think for some people the magnetico is the magic pill. For others, perhaps they need a combination of 3-4 things. I thought she probably should have kept the pads, but I felt I had been rude enough, me a stranger questioning her about her child. I'm sure Dr. K would have not hesitated to be rude. ;-)

I don't like handling magnetico pads with the south side out, my fingers will start to hurt a bit. And they weigh so much, you just have to grab them up with the south side right next to your heart. I always lay down on the finished bed for a bit, to mitigate the effect of the south side. And I hold a small magnet in my hands for a few minutes, to heal my joints.

She said her son used the pads for several months, with no improvement. So I guess she just didn't see any behavior changes. I think with the wrong side up you would see real energy problems within a week.

I didn't ask your specific question Joe, but I did ask Magnetico about splitting up a king pad (since it comes in two vertical pieces anyway) and using those on twin beds and they said that was fine. The only problem that we may have had with it is that the king pad is a few inches longer and the lady from Magnetico said if the pad was somewhat folded at the end of the bed tha magnets may have pushed through the foam and torn it and then the 6-month warranty would have been void. ### nonchalant — 2013-09-09T21:38:11+0300

I watched a short youtube video on the Hall Effect, so now I'm an expert. ;-)
Perhaps the Hall effect is the reason putting a large magnet up on the bathroom counter, turned on its side so that the N pole is pointed towards me in the tub, keeps me warmer during CT? Months ago I used to run a ground wire into the bathroom and attach it to a wrist strap, but I stopped that after a while because I didn't feel a benefit. However, I have recently tried grounding again during CT, with the addition of the magnet. Yeah, I felt a benefit! I had, for once, zero difficulties laying my upper back into the water, though it was at 32.5F. \[My upper back is always reluctant. I usually have to close my eyes and think of sunshine before I can lay back.\]

Today I tried it again, to be sure it wasn't a fluke. Put the strap on my ankle this time. Looks like the ground wire and the large magnet are going to be a permanent addition to my CT, as essential as the ice cubes.

Ok, I confess, I don't know much about the Hall Effect and if the ground wire enters into the equation. But I like it.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T16:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-95446>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=95378)
>
> I thought someone said you shouldn't spend a lot of time during the day near the pad. It occurred to me at the time - in a child's or teenager's room when they spend a lot of time in their room it wouldn't be good at all. Maybe someone will remember where those posts are?
> the magnetico thread?

The problem is that the magnetico pad is up off of the floor, so you get the opposite charge to your lower legs and feet. So you shouldn't spend a lot of time near the pad, unless you are on top of the bed. So you can spend all day in bed.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-01T17:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96606>

I think the most important thing about magnets is to buy a ball compass first. When I buy magnets they stay in the trunk on the way home and I don't touch them much until I get the North side labeled. My hands will hurt if I hold the south side of a small magnet for more than a minute or so.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-01T17:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96608>

> [Awainer1 said:](/goto/post?id=96603)
>
> Nicld-
>
> I have a 150 lb harbor freight magnet under my mattress. It replaced the earthpulse. First couple nights with it are weird but after that Ive had the best most restorative sleep ever. Also have a 100 lb under my car seat but dont really feel any effects. But ya give it a couple nights and then see. Im ready to crank it up to the 250#.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-01T17:30:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96610>

> [Awainer1 said:](/goto/post?id=96604)
>
> Oh ya and the way to tell which is N. Both my magnets I got the sticker was on the north side but a compass' red arrow will point away from it since like poles will be pushed apart.

No, they label the arrow on the compass (that is actually on it's south side) as N, since it is attracted to the North pole. The N side of a compass is always attracted to the North pole.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-01T18:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96616>

A funny thing happened this weekend. Our cat, who was at first spooked by the south field surrounding our magnetico bed, was found sleeping under the bed! In the south field. I was astonished. I ran to get my trusty compass and verified that north was up on that bed, on both sides. No, I can't explain it.

In the past few weeks she has gotten attached to the bed, sleeping for hours on it during the day. She wouldn't spend much time on the floor near the bed, but she knew that is was comfy on top. Or she will be on DH's recliner, which has a couple magnets on the floor underneath. I was happy because she was eating less liver and fish and cream and such.

I'll watch to see if she repeats that behavior.

### nicld — 2013-10-01T19:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96620>

Ok, so do I put the magnet under the mattress or is just under the bed ok? I will have to stop and get a compass or see if I can nab the one from our RV but in the meantime (like now at work), which way should have the sticker facing. From what I gather the side with the sticker, if it is north should be facing north, correct. What about a round one?

### nonchalant — 2013-10-01T20:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96623>

If you bought the 95# round ones with a small hole in the center, shiny metal on one side, and it is flat (you can put a mug on top of it), then the shiny metal side is North. I check all mine to make sure, but every one has been oriented that way.

For the larger magnets with white stickers...it's less certain. Most of my 150# magnets have the sticker on the north side, but I have one that is the reverse of that.

I've never put one of those magnets under the mattress. I had a difficult enough time with it just on the floor.

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-01T21:00:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-96626>

Nonchalant....this is what I understood from speaking with the Magnetico folks....animals will not be in the south field if they are under the bed....only if they are in the area out to the sides. The positive spikes going down to the floor would be absorbed by the box spring and then any critter under the bed would be in a negative field like we are lying on top of the mattress. My 12 year old standard poodle loves to sleep under the bed and he is still as active and athletic as he was a 2.

There is a good picture in the literature and in their pamphlet that shows you how there is a negative field underneath the bed. Animals are too smart to do the wrong thing....not like us are they???

Once I get the money, then it is on!!!

I will give it about a week before I take the magnet away. Even the DD seemed a bit more agitated last night since I stuck a 95# one under her chair. I have read several times on this forum that it's important to expose oneself to the north pole of a magnet and avoid the south side. If that is true, you may be in severe danger. If your compass points AT your Magnetico from above, it means that you actually sleep on the south pole of it.

I was wondering if the magnet magic was going away, but then when putting things away I discovered that I had connected my grounding strap and long wire to my bed grounding sheet, LOL! ### nonchalant — 2013-10-24T17:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-98855>

I removed my 150# magnet from under my chair last night. I have been having some hip pain (which was a big problem years ago) that just won't go away. This morning my hip seems better. A few minutes after removing the magnet, though, I started feeling warm. This chair has a microfiber upholstery which traps body heat. A nearby ceiling fan came to the rescue. I am positive that for me, magnets allow me to be comfortable in high or low temperatures.

I think my hip pain started the combination of my grounded computer and my grounding sheet on my chair, over a period of several months. It was also recently affecting my shoulder and elbow.

I still have magnets on the floor near the sink and stove, and under the car seats. And I wouldn't CT without one.
At least maybe I won't have to fight the cat for my chair any more. There is still a small one under DH's chair, she can be content with that. And my Magic Magnetico never irritates my hip.

Hope I don't have to fight the munchies again. That was the main incentive for the magnet in the first place.

Perhaps some day I will be able to transfer the energy more efficiently. Working on those nanotubes!

### Inger — 2013-12-06T19:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-102602>

Sally I did not even think about me being cold adapted (because I am NOT sitting an hour in ice water like Nicole..huh! ) until I talked to my brothers girlfriend the other day. She told me they have so cold a bedroom! She always freeze in the night. I asked if she keeps the window open, but she said no... They live in a 2 room apartment. I guess they have around 15-18 degree C or more... so way warmer than my close to freezing temps (even below if cold nights) and she said she have to use wool socks and pajamas and she still is cold. Then I realized I really must be cold adapted to some degree. It is like fueling on fat. It is so natural after years on that WOE you really forget normally people feel bad if they do not get their CH. You get so used to how you live - weird to realize it is pretty different than most

Was driving pretty far with hubs today to buy some organic hay for our sheep and we stopped at a fuel station where was also bistro, pizzeria MC Donalds style.. I walked through the room full of people eating to come to the bathroom and I watched them.... and it was so weird... I have not been in such a place for so long and now it felt like coming to another planet? So weird things I saw in peoples face. Interesting experience. maybe it was a little sad

I just started to feel way too warm. I wondered how is it so warm? No heating on at all? I watched the temps and it was 18 degree C. Holy moly I have now opened the balcony door to get some cold in. ahhhh amazing fresh air. I have no heating on at all but hubs have it so warm his side of the house! And his freaking Sauna is poisoning me...  When he is away I keep it cool in both apartments. nice and cool and fresh.

### nonchalant — 2014-03-10T04:31:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-114453>

When the magnet is lying on the floor, north side up and south touching the floor or carpet, the positive field is directed under your house, while the negative field is directed upwards. So as long as the south side of the magnet is not elevated above your floor, you should be fine.

I don't know if anyone besides myself would get bad effects from small magnets. I do know that occasional use (such as when I CT for an hour) does not give me any side effects.

Hey all, I wanted to ask a question about this. Is there anything one can do to reverse this, or at least slow this down? I'm not certain on all the details because I don't stay in contact with this side of my family, and they've also had 'scares' before with other things that turned out not to be true. Regardless of the relationship and what not, I must help when I can. I'll attach a photo of her brain (my aunt) to this post. I exchanged emails with her daughter about a lot of things I've learned and delved into ketones, paleo diets, mct oil, tDCS, neurofeedback, yada yada yada. I sent a lot of clinical studies her way and I also posted a few of Dr. Kruse's blogs that delve into disorders of the brain (Is your protein bent/How to avoid the fates of Michael J Fox) and a couple of the CT blogs and Brain Gut 6. I sent a lot of Dr. Mary T. Newport's presentations as well - my cousin bought Upgraded Brain Octane Oil and Upgraded MCT Oil, and they stocked up on coconut oil. I tried to stress that all facets need to be looked at - the whole diet spectrum (not just including coconut oil), starting to move, doing brain exercises, looking up more practitioners, looking into advanced brain therapies, etc. I spoke about cold thermogenesis and even hinted towards EMFs. After seeing the picture of the brain my dad asked me to ask on the forums I go to because I was going on and on about Dr. Kruse and how apt he is to help his members on here and how knowledgeable he is. So, basically anything that is said here I will send his way (unless it is really really negative) and he can forward it to her. I'm going to try and get my dad to get her to seek out these forums as well.

I was assuming the starting point would be something like this:
1) Change diet ASAP (vegetarian-SAD diet currently) to epi-paleo and probably loaded in MCTs
2) Do lot's and lot's of CT
3) Maybe throw in the initial recommended supplements
4) Start moving and using the brain a lot (from some of the smart life forums presentations on AD and Dementia, physical exercise trumps mental exercise in warding of brain disease, especially vascular dementia)
5) Seek out additional testing, imaging, and therapies

Anything would be helpful!

# Thread: GERD going
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T17:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/post-92103>

A very interesting thing happened yesterday with DH. He forgot to take his morning Nexium. This does not happen very often. I noticed at about 1pm that he hadn't taken it, and made an executive decision (yay dopamine!) to not tell him. I hesitated, because he suffers quite a bit when he gets heartburn, and he doesn't get true relief the entire day. But I knew that a year ago he would have realized his mistake in mid morning, more recently he wouldn't get tummy problems until the afternoon, and once lately not until evening. This morning I saw that he still hadn't taken yesterday's pill, though he did take today's. He has no complaints about heartburn.

This is significant, imho. I'm not sure what has caused this improvement. His diet hasn't changed much, though recently he has agreed to cut down on commercial fried foods (but I fry stuff for him at home in CO, bacon fat, etc.) For the last year maybe he has slightly reduced his wheat consumption. He has slightly increased his seafood consumption this year. I have found HFCS free catsup, sweet relish, & BBQ sauce for him. I make his salad dressing. He haphazardly takes chewable enzymes with meals.

Other factors are that the fluids he is drinking are non-fluoridated, for the most part. I pack a quart and a half of tea in his lunch so that he doesn't drink as much of what's available at the work place. I use some parts of the Mitochondria Rx in his weekday morning shake. Also he is used to having low lighting at night. And we have been sleeping on the Magnetico for 2 months. His sleep has improved; he falls asleep within 5 minutes of hitting the mattress. But he still wakes up in the middle of the night sometimes. He doesn't want my grounding sheet on his side because it curls up, but he often crowds my side now.  He hasn't increased his sun exposure, but he always has a fairly good tan. He has been wearing shorts more often this past couple years, and lately he has decided that he doesn't dislike sandals anymore. He goes barefoot a little more often.

From a tantalizing hint Dr. K gave out a while ago, I'd guess that it is the better water that is helping him the most. If he decides to kick the Nexium habit, I will be SO relieved. He has been taking prescription drugs for heartburn for over a decade. I imagine his body would really like some more protein that is actually digested first, for a change. I tried before to wean him from Nexium using Betaine HCL, but it didn't go well. I guess you really have to want change...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-11T18:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/post-92108>

Photoelectric effect, water, and magnetism.......3 legged stools needed for life evolved on this planet. So far your hubby is apply two of three.

Thanks for the reply, Jack. Yes, I've been contemplating your three-legged stool. I think I'll up his exposure to the 3 factors. Perhaps put a small magnet under his recliner.

I forgot to mention that he hasn't had sunblock (with a few exceptions) for about a year. That has helped.

### Nathan — 2013-08-11T21:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-optimal.7866/post-92137>

Hello! I am so glad to have found Dr. Kruse's website. I have been dealing with health issues for the past 8+ years despite avoiding most processed foods for most of my life. A year ago I was diagnosed with Lyme disease and Candida among other things and have been working towards healing myself ever since. I have made so much progress and reduced nearly all of my symptoms and the main thing I am working on now is improving my sleep. As luck would have it that's actually how I came across this blog. One of my many Googles landed me on a CT blog post and I've been reading away ever since.

I started the Leptin Rx and CT about 2 weeks ago with positive results. I'm actually struggling with being underweight (went from a consistent 170 to <150 since starting treatment of antibiotics and my yeast free diet to deal with the candida. I lost a lot of weight from cutting carbs out of my diet when I went yeast free but once I got rid of the candida I went back to carbs hoping to put back on a few lbs and the carbs just go right through me and I have not been able to gain back any weight....until the Leptin Rx. I've actually gained 4-5 pounds since starting the Leptin Rx so am very happy with that and look forward to the other positive benefits.

Currently on Phase 2 of CT and will be going to Phase 3 very soon. Can't wait to get the benefits of a solid nights sleep.

Also just discovered the effects of blue spectrum light and wearing my blue blockers every night. Trying to reduce EMFs and have ordered some earthing bands and grounds for our laptops.

Anyway, I am very excited and look forward to my continued journey down this new path.

### Nathan — 2013-08-13T06:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-optimal.7866/post-92330>

I did my first CT bath yesterday. I did it for about 20 minutes and it felt good during and afterwards. It was actually easier than I thought it would be. It sure does take a lot of ice to get the bath to temp so I'll need to start making some ice blocks rather than buying bags of ice.

Sleep was no different and I did experience some mild herxing and guess I must have burned some fat and released some toxins. This morning was rough and I felt better as the day went on. I have upped my chlorella and activated charcoal to help deal with the detox effects.

CT is actually something my body has been craving most of my life. I grew up on Lake Ontario and when I was a kid I would always jump in the lake in the dead of winter. Not sure why but something always drew me in. I wouldn't stay in long, more of a jump in and then run into the house and dry off but I always liked that feeling of cold radiating out of my body. Now I know why.

I rented a ski house with some friends a few years back. A few of us were hanging in the hottub after a day of skiing and a buddy and I kept challenging each other to see who could lay in the snow the longest. Of course we had a few drinks in us so that made it easier. I think we each maxed out around 2 minutes. I'll be prepared for the next snow challenge.

I CTed in the tub again tonight and everything started out fine. Was going for 30 minutes and a little over 20 minutes into it I started shivering like crazy. I held out as long as I could and made it 28 minutes before giving in. It took me forever to warm up. I just had uncontrollable shivers for 40+ minutes. Back to normal now.

Time for bed and hoping for a long, deep sleep.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-15T16:21:22+0300

> [Nathan said:](/goto/post?id=92598)
>
> Lahelada and Caroline,
>
> Thanks for the advice. I have been dressing immediately and getting under blankets.

Oh, I was miserable when I did that. Shivering for at least an hour.
Once your skin warms up just a bit, your BAT shuts off, I believe. Then the shivering starts, and the only thing to get you warm is the shivering. And it isn't as effective, but I bet your muscles get a workout!
I CT about 3-4 times a week. More, if I get time on the weekends.

# Thread: RT3 after being on  NT. Advice and help needed.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### karicat — 2013-08-11T22:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92151>

Hi everyone !! I need some advice.

I'm currently on Dzugan's program but I'm concerned because he doesn't test for RT3.
A few years ago, before starting any thyroid meds, my RT3 was off the range (high) and my ft3 was low. I don't have the exact numbers with me right now, but I remember clearly it was something like:
RT3 3.75 (range 1.50-3.50) with a ft3 of 1.9 (range 2.0-4.5)
This RT3 was only lowered with T3 meds.

But when I started with Dr Dzugan last year, they put me on NT. I told them T4 was nasty on my body, and the only time I have felt a truly difference on my symptoms was when I took only T3 (no t4 at all, just straight t3). The difference was like night and day, taking T3 was like waking up after many years, no more brain fog, energy, my mood was 1000 times better, my body was stronger, etc (my sight was also better, that was kind of weird, everything seemed much clearer). The problem is that these effects never lasted long, perhaps a week at the most, and then I started feeling bad again. I still believe that my problem was with the dose, perhaps too high too fast, too low, perhaps I needed a little t4, I still don't know the solution. My body loves the T3 but I don't know how to dose it correctly.

Last year, after being on NT 0.5 grains for a couple of months, I got some labs, and just out of curiosity, tested on my own the RT3. Results were:
Rt3: 2.10 (range 1.50-3.50), free t3 2.0 (range 2.0-4.5)
I really didn't think much about it at the time, and as I was also having side effects from some of the supplements, and perhaps of too much progesterone, I didn't pay the necessary attention to it.

Unfortunately, I've never felt quite good on the program, but I must admit I've had some good days. But for the past few months, I've been feeling worse and worse. I've been making my updates telling them how I was feeling and among other changes, my nt has been increasing (0.5, 1.0 and 1.5).
I was on 1.5 grains for approx two months, when I told them I was really tired, incredibly brain fogged and feeling just awful and even depressed. And as I was feeling so lousy I made an experiment on my own and used some t3 I had left and started taking 0.25 mcg t3 plus de 1.5 on NT (probably too much T3 but I wanted to see what would happen). The same day I took the t3 around noon, I started feeling already better by 5.00 pm. As usual it was amazing, the good mood, the sharp mind, the focus, improved sight. I kept on this dose for 5 days, and then, around the 6th day I started feeling bad again. So I made my new update telling them about my symptoms and my experiment with the T3 and they just raised the NT more (to 2 grains) and no additional t3 of course.

I was on this new dose of NT for 8, 9 days and feeling even worse. And then I remembered about the RT3 test, and wondered if maybe that could be part of the problem. So I tested it a couple of days ago, and the results were:
RT3: 3.45 (range 1.50-3.50) with a ft3 of 2.4 (range 2.0-4.5), for a ratio of 7.
I'm now almost out of range with my RT3.

I'm going to make my update do Dr Dzugan in a little while, but I would like to know what you guys think about this situation. It worries me that they don't test for the RT3 lab, and if it wasn't that I though about testing it, they would have probably given me more NT or kept me at the same dose.

I don't know if I need to stop the NT completely (I think so, when I looked at my journal, it seems that with every higher dose of NT I wasn't feeling better, just slowly getting worse). And I don't know whether to ask them for just t3, or a little T4 with more T3 or what to do. I need someone really skilled on these issues and with the right T3 meds and amounts in order to truly help me and guide me through this process. Clearly the NT is not working and I think it is making me worse. I've always had this problem with the T4, it seems I don't tolerate it well. On the other hand, the T3, when it worked, was like being truly alive for the first time in many, many years.

Any feedback or advice will be greatly appreciated.
Best regards from Lima

Karina

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-11T22:57:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92154>

Karina

I always run Free T4, Free T3 and rT3 as well as TSH when I do labs for Dzugan, I run the labs that help me the most. I always make sure I have the labs I think I need to help me figure out what's actually going on.

If you don't feel good on NT - put that in your update, and comment about how you felt on T3 - you know you the best. ASK for T3 for the next 2-3 months to see how you feel on it. make sure Dzugan knows how you felt when on T3...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-11T23:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92156>

I agree with Gretch. You decided what to run not them. And then when you prove why you are not doing well you help suggest an acceptable change to you both.......When I hear this type of story I suspect an microglia problem.......meaning neurons cant make pregnenolone from cholesterol cause free T3 in brain is low........you might want to try a GABA test to see if you have a leaky BBB.......then for sure you know the gut is likely bad or you have a bad EMF field across your brainstem.........another way to look at this is to look at your progesterone and LH levels.......if both are low I am betting on a field effect and not a diet effect. If this is found.......you also bet phase 2 and 3 detox in liver sucks too.......and your dehydrated inside your cells losing Magnesium all of which make you feel like shit.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T23:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92157>

I have noticed, as the years go by and I progress on my health journey, that when I find something good, I feel the effects only temporarily. I was giddy when I started a low carb, high fat diet. Incredible mood. It lasted about a month. I didn't stop the diet, I just found something else I needed, like the Leptin Rx. And CT, seafood, grounding... all of these (and others) made large jumps in my health, with good mental effects also. But the feelings normalize.

Perhaps stay on T3 and pursue other ideas. It seems that so many different things are wrong with everyone. It's amazing we are alive still.

### karicat — 2013-08-12T00:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92164>

Thank you so much for the quick answers !!

Gretch, I usually run other tests that are not required on Dzugan's panel. But as I have to pay for everything (I'm from outside the US and my insurance in Peru won't pay for any of those labs) sometimes I admit I skip some labs that are not on the panel. And I have sent them these additional labs but I don't know if they even look at them or what. For instance, a couple of months ago, I sent them some labs of progesterone and estradiol taken on the 20th day of my cycle and the estradiol was really low (18 pg ml). the progesterone was also low (0.9 ng/ml). I was on just a small dose of p as I was having a very hard time with it, and no triest. They said nothing at all about this, and it is only a couple of weeks ago when I complained of having incontinence issues and a couple more things, that they have given me a dose of triest.
Perhaps I'm wrong but if they are seeing such low levels on the labs, why wait till I start getting hot flashes or some other symptoms instead of acting faster based on labs.

I used to think if they are the hormone experts why don't they ask me for these other tests if they consider them relevant (like the frees, or reverse t3, estradiol or ferritin, etc). I mean, I try to read everything I can regarding these issues, but surely it is not my main area of expertise and that's why I searched for what I thought was one of the best hormone specialists. I'm only depending on them for my current treatment, as the doctor where they sent me in FL (the one who writes the prescriptions) knows nothing about these problems.

I have already told them many times on my update how I felt with the T3 but I was still given the NT, just more of it.
I think it makes sense asking them for some t3. Thank you for your input

Dr Kruse, you are absolutely right. Now I know better than to wait for someone to adjust my program based on just labs, or random symptoms. I have to start making my own suggestions on what to do and what to change.
I'm going to find out if we have that gaba test available in Lima.
I haven't tested my LH levels for quite a while, but when it was done in the past (because of my period stopping when I was 33), all sex hormones (specially e and p, but also t) were very low as well as the LH. That's why I was told that I had a problem with my pituitary or hypothalamus, it didn't matter which they told me, because there was no cure for it, just replacing hormones.

Nonchalant, I understand what you say, but with the t3 I'm sure it is because of the effects (physical and psychological) it has on my body. The first thryoid replacement I had was t4 and I begged the doc to give it to me thinking it was the answer to my problems. To my surprise it was the opposite, and I felt quite lousy on it. When I tried the T3 (which I didn't have much hope for after my t4 failed experience), I couldn't believe the difference and how I felt on it. It was like a thick fog was lifted almost instantly. And every time I have tried it, at different times in the past few years, it always had the same effect. It is like I reach the so called "sweet spot" most of the times in the first day I take it, only to last around 5 days, and then I go back to the fog and feeling bad. It is interesting now that I think about it that when I was given welbutrin I had a similar experience the first two days, and after that I started feeling my normal self again. When I was given zoloft or paxil I didn't feel anything similar to the good feeling of welbutrin.
But yes, I definitely agree that implementing other changes like CT, seafoood, etc also helps.

Any additional suggestions on what to do with my current thyroid dose (2.0 nt) until I receive my new program ? I'm feeling quite lousy and don't really know what to do in the meantime. Between them reading my update and the Dr in Fl approving it, it might be at least 4 days till I get some answer.

Thank you once again !

Just to let you know about my experience, frustrating really.
Have a nice day everyone !

I am wondering if we could update our successes - specially for new people coming on the forum ....but for us too - of course! I get so many pm's from new people who are mesmerised by all of this - there is so much to take in. All of us have kind of grown together....we know where the bodies are buried - so to speak

I know for me - things just creep up ..... there is so much to think about and take in and I don't think we notice the little changes that are happening.

I have been noticing lately that my arms are alot stronger - just something silly like sitting down on the beach and especially getting up - I do it with ease - now I just kind of hop up! That is big for me!

so - in the spirit of helping others and reminding ourselves about how far we have come.....

Has your sleep improved?
Is your weight where you want it to be?
Have you made progress in limiting EMF's?
Photoelectric effect?
grounding?
water - hydration? C/T?
Are you stronger? Mentally? and physically?
Are you getting the oxytocin effect?

Are you appreciating/seeing the beauty in your life more? in spite all of life's little problems and setbacks

Do you dance even if the only music is in your head?

How have you changed? It is too bad we don't have pictures to document...... well Martin does - but his is in reverse

### nonchalant — 2013-08-13T16:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-update.7870/post-92357>

Caroline, I'll just repost this here.

Well, let me tell you a bit of my story. I came to this place fairly early, and was able to implement each protocol as Dr. Kruse slowly introduced them. With each one, I found some improvement. Some remarkable changes, like my skin texture changing completely for the better, and the scoliosis in my back going away, and sleeping through the night, and excellent hormonal changes just from cold water, and improved vision, and dramatically better night vision, and loss of anxiety, and and interesting mechanism that deadens pain to the skin in a few minutes (like when you run into a stinging plant). What else... Skin that rarely burns, and when it does it is fine the next day. Hearing that doesn't ring. I'm sure I'm forgetting several things.

I came here to lose weight, and I discovered the way to health. And I discovered that it wasn't just one thing that was wrong with my diet/lifestyle. So there isn't one fix. Oh, and the Magnetico? I think it's magic. I believe it is detoxing heavy metals for me. And recently it looks like it is what my body needs, to finally let go of the fat. I'm getting a little giddy recently about it. I like the Magnetico so much I'm working on convincing my elderly parents with Parkinson's to buy one. Do they need to lose weight? No. But they need better health. More energy. And the Magnetico gives energy in spades. Pure energy that the body doesn't have to digest. Energy for anything the brain thinks it needs.

As someone (Jonathan) said on this forum, everybody is at peak physical health, given the situation (field) they are in. That is what the other 90% of our brain is for, maintaining health and function. Your brain has a prioritized Christmas list it is waiting to fill, as soon as it gets the money. When some of us first get the money, we lose weight. Others gain the ability to walk gracefully. (That was another benefit I got from Dr. Kruse's advice.) It just depends on what is needed the most, and I suppose what is easiest to fix.

edit: And plantar faciitis cured! How could I forget being able to walk barefoot again! And relief from occasional constipation. Thanks Dr. K!

### cantweight — 2013-08-13T17:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-update.7870/post-92372>

I'll play Audrey

My successes have come in very different forms from what I had expected and thought that I wanted.

I had already spent years recovering from illnesses I honestly suspect would have killed me. Big scary diagnoses like LUPUS, Lyme, Babesia, Bartonella, parasites, Bi-polar disorder, Hashimoto's, Epstein Barr, PCOS, sleep apnea, Metabolic Syndome, and Fibromyalgia. Symptoms such as migraines that would render me unable to function and took days to recover from ruled my world, periods with so much blood loss that I would end up in the ER were the norm. My gallbladder was removed at the ripe old age of 28 and by 34 I had had 3 surgeries to remove ovarian and peri ovarian cysts. The amount of medication I was taking was staggering. The destruction of my body took years. Years of poorly trained medical experts giving a name and a drug to every group of symptoms my body produced in its effort to scream for help.

I came upon this knowledge bank of people in March of 2012; I spent hours and hours devouring information I had never seen or heard before. By April of 2012 I was waist deep in cold water and learning to like seafood.

My initial results with CT and epi-paleo were life changing. I had the answer to one little chunk of the puzzle. My application of this knowledge was often less than stellar but it was ok because I knew what to do....I had the tools in my bag. As the information kept coming(and coming, and coming) from Dr Kruse the realization that I had only come a fraction of the way towards my goal of true health really pissed me off. "But I have already done so much." constantly echoed in my head.

But its true...when you know better you do better. So I have and it is over a year a later. I have moved to home with low EMF, I spend my days barefoot, I am mindful of exposures and do my best to counter. We have miles to go in regard to artificial light, EMF, circadian rhythm...etc. But we are listening and learning.

Dr Kruse had a facebook post months ago that is still resonating with me....it was about organization, clearing physical clutter, the mind body connection with the state of the home.

My biggest day to day struggle is housework. Why is it a struggle? Mitochondrial dysfunction/dehydration. We moved into this house last June and endured months of renovation. My dining room had become my "hoarder" room. Full of boxes and crap I had no idea what do with and no energy to address. In the past week I have mowed a big chunk of land with a push mower, almost cleared my hoarder room, moved furniture, cleaned out half the garage....and all with little to no recovery time!! This may sound lame but it is huge for me! To have the energy to do it was massive in itself but to not lose 3 days after doing each job is something I have not seen my body do for close to 10 years!!! My body is ready to exercise now. This is what I've been waiting for; working for. I had defeated so many horrible symptoms, restored happiness and calm to my world, retrieved my brain from intense cognitive decline, but never was able to get even an ounce of energy. It took a damn long time to come...but it is here and I am grateful. To comprehend the extent of my body's decline beyond the illnesses and their symptoms and to respect its need to take years to fix what took years to break....success.

My husband learning that he can control his alcohol cravings with CT....another huge success.

Having more knowledge on becoming well than most doctors....success.

Having the tools to raise healthy children in this mess of a world....success.

I have shared this blog and its infinite wisdom too many times to count. Most people smile and nod politely when I start in on it all. Eventually they glaze over and I realize I have lost them.

One mom I met last fall has a son with an ADD/ADHD diagnosis and had him on multiple meds. I shared this info and at times feared I had pushed her away with my "nagging" about his diet. Two weeks ago we sat by my pool and with eyes filled talked about how her son is off a med and doing so well, how the whole family is calmer and happier than ever. She thanked me. That is my biggest success.

I had initially expected my success to come in a pair of size 6 skinny jeans. My vision was narrow. My eyes are open now.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-06T10:18:22+0300

### Albert83BCN — 2013-08-12T09:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-problems.7872/post-92222>

Technology RX #1 - Why do we get cached?

Technically, this is a compromise that many forums -and extensible to other kind of websites- take to optimize access. Each time a webserver receives a request to get a page (from your internet browser) it must be fetched from the database, get rendered into HTML (the hypertext format document that you are just now reading via the browser) and send it back to you. This consumes some processor and memory from the server, and if the database gets hit too much, performance goes down, and if the hardware capabilities of the server are less than the number of requests, the website slows down.

So what many web solutions do is caching the websites that have been requested once, so that they are kept in memory and when another (or the same) user requests it again, the page is directly sent to him based upon what was sent last time, if the server thinks that the document is the same. Problem is, the same document (same means same URL on your browser) can change (and this happens a lot in the forums) and then you get a response that is old. This is a minor drawback that the forum accepts in order to ramp up performance. This implies that the unaware user might think that his post has been deleted or not posted, that's not necessarily the case, it's there but you're still not seeing it because you get an old version of the thread until the server deletes the old cache and refreshes it with the latest content.

The trick to get the latest content always would be to request the server the full page, this can usually be forced down by hitting the F5 several times or depending on the browser there are some other combinations.

Hope that clarifies the issue!

### caroline — 2013-08-12T11:11:17+0300

David

I'd caution you - on the survivability of hurricanes here in the US b/c we don't build to the same standards as they do in Oki or Guam to survive typhoons. We haven't seen a Cat 5 make land in the US Since Andrew in 1992. I lived in Guam when Super typhoon Paka came through and set a land speed wind record of 233mph and broke the windometer in the 1st 4 hrs of that lovely storm. building standards are for withstanding both Super Typhoons (Cat 5 hurricanes) and 8.0 earthquakes. in addition to that the land we're building on in the US isn't as stable as coral reef and remnants of volcanic eruptions...

That said - if you're far enough away from the 500 year flood plain and the flood surge - you should be good to go.

We're considering Maine as a possibility, BC north of Vancouver, Utah, upto Yellowstone and on to Glacier national park, as well as Texas. of course we'll be testing, but we're also looking for opportunities to continue being able to pay the bills...

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-26T07:00:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-area-for-relocation.7880/post-93800>

To bring back this topic, I have a question - is Poland a good location to live, based on any criteria (EMFs and magnetism primarily). I've been taking the whole 'field' idea seriously as of late in my quest to heal myself. In my honest opinion, I think air should be added to the list (sunshine, water, magnetism), since we are aerobic organisms and anyhow many people breath poorly as it is.

The reason I ask this is, my fiance has family out in Poland. We may be able to live with them for free if we were to move out there (or at least very cheaply). I'm worried because of my current HPPD symptoms, but I've got ways to keep that under control; and if I were to relocate to a better 'field', it may help me further. I know that I would be within long walking/hiking/short driving distance to a nice beach, but I'm not sure about the magnetism aspect of Poland. From my knowledge of my fiances family out there, it doesn't seem like everyone has 2-3 or more computers/laptops in a home in addition to smartphones and ipads. For instance, my fiances cocia (if that's how you spell it, it means grandma) calls off of a land line phone rather than cell phone, and her siblings/nephews can only communicate through skype off of a older desktop computer.

Education is paid for by the government out there, as well. I would be able to go to the same college as my fiances sister who got her PhD through their state funded school systems, and hopefully go through the route of medical practice. I was also thinking.... IF (and only if) Poland is good in terms of EMFs and magnetism, we would be able to create the ultimate bedroom in a basement and keep it pretty much freezing - cold and dark, only used for sleep and sex; get one of those Japanese organic floor mattresses so we're even closer to the ground (although already underground) and throw a Magnetico under there - we'd have ourselves the ultimate place to sleep.

Any input?

### MeghanK — 2013-08-13T05:09:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-mats-the-rebuttal.7883/post-92323>

I emailed the Earthing Company... here is what they said??????
MY EMAIL:
**
Any rebuttal to below.... is this something we need to be concerned with?**
"I get asked why I donâ€™t use an earthing mat or grounding mat and thereâ€™s a reason that I use something like the Earthpulse, which is achieving a similar effect rather than an earthing mat or grounding mat. And the reason for it is because of the way that US Electric Utilities here in America have set up their electrical infrastructure. What happens is, unlike Europe, about 70% of the electrical current in the US is returned into the ground via face wires as it travels back to the substation, so pretty much everything that is in physical contact with the ground gets bombarded with this extra energy. But especially, if youâ€™re plugging yourself into a grounding mat that youâ€™re gonna be sleeping on and earthing mat in your grounded wire in your house, what youâ€™re doing is amplifying that effect even more. Youâ€™re exposing yourself to more electrical pollution when you use a grounding mat or an earthing mat compared to if you didnâ€™t use one at all."

THE RESPONSE:
**Your concern: Our products have a resistor built in so stray electricity is not an issue.**

I doubt I have IR because I am thin and my fasting insulin is <.2. But I wonder if I am still LR for some reason. My leptin was only 7, but I have a rt3 problem. Rt3 is always top of range while ft3 is at bottom. And I feel hypothyroid because of this. Doctors are always putting me on t3 meds.

Thank you,
Kathleen

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92455>

...There was a credible, longitudinal study that followed adolescents who smoked weed into adulthood. Their findings...Their IQ on average dropped 10 points (if I am remembering this correctly, but I do remember it was a significant amount). This may not mean much if you are a genius with an extremely high IQ, who can afford to lose 10 points. The average IQ is about 100 with a standard deviation of 15 or so...so approximately 85-115. Borderline intellectual functioning, also called borderline mental retardation, where the person has below avg cognitive ability falls in a range of 70-85. If you have an IQ of 75 at 15 years old, can you afford to lose another 10 pts by the time you are in your 20s?
I've also read studies indicating that chronic use of marijuana makes one more susceptible to psychosis. Additionally, the toxins that are produced by smoking one joint is drastically more than a cigarette. Overall, however, I've learned that the research thus far on the (negative) long-term effects of marijuana are inconclusive and that more research is needed before any definitive conclusions can be made.

However, I wonder how the body can account for potential respiratory damage or even cancer, especially in the immunocompromised AIDS patient, since smoked marijuana is deemed more effective than dronabinol or nabilone (both synthetic forms of THC) according to anecdotal reports. Raw marij. also can contain bacterial and/or fungi, posing a serious risk of infection for this group. (Regular marijuana smoking is associated with an increased risk for bronchitis and microscopic examinations of bronchial biopsies from marij. users have shown several kinds of cellular abnormalties, some of which are considered precancerous).

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-14T07:09:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92471>

Anybody who thinks marijuana is bad for us is sorely mistaken. We have an endocannabinoid system within us - it accounts for the cannabinoids found in marijuana (and some other things that activate it, like the anandamides in chocolate, I believe). There are hundreds of compounds in marijuana, and they all have different effects. CBD, for instance, is a cannabinoid that, if I remember correctly, doesn't get one high, but has a large therapeutic effect. Then there are 'hyper' concentrates such as budder, which are 99% THC compared to the upper 20's in high grade marijuana leaves (i.e. you can smoke much less and get an even stronger effect).

I no longer smoke weed because after an event which really screwed with my brain, hence how I wound up here - anytime I would use it I would not only get anxious, but I would almost have flashbacks, have odd pressure buildup in my head like my skull was going to implode, etc. That doesn't mean I think it is bad now, I think more people should use marijuana and there isn't enough research (although there is a lot, just not enough) on marijuana and psychedelics as options for healing and/or spiritual/consciousness progression.

Take a look at these pages for loads of FACTUAL information: [http://www.erowid.org/plants/cannabis/cannabis.shtml](http://www.erowid.org/plants/cannabis/cannabis.shtml) (this site is a fact based site on drugs to prevent deaths and other negative effects from using substances). Here is a rebuttal to one of the previous statements - [http://www.erowid.org/plants/cannabis/cannabis\_myth8.shtml](http://www.erowid.org/plants/cannabis/cannabis_myth8.shtml) marijuana does not cause brain damage or any IQ loss. There original study was used in Rhesus monkeys and I believe they even exposed them to THC via gas masks therefore they lacked oxygen AND they were taking in absurd amounts of THC in a short span.

Watch this: [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jxJmFSmOrqo](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jxJmFSmOrqo) (cannabis as a nutritious vegetable that can be eaten raw/juiced) AND:
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aGjC4HReFL0](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aGjC4HReFL0) (Rick Simpson Hemp Oil documentary - a 'hyper' concentrated source that has cured tumors, brain cancers, etc.)

This is not to advocate its use for everyone at every imaginable time, though. As I said, I no longer use it - I would in the future if my brain 'heals' itself and I feel safe with myself enough to use some, but right now that's not a choice.

### Eve — 2013-08-14T15:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92490>

> [ColdKnight said:](/goto/post?id=92465)
>
> What if one ingests it orally? or transdermally? Smoking is not the only method of ingestion. Those studies showing degraded iq could be propaganda from the pharmaceutical companies that don't want to lose a ton of money.

This is why for treating AIDS patients and others with debilitating conditions that have not responded to other treatment methods, researchers are looking into developing a THC patch, etc. Also, as I recall, ingesting marij. in food, for example, can have negative effects like nausea and vomiting for some people. Additionally, the effects of THC are significantly less through this method due to the first-pass effect.

Also, I would have to disagree that the study suggesting decreases in IQ in adolescents who smoked marij. into adulthood is "propoganda" from pharmaceutical companies. (Note that this study is different than the one conducted with rhesus monkeys. The sample in this case is humans). If anything, I think pharmaceutical companies would want people to think there are positive effects to THC, so they can market their synthetic versions (that might bypass the respiratory effects of smoked weed).Here is a link to the abstract of the former:[http://www.pnas.org/content/early/2012/08/22/1206820109.abstract](http://www.pnas.org/content/early/2012/08/22/1206820109.abstract) By clicking on "Author Affiliations", you can see that this study was conducted by authors affiliated with Duke University, etc.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-14T17:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92502>

I'm always skeptical of these long term studies. They never account for other variables like the diet of each of their subjects. Another item they don't control for is other drugs used. I'm curious as to what is their control group? How do they know what their functioning would have been like without the cannabis? If one is not careful, cannabis can cause great appetite for brain devolving sugary foods. Starting at age 13 probably isn't the best idea either. For pain from inflammation, used carefully, I think it could be used without hampering ones brain power significantly or at all. As far as the pharmaceutical companies, they can't patent the herb so they are trying to separate out just the THC compound. The problem is, thc is only one of many components to the herb that are needed for therapeutic benefit. Therefore, they realize they can't make money from it so they demonize it.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-14T19:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92526>

[http://libertycrier.com/top-10-cannabis-studies-the-government-wished-it-had-never-funded/](http://libertycrier.com/top-10-cannabis-studies-the-government-wished-it-had-never-funded/)
"Veterans Affairs scientists looked at whether heavy marijuana use as a young adult caused long-term problems later, studying identical twins in which one twin had been a heavy marijuana user for a year or longer but had stopped at least one month before the study, while the second twin had used marijuana no more than five times ever. Marijuana use had no significant impact on physical or mental health care utilization, health-related quality of life, or current socio-demographic characteristics."

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1253627/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1253627/) - here is a study that shows a synthetic cannabinoid increases hippocampal neurogenesis

[http://norml.org/component/zoo/category/cannabis-and-the-brain-a-user-s-guide:](http://norml.org/component/zoo/category/cannabis-and-the-brain-a-user-s-guide:) "Not only has modern science refuted the notion that marijuana is neurotoxic, recent scientific discoveries have indicated that cannabinoids are, in fact, neuroprotective, particularly against alcohol-induced brain damage. In a recent preclinical study -- the irony of which is obvious to anyone who reads it -- researchers at the US National Institutes of Mental Health (NIMH) reported that the administration of the non-psychoactive cannabinoid cannabidiol (CBD) reduced ethanol-induced cell death in the brain by up to 60 percent."

"the Journal of Neurological Sciences recently pronounced, "There is accumulating evidence ... to support the hypothesis that the cannabinoid system can limit the neurodegenerative processes that drive progressive disease,"

"But what about claims of cannabis' damaging effect of cognition? A review of the scientific literature indicates that rumors regarding the "stoner stupid" stereotype are unfounded. According to clinical trial data published this past spring in the American Journal of Addictions, cannabis use -- including heavy, long-term use of the drug -- has, at most, only a negligible impact on cognition and memory."

I'm sure this made me look like the largest pot head ever (I'm not!), but I just see NO reliable evidence whatsoever that ANY form of marijuana consumption is bad in any way. For instance, on smoking marijuana - "Researchers at the Kaiser-Permanente HMO, funded by NIDA, followed 65,000 patients for nearly a decade, comparing cancer rates among non-smokers, tobacco smokers, and marijuana smokers. Tobacco smokers had massively higher rates of lung cancer and other cancers. **Marijuana smokers who didn’t also use tobacco had no increase in risk of tobacco-related cancers or of cancer risk overall. In fact their rates of lung and most other cancers were slightly lower than non-smokers, though the difference did not reach statistical significance**."

It's all in how you use it.

### Eve — 2013-08-14T23:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92559>

My reply with quotes isn't working, but this is primarily directed at ColdKnight's question earlier...

I think you are (or any motivated reader is) right to be skeptical of any study before you read it in whole and evaluate its reliability and validity. The longitudinal study I was referencing earlier did have a control group (never used group) and they did exclude for such things as persistent tobacco dependence, persistent hard-drug and/or alcohol dependence, etc. As you probably are aware, it's really best to read the whole article (of any research study, whether it purports or negates benefits of cannabis use) if are able to given you have access to an Online Journal Database.

It might be true that pharmaceutical companies "demonize" the use of street cannabis, but I am not sure how you are deducing that. The studies I have read and I think even the ones the other poster linked to here, were NOT funded by any pharmaceutical company but were initiated in universities, medical centres, etc.
Additionally, as far as I'm aware, pharmaceutical companies are isolating other components of cannabis aside from THC that have been proven effective in clinical trials (for a particular population, e.g., patients with AIDS wasting syndrome).
