### seanb4 — 2017-09-23T16:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-224186>

UPDATE:
Since last update I have made progress in some areas, albeit small.

Thiamine! Despite my diet having roughly enough I decided to try some transdermal allithiamine after hearing how it helps some with POTS. Instantly noticed improvement in symptoms after eating and noticed slight improvement in POTS overall. Unfortunately I have not been able to build on this, attempts at increasing dosage lead to increases in different POTS like symptoms. I am taking a B-Complex and mg, k but I still am unable to tolerate any more than slightly over the RDA.
Thiamine helps stomach acid production, and the ANS which is how it could be helping my POTS.

Now the question is why am I thiamine deficient since I eat lots of offal and generally get enough through diet. The obvious answer to me is poor digestion. I think the main culprit is low stomach acid possibly from screwed up ANS not letting me get into parasympathetic mode. Could also be gut bacteria eating it up.
I have tried many times to fix my stomach acid but failed always. I can take massive amounts of Betaine HCL with no burning but then develop over symptoms from it, presumably from pushing methylation too hard.
I think the key to fixing this issue would be fixing my ANS which would also fix my POTS and allow proper blood flow to the gut, also allow proper parasympathetic signaling to make stomach acid and increase motility. How to fix this is the million £££ question. Grounding seems to help a little, the sun light if too warm makes me more sympathetic, CT does...

Usually I don't get ill but I got a fever 2 week ago and my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days. I had to go to the hospital as this combined with my POTS was unbearable, I stayed in for 4 days and now it's mostly gone. I am sick to death of this heart pounding, it's no good for me short nor long term however the doctors don't care.

My vit D3 test came back at 121 nmol/L = 48ng/ml. Disappointing for saying I have spent literally all summer outside as I no longer work. I know that people who have CFS/POTS have lower vit D but I was hoping it would be higher...

**Allithiamine** is a lipid-soluble form of **vitamin B₁** which was discovered in garlic in the 1950s along with its homolog **prosultiamine**.
\===================
There was discussion on Marik Protocol
sulbutiamine (goes thru blood brain barrier, BBB)
benfontiamine(does not go thru BBB)

Possibly you may want to try it (supposedly it wears of, so not sure about increasing doses (assuming that it would initially been helpful)

### seanb4 — 2019-07-05T09:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-273712>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=273677)
>
> **my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days.
> \--------**
> reduce
> or temporarily discontinue
> thyroid hormone supplementation if any.
> **
> .**

Hey JanSz, hope you've been well. The increase in HR is due to POTS which is a form of autonomic dysfunction and this + CFS I suspect is the cause of all my symptoms. No thyroid supps here.

"Possibly you may want to try it (supposedly it wears of, so not sure about increasing doses (assuming that it would initially been helpful)"

I did get significant initial success with allithiamine but like you said it wore off. Could you tell me more about how it wears off? I had assumed I had corrected a deficiency so I stopped gaining further benefit.

I may as well pop on update here as it has been a long time.

Like I said above, I feel I have a decent idea on what has been causing my all these problems, fixing it however is not so easy. My illness started September 2011 after getting Tonsilitis and a stomach ulcer. Afterwards I developed symptoms of CFS and POTS. It is very common in both these diseases for the trigger to be a viral onset. I have received official Dx of these diseases after going through a lot of ignorant doctors.

I have been making very slow and steady progress since I hit rock bottom in 2016. I feel most of the progress I have made has been by avoiding or maneuvering around the disease as opposed to curing it. Examples include, avoiding heat!, keeping legs raised, mirtazapine, adding MCT to carbs, avoiding carbs, soft meals.

With regards to carbs there are quite a few studies showing malfunctioning energy metabolism but particularly glucose. This rings very true in my case as my heart symptoms increase like clock work no matter the carb source after carb meals in correlation with the amount of carbs. It also follows a patern of increasing symptoms up to 1-1.5 hrs, peaking then slowly dropping to baseline over the next 4hrs.

There is particular interested in Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Inhibition. Long story short, I thought I may be able to get around PDH inhibition by eating MCT with carbs as MCTs are metabolised straight away so the liver will have a large supply of acetyl CoA so any Pyruvate ingested will skip PDH and go through PC instead to make oxaloacetate and the TCA will keep spining.

Anyway it seems to have worked as my symptoms are siginficantly reduced after carbs compared to before however they are still present. You may say just eat ketosis however gastroparesis then comes a big problem but I have recently managed to get keto to work by using very soft foods and relying on some excess body fat.

Although I have no real idea what is going on I will say this is my leading theory so far. I get virus that goes into cells yet is non cytolytic. The virus then causes dysregulation in my system (cytokines, IDO, exosomes) that lead to poor energy metabolism and the resulting dysautonomia etc. This is obviously very simplified.

Another idea is tonsilitis caused brainstem compression / CCI / etc which then caused malfunctioning autonomic system. I recently found out that ENT infections can lead to cervical spine instability, I believe it is called Grisels syndrome. There are a bunch of other reasons to suspect this.

I have a number of other theories and things to work on. I mainly post over on phoenixrising forums now. I still do a lot of things I learnt from Kruse but I eliminate that which isn't compatible with my symptoms. For example I regularly go outside and get sun in my eyes but I do not allow myself to get hot (which is very awkward in summer), the heat causes more blood pooling in my legs and I do not sweat at all which make everything worse and absolutely kills my heart.

So I am doing ok for the most part, still lots of things to try and optimistic I will get better however the question is how much more of my youth will be wasted before I do get better?

### Dextery — 2013-08-16T03:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-do-they-regenerate-like-bone.7913/post-92718>

But there was that article about growing teeth in urine. [http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/07/30/urine-stem-cells-grow-teeth\_n\_3677753.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/07/30/urine-stem-cells-grow-teeth_n_3677753.html)

"Need a new tooth? Chinese researchers may have developed a way to grow human teeth, but those in need of a replacement may be a bit turned off when they learn how the substitute is created.

In a study published Tuesday in peer-reviewed journal Cell Regeneration, a team from the Guangzhou Institutes of Biomedicine and Health reveals how they used urine stem cells to grow teeth in mice.

Working with cells passed from the body in urine, researchers created stem cells that could potentially form rudimentary teeth and implanted them into the kidneys of mice. After three weeks, the urine stem cells grew into tooth-like structures that contained dental pulp, dentin, enamel space and enamel organ.

The team compared the teeth grown from urine stem cells with structures developed using human embryonic stem cells, the study's control group.

urine stem cells teeth
The above figure shows a tooth structure created from human embryonic stem cells (top) and induced pluripotent stem cells (bottom), which are derived from urine. The images on the left show the structures removed from the mouse kidney. (Image courtesy of Cai et al./Cell Regeneration)

The use of urine stem cells may be an unusual method for growing teeth, but it's not the only ongoing research into bioengineering replacement teeth. A recent study in the U.K. published in the Journal of Dental Research, indicated that cells from human gums combined with mouse embryonic cells may be used to form human teeth.

"It is well known that adult dental stem cells have been successfully applied in tissue
engineering research. It has proven to be possible to generate dentin pulp complexes, even whole teeth out of isolated cells," the Chinese researchers write in the study. "Although these approaches could lead to tooth formation, the sources of endogenous dental epithelial stem cells seem to be scarce."

That's why, as the authors explain, they decided to produce substitutes for the specific type of dental cells using stem cells derived from urine.

While the research provides a potential practical use for urine stem cells, some have questioned the feasibility of the method to produce teeth that would be suitable for adults. As stem cell scientist Chris Mason of the University College of London points out to BBC News, the risk of contamination from bacteria may also be an issue."

Thoughts on ZMK for athletes? Better than transdermal magnesium spray and Natural Calm at night?

### Ricardo — 2013-12-18T05:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-103724>

I take 1 gram of Magnesium citrate, 50 mg chelated zinc and a sublingual tablet of b-12 (methylcobalamin), b6 and folate. Its a homemade version of ZMA. ZMK is like ZMA with the addition of some other nutes. My bloods showed a magnesium deficiency before I supplemented with Magnesium citrate. Now they are normal.

I prefer Magnesium citrate because its good value for the $. I get it in bulk powder.

My favorite is Magnesium Malate. However, I can't find it in bulk.

Many people are deficient in Magnesium and/or Zinc. I had stiff muscles from working out: the Magnesium solved that.

Magnesium levels fall during the night so its a good idea to take this one before sleep. Its relaxes and seems to calm food cravings.

I have read so many good things about Magnesium that I am a regular consumer of it.

I haven't finished reading all Dr Kruse has written about Magnesium. So far, it seems like he advocates supplementing with it.

My bloods have shown that I need to supplement with Magnesium. Agricultural conditions have changed and we don't get as much of it from food as we used to.

Does hot water freeze faster than cold water? This was shown decades ago by Erasto Mpemba, who found that ice cream made with warm water froze faster than ice cream made with cold water. ### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-16T03:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-just-the-temperature-of-water-matter-to-biology.7918/post-92734>

More to contemplate about heat and cold and energy transfers: Heat transfer is a discipline of thermal engineering that concerns the generation, use, conversion, and exchange of thermal energy and heat between physical systems. Heat transfer is classified into various mechanisms, such as thermal conduction, thermal convection, thermal radiation, and transfer of energy by phase changes. Engineers also consider the transfer of mass of differing chemical species, either cold or hot, to achieve heat transfer. While these mechanisms have distinct characteristics, they often occur simultaneously in the same system.
Heat conduction, also called diffusion, is the direct microscopic exchange of kinetic energy of particles through the boundary between two systems. When an object is at a different temperature from another body or its surroundings, heat flows so that the body and the surroundings reach the same temperature, at which point they are in thermal equilibrium. Such spontaneous heat transfer always occurs from a region of high temperature to another region of lower temperature, as described by the second law of thermodynamics.
Heat convection occurs when bulk flow of a fluid (gas or liquid) carries heat along with the flow of matter in the fluid. The flow of fluid may be forced by external processes, or sometimes (**in gravitational fields**) by buoyancy forces caused when thermal energy expands the fluid (for example in a fire plume), thus influencing its own transfer. This is known as a field effect. The latter process is often called "natural convection". All convective processes also move heat partly by diffusion, as well. Another form of convection is forced convection. In this case the fluid is forced to flow by use of a pump, fan or other mechanical means.
Thermal radiation occurs through a vacuum or any transparent medium (solid or fluid). **It is the transfer of energy by means of photons in electromagnetic waves governed by the same laws**

Cites:Geankoplis, Christie John (2003). Transport processes and separation process principles : (includes unit operations) (4th ed. ed.). Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall Professional Technical Reference.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-16T21:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92825>

He told the Mail: "If things continue as they are, I predict that 40 to 50 percent of 30-year-olds could have myopia by 2033 as a result of smartphones and lifestyles in front of screens -- an epidemic we call screen sightedness."

Screens place a greater demand on the eyes. Moreover, what's clear is that people are staring into them for previously unthinkable hours at a time.

Whole lives are being lived through screens and in them. Human bodies seem unprepared for this entirely radical shift.

[http://news.cnet.com/8301-17852\_3-5...rtphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims/](http://news.cnet.com/8301-17852_3-57598890-71/iblind-smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92830>

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T22:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92835>

Yes. It is not good to look at a screen of any kind for a long period of time without breaks. One should look at things further away to excersize the eye muscles by focusing. It's probably wise to keep the total amount of time to a minimum per day. I could see how keeping your eyes focused on one distance for an extended period of time would make you unable to refocus on objects farther away. This goes back to the old saying use it or lose it. Going with the theme of ancestral wellness I can't see our ancestors starring at the ground for hours on end at a bug or something like that.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T22:40:40+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-your-opinion-on-this-article.7927/post-92920>

@AutoMoonMonster

I'd nix the home outlet filters. Our EMF Webinar guy Michael Neuert says they _could_ worsen one's situation. It depends on many other factors going on in your home/apt. wiring and proximity to power lines, transformers, smart meters, etc. Too much money at $30 each times number-of-outlets-in-your-home when all they are is a plastic box with a few capacitors inside.

The schumann resonators are intriguing, but I've not found decent research and comparisons on what they do. And it's an electronic device, so I have a fundamental skepticism about using them. Still, if I were to get some good, credible advice on whether to get one, I would definitely consider a whole-house unit.

I think a magnet pad is what one ought to shoot for. So much data coming out, and already here, on the positive effects of magnetic fields. Magnetism is fundamental to life, so why not up the field and grow like a dinosaur when you increase that field.

You will find differing results on all these things because we are all different in how we've lived and the effect it's had on us. That fact alone has helped me to make sense of this whacky world and why one thing works immediately for one person, and not so much for the next. But I always think that if you ran labs on both, you'd see changes in each. Just different values and effects.

The Big Giant Head has spoken.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-17T17:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-92927>

So you're outside, doing some yardwork. You come inside to cool off, and notice that you are hungry. You must have been too busy while you were working to realize you needed food. Right?

Wrong. While outside in the sun, you were soaking up the rays, relying on the sun to power your work. All was fine, until you came inside. Switch turned off, and your body/brain/leptin receptor says it's time for Plan B.

It's the same with magnetism. You are fine while snoozing on the Magnetico, or even playing cards on the bed. But get up just for a few minutes, and you might realize that you are now hungry. Plan B time.

It's so simple. Well, assuming everything is working right.

> So you're outside, doing some yardwork. You come inside to cool off, and notice that you are hungry. You must have been too busy while you were working to realize you needed food. Right?
>
> Wrong. While outside in the sun, you were soaking up the rays, relying on the sun to power your work. All was fine, until you came inside. Switch turned off, and your body/brain/leptin receptor says it's time for Plan B.
>
> It's the same with magnetism. You are fine while snoozing on the Magnetico, or even playing cards on the bed. But get up just for a few minutes, and you might realize that you are now hungry. Plan B time.
>
> It's so simple. Well, assuming everything is working right.

brilliant insight.... Non

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T16:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94048>

Looks like the sunshine works, at least for killing hunger, behind glass. I was riding in the car with DH, who does NOT like the windows rolled down while the A/C is on, when I got hungry, despite the magnet beneath me. I couldn't stick my arm out the window as usual. So I moved my solar panels while still behind the glass. Half my right thigh got some sunlight, and my lower arm as well. Sure enough, the magic happened. Hunger went away. I was a bit surprised, because I thought the lightly tinted (standard) glass blocks beneficial light, but it was enough to make me happy. Until he turned the corner. Darn it!

But there was a solution to that as well. I just put my feet on the dash. He didn't care much for that either, but my tummy overruled him. Sunlight on the tops of both feet, almost to the ankles, was enough.

I feel so .... enabled! Like I know The Secret!

### cinnamon — 2013-08-28T17:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94051>

nonchalant,

I have a solar powered radio. It can run on ordinary AA batteries, or on a rechargeable battery or directly from solar. When sun hits it (has to be strong enough) it can play the radio without a battery. The sun also recharges the rechargeable battery.

So, I'm wondering if the sun you got in the car was sufficient to keep you "playing" while it was shining directly on you. I wonder how much you would need so that it was enough to actually start recharging your rechargeable battery as well...

Did your hunger return when you got out of the sun? I mean, did the sun replace food? Or did it just allow you to delay eating?

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-28T18:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94063>

I love this thread.

I can already feel the beginnings of the seasonal change hitting me. mostly from the lack of sunlight upon waking during the work week, even though I'm stepping out side.

This morning, I grabbed my breakfast and went and sat out on the grass in my front yard to grab an little extra oomph... and felt much better, even though it was overcast and rainy.

I'm actually trying to figure out how to swing photoelectric therapy courtesy of a tanning bed and red light therapy at a tanning salon this winter....

this thread has highlighted one of the reasons I did so well when Living on Guam 12hrs of sunlight/darkness. I was at my lowest weight ever and I now know why... amazing isn't it

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T18:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94067>

Cinnamon, I'm guessing I would have to be outside for hours to begin to recharge anything. While I was in the car, and the sun became obscured by a cloud, I felt it immediately. Hunger soon followed. So it was 'just enough'. Plus I had a magnet below my seat, that probably helped. I usually have my window rolled down.

The sun allows me to delay eating. Usually hunger comes back right away. However, I have noticed, on occasion, that the hunger sometimes doesn't return when I leave the car and go into a house or a store. I think maybe I have had my arm out the window long enough to recharge a bit, or perhaps my body decided to burn a bit of fat instead. And there seems to be a difference in real hunger and just cravings. If it's time for dinner, it's time for dinner!

I remember long ago we had a vacation where we were sailing in a bay. One day we skipped breakfast, and then skipped lunch, and then about 4pm I figured we really aught to have a bite to eat, though neither of us were hungry one bit. We had no idea why, we figured we were just having too much fun to be hungry. We were young, and I wore long sleeves and a hat from fear of the sun. But that was a remarkable day. Spent the night in a pup tent on a sandbar. Woke up to stark terror, but that's another story.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-29T03:14:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94125>

Hi BJK! I've been using these under my car seat:

[http://www.harborfreight.com/set-of-...oks-65528.html](http://www.harborfreight.com/set-of-...oks-65528.html)

I currently have four under the driver's seat of my car, and for our other vehicles I have 1 or 2 under the seats, with the **north side up**.
I discussed how I figured out how many in this thread: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7853-Fun-with-Magnets/page2](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7853-Fun-with-Magnets/page2)
You might want to start him out with one magnet for a week or so, then double it, etc. To make sure he doesn't get any aches or pains.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-06T01:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-104841>

It seems we humans have adaptations to maximize our exposure to the 3 legs. Our skin has reduced hair coverage to increase our exposure to sunshine. We have several adaptations to water, like the dive reflex, nostrils that allow us to be under water, bipedalism, etc. And even our feet allow maximum daytime exposure to the earth's magnetism -- we have lots of semiconductors in them, and we walk on our entire foot, not just on our toes.

### nonchalant — 2015-07-27T18:23:51+0300

We have several adaptations to water, like the dive reflex, nostrils that allow us to be under water, bipedalism, etc. And even our feet allow maximum daytime exposure to the earth's magnetism -- we have lots of semiconductors in them, and we walk on our entire foot, not just on our toes.

Is bipedalism an adaptation to increase our exposure to sunshine? If you are standing, almost all of your skin gets some sun exposure constantly throughout the day. Sure, being on two feet is also handy for carrying things, and walking in water, etc. But light exposure on more of the skin, including the belly, must have been really pivotal.

# Thread: Speech issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-08-18T05:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93004>

Wondering if any of you knowledgeable folks can help me with this....

When I was very ill for a few years my speech became very mumbled, shallow breathing led to low volume, and it was very hard for people to understand me.

I know years ago I read the causes of this and I cant remember what it was to save my life!

I was a former opera singer and spoke with great clarity and diction. I never had trouble carrying on a conversation.

My 13 year old daughter is having this same problem, it has been going on for quite sometime and effects her socially as she is nervous about speaking up. Some times it is not happening and she is loud and clear and full of wonderful things to say. My heart breaks when I see her suffering with this.

I know its totally fixable....I fixed mine....I just don't know how i did it!! So frustrating!!

Thanks for the input.

(she has been treated for lyme, bartonella, hypothyroidism,babesia, yeast) Lots of abx....gut dysbiosis, vit A deficient......clues?

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T05:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93005>

I believe there is an association with parkinsons....similar mechanism

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-18T07:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93011>

read brain gut 5..............

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T16:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93029>

Read. Again. It certainly explains why she craves sardines!

She also takes a bunch of spirulina, 10k mcg B12 daily,drinks raw milk, eats a couple Brazil nuts, takes vitamin c with zinc.....not sure how else to help her.

More raw seafood seems like the answer but I am at a loss there as she didn't like oysters, and sushi which she loves is loaded with rice.

I'll just keep pushing on....thought maybe there was something I had missed.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-18T17:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93032>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=93004)
>
> I was a former opera singer and spoke with great clarity and diction.

Wow! Interesting folk on this forum.

I thought about asthma first.

I don't know if there is any connection, but I noticed a couple years ago that I started getting a foul mouth, to the point that I was wondering about myself. This was not in public, just in private with DH and also in my thoughts. When I improved my diet that went away. I donno, maybe I was able to think of better words?

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T18:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93038>

Seems very likely there is a similar mechanism at work.....she exhibits some belligerent behavior which is soooo unlike her. really short fused and bitchy. rude to her brothers. Its hard for me to decipher what is emerging teenager crap and what is occurring due to state of the body.

When the symptoms are not present she very apologetic and back to herself. She just started menstruating this spring....I am positive a lot of this is cyclical.

As if it wasn't hard enough to figure out my own cycle/hormone issues!!! ugh!

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-18T20:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93050>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-18T15:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/energy-and-epigenetics-2-is-now-live.7936/post-93022>

[http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-2-the-real-dha-story/](http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-2-the-real-dha-story/)

Commitment will sacrifice by nature because they have already laid down their all to get here. It's easier to die when you are already dead first in your mind. Just as it is easier to sacrifice when you have already sacrificed first in your mind. The greater the love, the greater will be the sacrifice. Love sacrifices with no strings attached. It never asks for a penny in return. It sacrifices initially and it sacrifices always. And that's why sacrificial love grips our very being. This is why a parent will sacrifice for their children, and how a person can fight a war they do not believe in. A soldier doesn't fight because he hates the enemy in front of him, he fights because he loves what he left behind. Committed people ask when should I sacrifice and how much? Involved people ask why should I sacrifice and how come? How much are we willing to sacrifice a part of life, our time, our comforts, or just a slice of ourselves? How considerate are we towards others who are less privileged, less informed, or who have less in life? Are we unselfish enough to share what we have for others? We give love and affection when we sacrifice something. If we have no success in our battles of life yet, it maybe because we have forgotten that we belong to each other. Today, share something with someone. A bone to the dog is not charity. Charity is the bone shared with the dog, when you are just as hungry as the dog.

# Thread: Solid Improvements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-08-19T00:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/post-93062>

**The Ache**
My latest successes have been around the Mitochondrial RX and how it's given me some much needed relief with this chronic fatigue and fibromyalgia that got ramped up five years ago after a severe illness. Some things have helped a little, but when I got into my third week of the RX, things shifted towards less pain, more energy.

**Useful Work**
I continued adding and subtracting the different supplements to get a sweet spot and it has really helped. I am able to do more chores and work on projects and actually have fun doing it! I take things in stride a lot better. I have done some gardening and orchard work after nearly two years of virtual inactivity. I've been able to redo some poor wiring in the house to lessen the fields.

**Focus!**
Mental clarity has improved as well. I'm not burning pots on the stove so much these days. I don't forget to feed my chickens… and my dog. And, oh yeah, my DW! I do the cooking these days, as some of you may know. Mentally, it's brought me out of a funk that was no fun at all.

**Going Exterior**
I find myself being further "out" from my body with less pain. I'm not imploding with the chronic ache in my legs and arms like before. I'm looking "outward" like I used to do, and re-starting old dreams and projects, and even some art has come up on the radar.

**Self-Absorption**
I move around the farm here, but don't exercise as such, but I've become more muscular by just eating more like a Great White. My skin staying tighter than the other guys I know that are my age. Lots of water and bone broth soups. I pass a mirror and have to stop and admire my good looks, and it's hard to get away from a full length mirror these days. That's what Big Headed people do. It's a healthy ego trip. Just wait 'til I start working out! A certain Aussie we all know and love may start paddling northward heading this way!

**Sleep like a Dummy**
I sleep like a baby, it's usually 9 to 10 hours, and while it's still tough to get up, it's a lot easier these months and years on this Paleo journey. I don't stay up late anymore, and find I look forward to sleep soon after the sun goes down. Blue blockers, of course.

**Meal Tickets**
My appetite has stabilized a lot. My in-between meal cravings and crashes have all but disappeared. I never snack… well, almost. I do sardines often. It's an inexpensive way to get my DHA. I've done oysters, salmon, other fish, shrimp, and all the paleo foods in between. Coconut oil is my best friend. Butter is my lover. Curcumin is my next conquest.

**What a Drip**
Okay, I'm going out here a bit. I didn't think I could ever admit to this, but it's huge for me and the perfect thing to share to cut-down-to-size the guy with the Big Head. Since the Mito RX, I've been able to stop wearing those g\*\*damn diapers! I was having a little trouble with the little guy drooling a bit and finally had to buy the things and figured that I'd have to for the rest of my life. Not! What a win! There, I said it. I know many of you are crushed and disheartened, perhaps put off a bit, but I can assure you, I'm back in the game!

I have a way of life now that I can do rather than try. I'm not lost in a world of latest and greatest "diet this", and "eat that", crusades that fall short of what a Human needs.

This is the place! I'm home.

And even posting more.

B. G. Head

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-19T16:20:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93104>

[http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true](http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true)

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-19T17:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93105>

As humans we protect what we believe is ours, when in reality it never was ours alone - it's everyone's. this is what Steve is challenging within the Status Quo. those in power are fearful of the masses waking up to realize this...

### beach bunny — 2013-08-19T17:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93107>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=93104)
>
> [http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true](http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true)

So much food for thought in this article...I keep feeling like everything in this life...it's an issue of consciousness and empowerment..or lack thereof.
Even if each individual that makes up an organization has "good intentions" collectively they can still dis-empower members of a society, and inevitably enslave them to their version of the truth (even if it's killing people).
Sometimes fighting "the system" can add fuel to the fire and they just push back harder. So then the truth is once again muddled by big egos.
It is such a paradox.
Every day I pray for each of us to wake up to our own personal power...then doctors and patients become one, learning from each other...then our ideas about health radically change..
This is why I so appreciate this blog, when you're not just saying "believe this" but instead encourage us to read, learn and test everything in our own N=1.

I personally would love to do a weekend somewhere, in an area that 'complies' with the things we all believe in, that is not a huge hotel in a crowded area so it's all authentic. And we could hire an dpi paleo chef so we are fed well, or feed ourselves, jump in the water and CT together. I 100% believe this could happen and be an amazing experience for whoever attends. I have had this desire for a long, long time (ever since I found this place last fall). This is just me, but I wouldn't do a cruise...too much EMF from wifi etc....and I am not sure they would comply with providing the kinds of foods we would want to be served. ### Hope — 2013-08-20T17:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93233>

The title of this article is actually,

Should Giving Diet Advice Be Against the Law?

I didn't have time to read the article, just the title on the website, but I know that the ADA (A. dietetics association) has been working hard on keeping non-registered dietitians from giving DIET advice to protect their people (who go to school and take exams and so on and work in hospitals etc etc). This is very very different than dispensing MEDICAL advice. Helping someone with food should not ever be illegal in my book. Doing medicine is different. No one has ever died I think from someone getting help from someone else, nutritionist or blogger even (at least not that I am aware of it) about how to change their diet. Medical advice, in person, phone (Which is what I do with my amazing MD who is out of state) is very different. I am sure everyone on here knows that the **3rd largest cause** of death at least in the US is.....consequences of medical advice. Here's a quick article about that....

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iatrogenesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iatrogenesis)

# Thread: Onions and histamine release
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/onions-and-histamine-release.7945/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-19T17:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/onions-and-histamine-release.7945/post-93110>

Onions: help decrease histamine release. An important health-producing ingredient in onions is quercetin. Studies have shown that baking, sauteing roasting or frying onions increases their quercetin content. Boiling is the only cooking method that reduces quercetin content.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-19T17:50:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/onions-and-histamine-release.7945/post-93114>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-salad.7948/post-93782>

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-20T01:10:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93152>

Dave asprey is saying that he got leaky gut from not eating carbs with his ketogenic diet.He ate mostly broccoli as his carbs. Supposedly, you don't make the right bile or enzymes w/out carbs/starch. I've been on and of keto for over a year, and a year ago or so I got a minor hives rash on torso only. Its gotten a little worse over the last 6 months or so. Considering you like the keto diet, I just wanted to know your thoughts on this Dr Kruse. My friend told me this today, and I didn't know what to tell him.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-20T03:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93175>

When youre done read this...[http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/...then](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/...then) school the guy.
Wisdom allows great people to become small to understand complexity.

### yewwei.tan — 2013-08-20T05:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93196>

Very interesting. One question that follows is how to manage the transition; From what I understand, until your cells regain their ability to tell time and access the PPP, you will not be able to make enough endogenous D-ribose to fuel the fat burning pathways, and thereby be dependent largely on the fast energy pathways and experience all the negative symptoms commonly seen in very low carb diets.

**You mention that this process of adaptation to living in the PPP can take 12-24 months, and I'd assume that the negative side effects that Asprey mentioned would persist that entire time.** You then mention in the EMF4 blog post that:

The change to optimal fat burning can be shortened when you acclimate to making enough D-ribose from your PPP.

So obviously getting the body to produce enough D-ribose endogenously is the key.

I've been on the ketogenic diet for about 2 months, since it's winter time in Australia where I'm at. Since starting, I've also experienced some additional skin breakouts (especially in the ankles), and feel that some of the tolerance toward sub-optimal foods has decreased (eg: I'm getting skin issues even after just a tiny cup of coffee).

I'm trying to think of a plan to reduce this transition time. So far, it's been the usual suspects:

\- eat Epi-Paleo + EPCOTx due to a leaky gut. Seafood is decently cheap and easy to find here in Australia.
\- get a magnetic sleep mattress and reduce EMF exposure
\- daily CT
\- lots of sleep (9+hrs a day, usualy 10pm to 7+am)
\- appropriate supplementation

**Now I'm wondering whether or not the following could be helpful in speeding up adaptation time to entering the PPP**:

\- not doing any exercise that depends heavily on the CP system
\- supplementing with D-ribose specifically when exercise is performed
\- adding some extra MCT oil in the mornings to accelerate the generation of ketones

\----

I come from an athletic background, and many times, "ketogenic diets" are implemented with the levels of protein often recommended by the athletic community. Typically, this leads to a level of 1 - 1.5g protein per pound bodyweight. **For a 22 year old 183lbs male like me,** that's 180g to 270g of protein a day. **I eat maybe 0.7 - 1.0kg of meat a day, so around 180 - 220g of protein.**

Protein ilicits an insulin response to some degree, and excess protein can be turned into glucose. **Am I right in assuming that my level of protein intake could actually hinder my adaptation to burning fat for fuel**?

I know you hate this type of question, but would you say an intake of 100-150g of protein a day for someone my size is appropriate?

\----

Speaking of skin breakouts Another misc point from EMF4.

Why are the symptoms first found in a leaky gut or in skin breakouts? The cells of both organs turn over in 2-3 days in a normal person.

Does this also mean that rate of skin recovery after a breakout is an indicator that you are doing things right again?

\----

Thanks.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-20T06:22:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93197>

Skin.........comes up many times.......and I have said three times in the last few blogs......neuroectoderm is brain and skin......is tied to the photoelectric effect: Vitamin A cycle and Vitamin D cycle...........you are assuming it only takes 2-3 days.......in you.......it does in a person with low NFkappabeta and IL-6 in tissues derived from these.......it means what you are assuming maybe not reality......this is why I advocating testing. When your on the road back you can not trust your own beliefs......use batteries of tests and keep your self constantly curious.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-20T06:24:07+0300

And coldnight......Dave is inundated with EMF risk even though he thinks he is doing enough.......when you swim in it.......protection is an illusion of security.

### yewwei.tan — 2013-08-20T11:46:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93206>

Late nights spent programming in the past obviously didn't help at all. Never again ...

You mentioned Vitamin D ... that motivated me to look back to my last lab panel (2 months ago -- when winter started) whereby I recorded a Vit D of a paltry 25ng/L. Serum ferritin was at 438ug/L in that same panel! At least my CRP was 0.5 and BUN/Creatinine ratio was 10.2 ...

# Thread: A Few Testosterone Replacement Quesitons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T01:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93160>

Haven't posted in a while cause I've been trying to spend less time on the computer but I'm still pluggin away JK style as much as possible.

Went to my endo today and I was so glad that he prescribed testosterone replacement but when I got to the pharmacy I found out that my insurance won't cover the Androgel that he prescribed so it would be about $325 - Not gonna work.

So my other two options are possibly the Androderm patch or IM injections.

I will have to wait 2 weeks to find out if my insurance will cover the patch.

If not my only other option is injections.

I've read online about subcutaneous injections for testosterone instead of IM.

I asked the doctors nurse about it and she said that's not possible with testosterone (obviously it's a newer technology).

I figured if I could find a pharmacy that caries the SubQ that I might get the doc to prescribe it.

Walgreens doesn't carry it and nether does CVS - at least not the ones I called.

So i've got 2 questions:

1) What do you think of the patch vs IM injections?

2) Any ideas about finding a pharmacy that supplies SubQ

If I'm gonna do this long term I think I'd rather do SubQ injections over always wearing a patch.

From what I've read the advantages of SubQ over IM is that they are less painful and you can do them once or twice per week so you don't have the UPs and Downs that you have with the IM injections.

Any input is greatly appreciated

Thanks for reading

### Huck — 2013-08-20T03:16:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93172>

Out of curiosity, do you have a varicocele (varicose vein of the testicles)? It's sometimes described as a sack of worms! It usually occurs on the left testicle and will feel swollen, sometimes more than others. Similar to a varicose vein in legs, but this occurs in the scrotum.

The reason I ask is there is evidence that low testosterone can be a result of varicoceles causing free testosterone to back-flow into the prostate rather than into the blood stream. A varicocele is a vein with a defective valve which prevents blood from flowing through the vein. If your left testicle hangs lower than your right testicle, that is a good indication of varicocele.

I am interested in varicocele because a couple of Israeli doctors have studies and case studies that show that varicoceles cause BPH and prostate cancer. They initially treated varicoceles as a means of treating infertility (low testosterone) in men. Treating varicoceles usually results in an increase in testosterone. So if you have one or more varicocoles, treatment may restore your testosterone and also prevent BPH and prostate cancer.

ETA: Direct sun for at least 2 hours a day increased my testosterone more than testosterone cream did and with fewer side effects.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T03:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93177>

That's very interesting.

Ever since I had my vasectomy about 4 years ago I have felt something weird down there.

It's as if the tube in each sac is just moving around.

I have always wondered about that.

I wouldn't say that one hangs lower than the other though.

If I do have that what is the solution and can it be easily diagnosed?

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=93172)
>
> Out of curiosity, do you have a varicocele (varicose vein of the testicles)? It's sometimes described as a sack of worms! It usually occurs on the left testicle and will feel swollen, sometimes more than others. Similar to a varicose vein in legs, but this occurs in the scrotum.
>
> The reason I ask is there is evidence that low testosterone can be a result of varicoceles causing free testosterone to back-flow into the prostate rather than into the blood stream. A varicocele is a vein with a defective valve which prevents blood from flowing through the vein. If your left testicle hangs lower than your right testicle, that is a good indication of varicocele.
>
> I am interested in varicocele because a couple of Israeli doctors have studies and case studies that show that varicoceles cause BPH and prostate cancer. They initially treated varicoceles as a means of treating infertility (low testosterone) in men. Treating varicoceles usually results in an increase in testosterone. So if you have one or more varicocoles, treatment may restore your testosterone and also prevent BPH and prostate cancer.
>
> ETA: Direct sun for at least 2 hours a day increased my testosterone more than testosterone cream did and with fewer side effects.

### Huck — 2013-08-20T03:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93180>

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=93177)
>
> That's very interesting.
>
> Ever since I had my vasectomy about 4 years ago I have felt something weird down there.
>
> It's as if the tube in each sac is just moving around.
>
> I have always wondered about that.
>
> I wouldn't say that one hangs lower than the other though.
>
> If I do have that what is the solution and can it be easily diagnosed?

It can be easily diagnosed if your doctor knows what to look for. There are 3 levels: identified by feel, identified visually, and identified by instruments. By instruments is the smallest and by sight is the largest. Many males notice a varicocele when they are in their twenties. I remember going to doctor when I was in my mid 20's thinking I might have a rupture. The doc laughed and said I had varicose veins of the left testicle. Doctors since then have said it is not a problem, but now I know better. it should have been repaired years ago. I think I read that about 70% of all males get a varicocele at some point.

I believe there is a standard treatment for varicocele for infertility and then a more complex procedure for varicocele and BPH.

Do a search on varicocele testosterone and varicocele embolization. There are also some youtube videos.

ETA: Don't forget CT. Cold helps increase testosterone.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-20T18:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93244>

If I magically find $600 on the ground today and were to buy one... which is best?

### Terminator — 2013-08-20T19:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93252>

Magnetico for sure. I have both. However Earthpulse is very good also especially in "Recover Mode" and after you get used to it.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-26T02:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93771>

> [marcoc said:](/goto/post?id=93252)
>
> Earthpulse is very good also especially in "Recover Mode" and after you get used to it.

Marcoc, can you elaborate?

### Terminator — 2013-08-26T05:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93797>

I suggest you to read / research earthpulse as much as possible before you buy. I did just that. Ben Greenfield uses it also. I use it to sleep with it, mostly in "Recover" setting. This setting is by far the best. Following is what it does to me ;
1\. Sleep much beter.
2\. Recover much faster after very hard road bike workouts.
3\. Heal wounds much faster. I only experinced small cuts. My magnetico pad did not do that.

I'm now debating if i should get another earthpulse unit to use at work or to get Total shield for whole house and portable unit.

About Magnetico pad.
The reason i liked magnetico (note that it's past tense) is beacouse sometime my sleep was so good to that i would wake up after 6 hours and did not know where i was. lol This good sleep diminished in past 3 weeks. Why ? Not suere but i may have not used it properly. I now know that you cannot be around the pad during the day due to positive magnetic fields. I did that alot for 1.5 months. I only own one single Core Pad which was placed under one side of king size bed. Me and my wife would switch side to get the benefit but according to Magnetico this is bad. The side without pad is exposed to positive magnetic fields and that is bad. This may explain why i had not so great results in last few weeks. I'm upset at myself for not fully researching ang learning how to properly use it and pissed that the user manual does not state this. I currently cannot use my bedroom for just sleep so to avoid bad positive magnetic fields i removed it. I was very close to get magnetico for all members of my family but i'm holding back because same reasons.

I was not able to use both, earthpulse and pad at same time. My brain acted as if i was on caffeine.

Now i want to do more research on magnetism, maybe call magnetico to get more info from them to learn how to use it properly.

# Thread: Tips to help someone very new to all of this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-08-20T19:55:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93251>

My cousin contacted me last night....she has been suffering with hives for close to 2 years now and major leaky gut issues. She endured the loss of a child and another born with hydro-encephalitis. Multiple mis-carriages as well. She has seen stress at her young age that I can not imagine.

She is an amazing person that smiles in spite of it all, has a deeply rooted faith and wonderful zest for life. She has three young children and is the kind of mom I aspire to be.

She contacted me last night and said that her doc said she has an enlarged thyroid and is scheduled for ultrasound and she has developed allergies to peanuts, strawberries, chocolate, milk, and soy.

I immediately tell her to read The iodine Crisis, send her here and some GAPS info, explain a little leaky gut, and talk paleo. She responded that she doesnt really have any GI issues so this is not her case and she couldn't cook like that and get dinner on the table with 3 babies in tow every night. She is on the other end of the spectrum diet wise....home cooked meals but SAD ingredients. SAD for the whole family. Does herbal-life shakes for weight loss that is not happening. My heart breaks for her.

It is interesting to us both.....we are cousins and resemble each other quite a bit, have weight issues, and we have the same blue eyes with a little brown speck in one iris.

My question is....do y'all have any beginner type websites or info that will not overwhelm someone that is starting at the other end of the spectrum? Good explanations? Diet that seem more doable to someone at the very beginning of this kind of journey? I dont want her to think my info is so extreme and "out there" that she doesnt try it.

Thanks

### fitness@home — 2013-08-20T20:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93253>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=93251)
>
> My cousin contacted me last night....she has been suffering with hives for close to 2 years now and major leaky gut issues. She endured the loss of a child and another born with hydro-encephalitis. Multiple mis-carriages as well. She has seen stress at her young age that I can not imagine.

cantweight, the multiple miscarriages part threw a red flag up for me. Has she been tested for MTHFR through her doctors? It is a simple lab test. Worth asking for, IMHO.

Also, the 23andme test is only $99 and can give a much larger picture. Also well worth the money.

### cantweight — 2013-08-20T20:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93257>

I couldnt agree more! I tried to make some similar points when she was initially struggling but it is a very different way of thinking with most of my family.

They think I am a joke with all my silly diet and biotoxin illness nonsense. (I used to always say Lyme, but Martin has me thinking biotoxin illness sounds so much cooler..lol)

They all eat SAD. Their doctors know best. My stepmother is a nurse practitioner and she tries not to laugh in my face when I tell her things that we are doing.....that are working!!!

No one will listen to me....that is...until I am no longer fat. It doesnt matter that I can walk, and speak, and read again....they just see fat. Fat=failure to them.

If I mentioned these things I would over shoot and miss getting her on board...I need to ease in with things that make her feel better before doctors get at her with drugs and she gets worse.

I dont want to use the words dumb it down....she is very intelligent....but that is basically what I need to do here.

### cinnamon — 2013-08-20T22:14:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93264>

cantweight,

It is the positive reinforcement of the continual successes that drives the journey for me. Maybe you just need to get your cousin started with something that seems not very risky but will be successful and then let her drive?

The first blog of Jack's that really resonated with me was [CT 7](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/). In that blog, Jack explains what goes on in the body during a 24 hour cycle. It's pretty easy for people to understand that we evolved on a planet that pulses on-off with light/dark cycles. It's not much of a stretch to think that might have an impact on some (okay probably ALL, but start by saying some) biological processes.

From there, I could easily see that it would cost me nothing to try going to bed at 10 and getting up as soon as it was light out. I could implement the BAB using bacon and eggs. So, I tried it and within a few days, I was already feeling better (not perfect, of course, but better than I had been).

Following my own experience, I started reading more and accumulated more and more changes - first paleo (but not really low carb) then CT, then epi-paleo, then high fat, low carb epi-paleo, then grounding, then non-fluoridated water, etc...

I hope she sees the kind of graduated success you found cinnamon...

# Thread: Extra-low-frequency magnetic fields alter cancer cells through metabolic restriction.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-08-21T00:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/post-93284>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915261](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915261)

Have you ever wondered why cancer seems so random. Could it be the emfs and how they alter the structure of the water we drink?

Extra-low-frequency magnetic fields alter cancer cells through metabolic restriction.
Li Y, HÃ©roux P.
Source

InVitroPlus Laboratory, Department of Surgery, Royal Victoria Hospital , Montreal, QC , Canada and.
Abstract

Abstract Background: Biological effects of extra-low-frequency (ELF) magnetic fields (MFs) have lacked a credible mechanism of interaction between MFs and living material. Objectives: To examine the effect of ELF-MFs on cancer cells. Methods: Five cancer cell lines were exposed to ELF-MFs within the range of 0.025-5 ÂµT, and the cells were examined for karyotype changes after 6 d. Results: All cancer cells lines lost chromosomes from MF exposure, with a mostly flat dose-response. Constant MF exposures for three weeks allow a rising return to the baseline, unperturbed karyotypes. From this point, small MF increases or decreases are again capable of inducing karyotype contractions (KCs). Our data suggest that the KCs are caused by MF interference with mitochondria's adenosine triphosphate synthase (ATPS), compensated by the action of adenosine monophosphate-activated protein kinase (AMPK). The effects of MFs are similar to those of the ATPS inhibitor, oligomycin. They are amplified by metformin, an AMPK stimulator, and attenuated by resistin, an AMPK inhibitor. Over environmental MFs, KCs of various cancer cell lines show exceptionally wide and flat dose-responses, except for those of erythroleukemia cells, which display a progressive rise from 0.025 to 0.4 ÂµT. Conclusions: The biological effects of MFs are connected to an alteration in the structure of water that impedes the flux of protons in ATPS channels. These results may be environmentally important, in view of the central roles played in human physiology by ATPS and AMPK, particularly in their links to diabetes, cancer and longevity.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-21T04:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/post-93318>

nice Dex.........i bet the FB PTEN thread got you thinking.........you see where we are headed.

# Thread: CT & Cycloset Effect Upon Growth Hormone Levels
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-08-21T03:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/post-93314>

Hi guys,

I am wondering about the effects of CT and cycloset upon growth hormone levels. I have been doing CT baths 4-5x per week for ~7 months and taking cycloset 2.4mg per day for ~4 months now. Although I started my CT protocol at a measly 70 degrees, after 7 months I am doing 30-45 minute daily ice baths in 42 degree water without any shivering during or after.

Although I do not have a baseline from the very beginning, over the past 4-5 months of cycloset + regular CT, my IGF-1 levels have increased by more than 100 points. In fact, I am now above the reference range at 373 ng/mL (range 53-331). From what I've researched, this is quite high for someone my age (34 years old). My endocrinologist said that he does not see levels that high very often in his patients and was quite interested in my CT protocol. Should I be concerned? Is this an expected side effect of CT? Cycloset is also classified as a mild growth hormone releasing agent, but 100+ points is not a "mild" increase so I am wondering if there could be some synergistic effect between the cycloset and CT protocols. Hopefully, elevated growth hormone is not a problem because the effects upon my damaged metabolism have been nothing less than remarkable!

I am morbidly obese and have been unable to loose weight for nearly 10 years despite heroic efforts at diet and exercise which did more to undermine my overall health than improve it... BUT over the past few months something really seems to be changing... I am noticing that I have become quite resistant to weight gain for the first time ever! In fact, I just bought pants 1 size smaller because my old pants were starting to look a little sloppy. I have only lost 4-5 pounds so I am guessing that there must be some muscle gain occurring in addition to fat loss. The truly amazing thing is that this has happened without any calorie restriction or increase in exercise! And on top of that I will admit that my diet is still a work in progress. I eat a lowish carb (~75-100g carbs per day), mostly paleo diet (some grass fed dairy), but no where near ketogenic or Epi-Paleo and my adherence to the Leptin Rx has been less than steller lately (started having mid morning coffee with cream again  ...BUT even in spite of a not perfectly optimal diet I seem to still be loosing fat and gaining muscle. And with that success, I am now finding myself more motivated to work on getting back on the Leptin Rx horse and adopting a more Epi-Paleo diet by increasing my seafood intake.

Trinity

You are doing awesome with C/T ......I can't wait to see your progress when you go strict epi paleo..... why not be 100% committed and go all in..... Please start a journal so we all can follow your great progress... It really helps everyone.

your IGF-l level sounds fantastic.....I am guessing that is a great thing ....but others will know more. From what Dr. K has told me - it is pretty hard to increase those levels as you age. If I remember right he told me when your hormones improve you can get a spike - hope I am remembering that right.

> You are doing awesome with C/T ......I can't wait to see your progress when you go strict epi paleo..... why not be 100% committed and go all in..... Please start a journal so we all can follow your great progress... It really helps everyone.

> your IGF-l level sounds fantastic.....I am guessing that is a great thing ....but others will know more. From what Dr. K has told me - it is pretty hard to increase those levels as you age. If I remember right he told me when your hormones improve you can get a spike - hope I am remembering that right.

Hi Caroline,

Thanks for the encouragement  I will try to start a journal sometime soon. I am slowly starting to move more toward epi-paleo. A Fresh Market just went in near my house and they have a great selection of wild caught seafood that I'm taking advantage of. I've went from eating seafood less than once per week to 3-4x per week now. I don't hate seafood, but it's never been my absolute favorite so I'm trying to slowly shift my taste preferences by having it a bit more often, trying new and different kinds of fish, and experimenting with different recipes and methods of preparation... Basically trying to find ways to integrate it into my diet in an enjoyable way. I figure that if I can slowly shift my tastes and develop more ways to enjoy seafood, then an epi-paleo style of eating will be more likely to become a permanent and sustainable dietary change for me.

Trinity

### Trinity — 2013-08-22T01:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/post-93376>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=93316)
>
> as long as you are minding your light levels and you are eating ketogenic........you will do great......This post is CT 6 testimony right here. I had the exact same result.....102 point increase when I began to my 6 month test......but I was meticulous about the light and diet.......

Hi Dr K,

Thanks for clarifying that the increase in IGF-1 is an expected result of sustained CT. That is really interesting that you also saw a 100 point increase in IGF-1. I just finished reading CT-6 in its entirety and I greatly appreciate your efforts to make this information available. Being in a research oriented field (not medicine), I have spent a significant amount of time and energy reading up on the metabolic and obesity research in an effort to uncover a fix for my own problems, however, it was not until I came across your website that I started to see the "big picture".

I am in this CT game for the long haul. As a morbidly obese person, I fully understand the absolute necessity of CT and the need to induce fat cell apoptosis. Without permanently destroying a significant number of my fat cells, long term fat loss will be physiologically impossible to sustain. I know this from first had experience because it was a substantial weight loss earlier in my life that severely damaged my endocrine system. I kept the weight off for several years through sheer force of will combined with unhealthy dieting and aggressive exercise... But in the end my self discipline backfired on me and hormonal havoc ensued. Despite my best efforts, I gained all of the weight back and then some along with a host of new problems including adrenal dysfunction, low DHEA, massively elevated reverse t3, trashed progesterone, pre-diabetes, and a destroyed circadian rhythm. I have spent what were supposed to be the best and most productive years of my life trying to recover my health with little assistance and a great deal of bitterness towards the conventional medical establishment.

In addition to the massive increase in IGF-1 I mentioned, I have also seen other improvements from CT that support everything you discuss in CT-6:

1) My t3 levels have improved over the past few months \*without\* increasing my cytomel dose

2) I have managed to get off of Cortef after almost 2 years on it. My cortisol is still not optimal, but its getting better. Sleep still isn't perfect either but my chronic insomnia has noticeably improved in response to CT, rigorously minding the light cycles, and now a magnetico pad.

Conventional wisdom's admonishment to restrict calories and exercise myself to death is simply not part of my plan to conquer my obesity anymore... That road led me away from health rather than towards it... And as they say, stupidity is defined as doing the same thing over and over expecting different results

My new strategy is 3-fold:

1) Use CT to work towards long term fat cell apoptosis: I actually invested in a commercial grade water chiller that I have hooked up to a submersible pump sitting in my bath tub. It is on a timer and starts chilling my water about an hour and a half before I get home from work. So far it has worked really great because it has allowed me to control and very gradually reduce the temperature as I have acclimated. It will cool down to 37 degrees and that is where I'm heading over the next few months (currently at 42 degrees).

2) Reduce Inflammation and Improve Mitochondrial Function: Although CT helps with this, I am also supplementing with Tumeric (Meriva), poly-resveratrol, CoQ10, Magnesium, D-Ribose, and am working on improving my dietary omega-3 ratio. I also recently purchased a Magnetico core pad. I have become aware that morbid obesity is a highly inflammatory state and CT-6 really confirmed my need to reduce inflammation when it talked about the inflammatory factors blocking leptin from binding to the receptor.

3) Work on improving circadian rhythm: To this end, I make a point to consistently get light exposure first thing in the AM and wear my blue blockers after 7pm every night. I am also consistently in bed by 10PM and sleep in complete darkness thanks to heavy duty light blocking curtains and a sleep mask. On the weekends, I make an effort to spend a few hours outside in the sun during the morning or early afternoon.

After reading CT-6, it appears that my results might be even better if I adopt a ketogenic diet. I am currently eating paleo and plan to gradually move in the ketogenic direction as the fall and winter approach. I have taken the carb reduction a bit slow because past efforts to go fully ketogenic have really backfired in terms of the effect upon my mood and energy. I now understand that this is because I have not been able to burn fat very efficiently. With my broken metabolism I have no idea how long converting to an efficient fat burner might take. I am currently planning to use D-ribose to temporarily fill in the gap as I attempt to make the adjustment. It is interesting that I have still gotten some of the benefits from the cold pathway without being fully ketogenic. The benefits I've seen so far are definitely motivating me to keep moving more towards optimal

Trinity

>
> My bed is aligned head side toward north and feet south. I confirmend this with a compas. When i place the pad under the mattress magnetic field reverses 180 degrees. Is this normal ?

If you use a ball/globe compass, you should see that North doesn't just reverse, it points down toward the bed, whichever side of the bed you are at. If you stand on the bed, and hold the compass above your head, it will point straight down.

I bet if an epidemiologist studied human populations in the same area you would also see a spike up in depression, autoimmunity, and cancer in these areas too. It also is happening during solar cycle 24 when we are at a historic double maximum that alters the Earth and Sun's magnetic field between the Tropic of cancer and capricorn making all life forms more sensitive to ELF EMF's. It sounds so hard to fathom because no one understands how Herbert Frolich's work and Becker's finding at Clam Lake Wisconsin tie to this story and are behind autism, cancer, and celiac disease..........Well, as crazy as it sounds.......it is precisely what is at play. If radioactive decay rates vary with sunspot activity, and [80% of Earth's internal heat comes from radioactive decay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth#Heat), this would mean that there's an additional and hitherto unknown mechanism by which the sun potentially influences global climate change. Quite interesting, to say the least.

During the absence of sun at night, while using the massive charges stored in cell water called the exclusion zone, cells become able to transiently able to become quantum coherent. This allows for regeneration and the creation of the living state. This implies that sleep was likely the original state that life began and living things evolved wakefulness during daylight hours. How would nocturnal animals have powered their metabolisms and mitochondria? Could it be the varying release of solar neutrino's at night? We know nocturnal animals reversed the diurnal process that uses sunrise and sunset as the major oscillator. Could they also use other formation of cosmic radiation from the Van Allen belts at night? John Ott thought about this possibility. We know IR light is present day and night to affect ATP creation and help yoke their circadian systems with other frequencies of light. These frequencies must not be reflective, but must be absorbed within the cells and tissues to work. Once absorbed by the atoms in light the light is changed in its frequency and in its color. That light is then re-emitted by cells for more down steam effects and signaling (Fermat's law). When the incident light is during daytime the incident rays are between 250-780 nm. That light re-emitted from the visible spectrum is in the extreme low UV range. Proteins have been selected for and built by nature to pay attention to these specific frequencies of light in the visible spectrum during day time. This is why certain amino acids are coded for in DNA and why all proteins are made from these amino acids by life (20). The resulting color a cell re-emits is the photonic signal at the detector proteins in adjacent cells and structures. Life really is color coded, and it the code likely extends way past colors we can see.......................

# Thread: Be careful of what you wish for, you just might get it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/be-careful-of-what-you-wish-for-you-just-might-get-it.7979/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### curves64 — 2013-08-22T21:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/be-careful-of-what-you-wish-for-you-just-might-get-it.7979/post-93422>

I've been seriously thinking about moving out of San Francisco since the EMF series came out. As Dr. K refers to cities like SF as "EMF soup" or an "EMF tsunami," I've been motivated to find a new home. We've done a lot of looking at places outside of the city but, I have to admit, I've been a little reluctant as it's been hard for me to imagine moving away from my close proximity to the ocean. Right now I live only 4 minutes away from the most spectacular little beach where I swim and earth and watch dolphins and seals play to the backdrop of the Golden Gate Bridge and the Marin Headlands. Recently I discovered Alpine Lake and Mt Tam (a 20 minute drive) which have hiking trails with streams and water falls. Truly spectacular. For a former City Girl like me, these discoveries have been mind altering. See, I've lived in the SF Bay Area since 1986 and never took off my high heels long enough to discover the lusciousness of my surroundings -- that is, until now.

Well, be careful of what you ask for. Last week my boyfriend accepted an offer for a position in a city just outside of Portland, Oregon. I'm going to keep my place in SF for a while, but do plan on spending large chunks of time up there in the trees. Although they call this part of Portland the "Silicon Forest" I'm sure living in a house with a yard where I can earth all day long will be a big improvement over living in an apartment in SF. I'm super curious to see how much better I'm going to feel in a less altered field.

Besides generally feeling better and watching the weight around my tummy disappear, I'm wondering if anyone has any suggestions for experimentation?
