### Kristi Lambert — 2015-02-03T23:30:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-154650>

This is an awesome thread!!! Thanks so much, it confirms my current timing and my goal to eventually fix myself to wake up with the sun (while only eating when there is light) - I used this thread and one other and have been going outside for 10 minutes at sunrise and grounding and then eating BAB - here is the other thread, which is also awesome:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...arly-and-bab-timing-question.7250/#post-87589](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/#post-87589)

Thanks so much, all of you, as this is working so well and I am sleeping longer and better all the time! xo

### Daniel Koetter — 2015-04-28T14:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162642>

My alarm was set to wake me up at 6:30 am. I woke up this morning at 6am, the sun was up and I used the bathroom then went back to bed till my alarm rang at 6:30 and I made breakfast. At what time did the 30 minute countdown begin? At 6am or 6:30 am?

### Lahelada — 2015-04-28T14:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162644>

At the time when you were both a)in a vertical position and b) your eyes were seeing daylight.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-28T15:02:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162646>

To clarify: if you looked outside the window or daylight entered and this is how you found out that the sun was up is the point when your clock starts. If you were to live in w windowless basement,went to the bathroom in the dark,returned to bed in the dark then your clock has not started.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-28T15:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162650>

So for the clock to start daylight has reached your retina even jf you lie in bed still. Conversely if you lie in bed awake in the dark it has not started

### Daniel Koetter — 2015-04-28T15:10:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162651>

[Lahelada](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/lahelada.6531/) Thank you! I have wondered that for a while.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-28T15:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-162654>

There is another limiting factor.
Lack of daylight is cancelled out by tjme of day. If you were to sleep in a dark room ,no windows until midday then you have lost the 8-11 beneficial frequency window and your metabolism is already out of kilter -no re-coupling etc (tensegrity blogs)

### AJC — 2015-05-14T23:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-164299>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=162646)
>
> To clarify: if you looked outside the window or daylight entered and this is how you found out that the sun was up is the point when your clock starts. If you were to live in w windowless basement,went to the bathroom in the dark,returned to bed in the dark then your clock has not started.

Lahelada, thanks for posting this. I have been looking around for awhile to try to figure this particularity of the Leptin Rx out. I am just starting out on the Leptin Rx and sometimes wake up at 4-5 am wide awake. However, I don't want to eat my BAB then because I don't want to "set" my clock for this time chronically. Lately, I've been setting my alarm for 6:15. I still wake up before it goes off, but now I just lay in my darkened room and wait for it to go off before going outside, then eating the BAB. I gather that this is the right way to do it.

Another question along these lines: I'm starting the Leptin Rx with my mom, who has to wake up at around 5 due to her job. Since this is before the sun rises, what would be the best way to still simulate "daylight" and effectively start the clock so it can be coupled to the BAB?

Thanks everyone.

### Frank Peters — 2015-08-14T18:39:52+0300

I am learning that timing one's first meal with first light has something to do with the physics of light and the biophysics of light's effect on the body. So far, I've learned (from [Jack's Podcast of June 25, 2015](http://fattofitmyquest.com/75-dr-jack-kruse-on-his-leptin-prescription-theory-as-neurosurgery-without-the-scapel/)) that light affects leptin and "leptin fundamentally works with light cycles.". (Scroll forward to 10 minutes and 52 seconds to hear Jack mention it.).

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-14T18:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-174674>

> [Frank Peters said:](/goto/post?id=174503)
>
> OB,
>
> Thank you for pointing this out. I did read it. But I didn't find an answer to my Q or didn't interpret what I was reading well enough.

[http://caloriesproper.com/meal-timing-and-peripheral-circadian-clocks/](http://caloriesproper.com/meal-timing-and-peripheral-circadian-clocks/)

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-14T18:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-174675>

Light entrains the Central Circadian Clock. Food Entrains the Peripheral Circadian Clocks. Big Breakfast in the AM sun is the key. And Make it protein heavy. That's really all you need to know. Now you just have to do it.

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-14T18:51:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-174677>

From the article entraining Central and peripheral Circadian:

**Morning bright light and breakfast (FEO) kickstart peripheral circadian rhythms, and one readout is diurnal regulation of known circadian genes in the periphery. This happens differently (almost predictably) in different tissues: liver, a tissue which is highly involved in the processing of food, is rapidly entrained by food intake, whereas lung is slower.

Starting the central pacemarker with bright light _in the morning_ but skimping on the peripheral pacemaker by skipping breakfast represents a circadian mismatch: [Afternoon Diabetes](http://caloriesproper.com/afternoon-diabetes-and-nutrient-partitioning-2/)? Central and peripheral circadian rhythms work together. Bright light and breakfast _in the morning._**

### Frank Peters — 2015-08-14T19:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-174681>

> [Joe Gavin said:](/goto/post?id=174675)
>
> Light entrains the Central Circadian Clock. Food Entrains the Peripheral Circadian Clocks. Big Breakfast in the AM sun is the key. And Make it protein heavy. That's really all you need to know. Now you just have to do it.

Hey Joe,

Thanks! Yep, I am learning. I've being doing the Leptin reset since mid June. However, I've been in a recurring restart though (four times now since mind June) because I am eating more carbs than are prescribed in the protocol. For example, a recent restart was because I gorged on Cap'n Crunch for breakfast and a pizza and philly cheese steaks for lunch and dinner. I am not worried about these restarts because I've noticed that each one lasts less than the last one. Their mix is changing too. The restart before this was a two-day binge on junk food. But the most recent was a day's long binge on bananas, peaches and blueberry's. With these, I am noticing that I am not as hungry for the grain based carbs and I am off the rails for a shorter time. So at least something is moving in the positive direction.

During these restarts, I wondered whether my body is telling me something like, "Screw this buddy! It's summer time; we're going to eat." Don't know the answer; I'm speculating.

### Joe Gavin — 2015-08-14T19:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-optimal-meal-times.8134/post-174682>

>

However, I've been in a recurring restart though (four times now since mind June) because I am eating more carbs than are prescribed in the protocol. For example, a recent restart was because I gorged on Cap'n Crunch for breakfast and a pizza and philly cheese steaks for lunch and dinner. I am not worried about these restarts because I've noticed that each one lasts less than the last one. Their mix is changing too. The restart before this was a two-day binge on junk food. But the most recent was a day's long binge on bananas, peaches and blueberry's. With these, I am noticing that I am not as hungry for the grain based carbs and I am off the rails for a shorter time. So at least something is moving in the positive direction.
>
> During these restarts, I wondered whether my body is telling me something like, "Screw this buddy! It's summer time; we're going to eat." Don't know the answer; I'm speculating.

Need to do it daily. Just a big ass breakfast filled with protein. literally as big as you can eat. the cravings will disappear. and carbs are ok in the summer, when they grow. Emphasis on grown food.

Not fucking Cap'n Crunch and pizza dude.

You'll get there. **Just focus on breakfast for now, just that one thing.** I have 4 eggs and meat or sardines or something every single fucking day. Along with at least a liter of good water (pelligrino or spring). And I don't eat again until an early-ish dinner.

You'll get there man! Just do one thing at a time!

[http://www.deandwyer.com/57/](http://www.deandwyer.com/57/)

Joe

### NeilBB — 2013-09-02T22:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating.8142/post-94696>

OK, most aspects of seasonal eating are pretty obvious and intuitive to figure out. And It's clear you want to be much lower carb in shorter light cycles and definitely ketogenic in winter, animal fats over tropical oils, etc. But as we approach fall and winter I was thinking about some subtleties that will come up.

Its obvious you shouldn't eat much fruit at all, but is it a big problem in terms of cell membrane PUFA-architecture for you to have, say, a squeeze of lemon juice in your water for vit C and flavor in the winter, for example? Or to use canned (BPA free of course) tomatoes in a crock-pot meat recipe in winter? A strict literal interpretation of "seasonal eating" I suppose would not even allow these variations, but I think even Jack has some tomato sauce based recipes in the winter section of the epi-paleo cookbook.

Another way to phrase this question might be, are fruits like lemons and tomatoes and say avocados relatively OK in winter because they are not particularly sweet? Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-02T22:12:25+0300

> I was just going to say that Josh. We are WAY more complicated

Not much new under the Sun...Around 620 A. D.

"A few points are worth mentioning: First, one of the greatest innovations in Sun Simiao’s encyclopaedia was the eminent position accorded to gynaecology. Earlier texts like the Essentials from the Golden Coffer (Jin Gui Yao Lüe 金匱要略, composed in the Eastern Han period by Zhang Zhongjing 張仲景) or On the Origins and Symptoms of the Various Diseases (Zhu Bing Yuan Hou Lun 諸病源候論, composed in 610 by Chao Yuanfang 巢元方) contained brief sections on disorders and treatments specifically targeting the female body, but these were always placed, like an afterthought, at the very end of the text, to be followed only by paediatrics. Information on diagnosing or treating the female body in the other Han dynasty classics (Inner Classic of the Yellow Emperor \[Huang Di Nei Jing\], Classic of Difficult Issues \[Nan Jing\], Treatise on Cold Damage \[Shang Han Lun\]) was minimal and limited to fleeting comments. By contrast, Sun Simiao placed his section on _**‘Prescriptions for Women’ at the very beginning, after the introductory scroll. Moreover, he began his gynaecological section with an essay that emphasised the need for any ‘gentleman engaged in the art of nurturing life’ to devote particular attention to this topic because ‘women are ten times more difficult to treat than men’, and yet are the mainstay of the family because of their pivotal role in reproduction. As a consequence, he devoted a full three scrolls, or roughly ten percent of his Essential Prescriptions to this topic and eloquently argued for the need for ‘separate prescriptions’ and special care for women’s bodies and minds.**_ He thereby set the stage for the fully-fledged emergence of gynaecology as a respected medical speciality in the official, government-sponsored system of medical training, research and publications in the Song dynasty several centuries later.
Related to this, I would argue that Sun Simiao radically altered the meaning of a key term in Chinese medicine, namely ‘nurturing life’ (yang sheng 養生)**_. In the early history of Chinese medicine, the notion of health and longevity, achieved and maintained by personal cultivation, was the overarching goal, the pivot around which elite medical authors contemplated and composed medical literature, especially in the context of materia medica and theoretical literature. This concept included such practices as dietetics, alchemy, a reclusive lifestyle, gymnastic and breathing exercises to stimulate the circulation of qi throughout the body, sexual cultivation (which, while emphasising the female orgasm as an essential component of complete intercourse, aimed at the male practitioner’s health benefits) and visualisation meditation. By reinterpreting the meaning of ‘life’ in a social, moral and cosmological context, Sun Simiao was, to my knowledge, the first author to widen this circumscribed set of individual practices, centred on improving the individual practitioner’s health and prolonging life. He included the female body in the larger perspective of life beyond just the individual body, as well as the survival of the family for generations to come, and ultimately the altruistic ideal of benefiting society and the macrocosm at large."_**

[http://www.jcm.co.uk/product/catalo...ugs-transforming-bodies-and-cultivating-life/](http://www.jcm.co.uk/product/catalog/product/view/10293/nurturing-life-in-classical-chinese-medicine-sun-simiao-on-healing-without-drugs-transforming-bodies-and-cultivating-life/)

# Thread: My most memorable sunrise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-most-memorable-sunrise.8143/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-09-03T16:20:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-most-memorable-sunrise.8143/post-94745>

I experienced an incredible sunrise last year, standing in the sand, immersed in the waves, listening to the seagulls, and smelling the ocean. It was spiritual for me. But my most memorable sunrise was back when I was young, and still in school. For some reason I can't recall, my parents dropped me off at school about an hour early. I am guessing that the roads were icy and I couldn't ride my bike. I do remember that I didn't want to walk.

I moved to the lee of the building, near the door, facing east and watching the slowly lightening horizon. Later I was joined by a couple other kids. I was desperately cold, standing in my coat, hat and gloves. There wasn't a lot of talking. The wind was relentless, as it often is on the plains of Texas. I remember the hairs on my legs stood out so hard that it was painful when my jeans brushed my legs.

The view to the horizon was unobstructed. I watched with such anticipation for the sun to finally rise. If only I could hold out long enough... Then, there it was! A tiny golden sliver, just visible. Yay!! And it got bigger and bigger, more quickly now. I could feel it on my almost frozen face. And I felt warmer, yes I did! But how can that be? It hasn't fully risen, hasn't really gotten warm at all. I didn't care at the time, I was just transfixed.

Later the doors finally opened, oh yes! I went into the building, and got cold. I remember sitting in the cafeteria, shivering. Others were urging me to open my coat, let the heat in. But no way was I going to do that. Might as well ask me to jump off a cliff! I was so cold. If I had known then what I know now, I would have found a window somewhere facing the rising sun, and continued to raise my energy and cortisol higher.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-04T00:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-most-memorable-sunrise.8143/post-94762>

mine is on my personal FB page

My sons are the epitome of the Tourette blog...tics, stutters, OCD, nocturnal enuresis....etc.

That said, they are doing incredibly well, couldn't tell anything is wrong looking at them.

Yesterday my 8 yr old had his first day of school...no tics, perfect speech, enough energy for soccer practice, no leg cramps, no outbursts....it was great!

After he settled in the evening his body seemed to explode in issues....he started throwing his head back, grunting, coughing, tapping his hands, lower eyelid droop....he just fell apart in minutes.

Why did this happen? Over stimulation fallout? What can I do to help him?

Thanks.

### BJK77 — 2013-09-04T17:51:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pediatric-neuro-question.8150/post-94806>

The first two nights after school started we had some issues with my son. Nothing like what you've mentioned, but just extreme moodiness and aggressive behavior. The type of behavior we used to see when he played video games. His school recently added wifi and has the kids working on laptops a few hours each day  We've been talking about homeschooling for awhile now and I think this may be the kicker that finally makes it happen. I can't be sure it's the wifi at school that caused this. It could just have been getting back into the routine and the long day at school, but I'm doubtful of that because he loves school and he doesn't have problems like this when we have long days out and about.

We found after those first two days that if we have him go outside immediately after getting home (barefoot of course) he didn't have any more episodes and was back to his normal calm and happy self. We also put our Magnetico pad under his mattress, so I'm hoping that's helping as well. I bought a Trion-Z bracelet and have him wear that throughout the day now too, so I can't be sure exactly what's helping, but I'm guessing it's a combination. I honestly don't really care as long as he's his happy self

### prAna303 — 2013-09-06T16:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pediatric-neuro-question.8150/post-95027>

One thing about fruit that i always had second thoughts about is:

Even if an apple grows in my region, was it here from the beginning? Is it put here by us humans because we liked it from another part of the world... This really makes a difference for me because i can eat berries like crazy in late summer / fall but when i eat fruit that has been placed here by us my system shuts down. Apples is a great example and other things like pumpkins...

When i look back at ancient texts, the only thing that was in my personal region was berries, nothing else.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-06T16:33:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pediatric-neuro-question.8150/post-95029>

>

>
> Thanks.

Come to think about something William A. Tiller wrote in some magazine... He thinks that many persons with these problems are simply misunderstood. They are out of sync with their brains and the physical body. This perhaps got right during summer when time did not matter, days comes and goes in a natural way. When he got into a schedule everything got out of sync once again.

### Linz — 2013-09-06T12:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.8153/post-95011>

Jude,

Perhaps eating it with fish or shellfish is the best way to improve o3/6 ratio. I think bacon with scallops is the best breakfast ever. Not sure how much is too much though.

You won't find grass-fed because pigs can't live on grass. They need similar nutrition to us - nearly always given as proprietary feeds with measured protein/fat etc. In the UK they were often fed 'swill' in the past - made from reject hotel/hospital/retail food which was all boiled together. It's not legal anymore because of the disease risks if it is not heated properly.

For the best quality look for outdoor reared, locally produced and organic, and for details about curing/processing. And it shouldn't leak grey water bubbles or steam when you cook it (means water has been added during processing). Pigs that are given access to some natural feeding (ie allowed to forage for acorns) are very rare here although outdoor pigs will find a few grubs, worms etc. Indoor pigs have horrible circadian and social conditions.

I now buy most bacon from a local but non-organic supplier. It tastes completely different but does cost nearly double the supermarket price.

### Jude — 2013-09-06T13:20:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.8153/post-95016>

Thanks for that Linz. Think the stuff I've consumed over this past winter has stuffed my ratio...for the worse. Have to start raising my own or catching some of our feral lot!

### AnnieW — 2013-09-10T15:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bacon.8153/post-95441>

Run, don't walk away from turkey bacon. That stuff is so disgusting. My husband used to eat it every morning. I would get nauseous just from the smell.

You don't have to buy grass-fed bacon. That's nearly impossible to find unless you have a Whole Foods or farm close by (and a huge bacon budget). I think Dr. K even said you don't have to worry about buying it nitrate/trite free. I was spending almost $10 for not even a pound of nitrate free bacon. Then I started going to Costco and buying just regular Kirkland bacon (four pounds for $15-woot!). I actually like that bacon better because I can't taste the sugar in it. The brand of nitrate free bacon I was using had a noticeable sugary aftertaste that was causing cravings for me. So just check the sugar content.

# Thread: Iâ€™m a believer . . . itâ€™s all about the field
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2m-a-believer-it%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-all-about-the-field.8156/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-09-05T17:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2m-a-believer-it%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-all-about-the-field.8156/post-94889>

I said earlier that I would post an update after being MIA and taking a huge break from technology for the last several months and Iâ€™m finally re-entering the world of technology very slowly so I thought Iâ€™d share a bit of what I found.

I am flying my freak flag high and proud and coming out to say that I am a total believer in all Jack has been telling us. I have absolutely zero doubts about the fact that my field dictates my health. I have struggled with so many odd afflictions the past several years â€“ everything from extreme muscle weakness to the point of barely being able to walk, to bloating, constipation, memory problems, brain fog, numerous food intolerances, etc, etc. Thatâ€™s a quick summary that doesnâ€™t even break the surface of what Iâ€™ve been dealing with. I have been super tight with my diet for the last 3 years â€“ NO grains at all, fruit may have been a handful of berries per YEAR, no nuts/seeds, no dairy for the last 18 months. I eat grassfed liver every single day. All my meats are organic, pastured/grassfed and vegetables are organic. Iâ€™ve been doing epi-paleo ever since Jack brought it to our attention. I CTâ€™d diligently every morning before work for months and months (Iâ€™ve slacked a bit this summer). Weâ€™ve been following the light cycle to the best of our abiliy â€“ lights out (propane lanterns and candles used) when the sun goes down. I felt like I was doing everything right, but I was getting nowhere.

This summer I took a major break from technology and got very serious about altering my field. We turned wifi off in the house except for one hour a day. We still have plans to only have wired internet, but just havenâ€™t made it happen yet. We will soon though! I took some extra time off work. I made sure I was outside more than I was inside - always barefoot and oftentimes touching nature too â€“ trees, etc. I started doing yoga and meditating outside first thing in the morning. We had a few vacations planned where we just totally decompressed. We traveled across the country to Olympic National Park and spent a week hiking and just exploring nature. I was worried the flight might affect me, but I used what I had learned here and had no problems â€“ grounding my feet while flying, took loads of CoQ10 beforehand and drank tons of good quality water.

What did I find? Nearly all of my health complaints went away. My energy level was fantastic, no more muscle weakness, no problems with bloating/constipation/digestive issues at all. In fact, I could eat lots more foods that I couldnâ€™t tolerate previously. I actually ate some melon this summer (more than I probably should have because the mosquitos loved me for it), berries, even an apple! I didnâ€™t go so far as to have a banana though  I had a few nuts â€“ not much, but previously nuts tore my stomach up immediately. No problems this time at all! Onions used to cause major problems for me â€“ Iâ€™ve been eating them lately with no issues at all. Brain fog? Whatâ€™s that?! I have read and re-read Jackâ€™s blogs that I printed out and I feel so much smarter. Itâ€™s all finally sinking in and I can understand what heâ€™s saying and where heâ€™s headed (well, some of it anyway!). Before I could read and re-read stuff and it just didnâ€™t sink in. I couldnâ€™t comprehend anything. My brain was fried. Itâ€™s like Iâ€™m a completely different person.

Iâ€™ve been back to work for two days now â€“ two days in the office anyway. I can work at home three days a week, so Iâ€™m lucky there, but not lucky enough! After just two days in the office surrounded by so much office equipment things are already starting to slide backwards:

\- Iâ€™ve actually gained 3 lbs in 2 days! I forgot to mention above that I lost weight and my body comp was great during my time away from everything. Iâ€™m already feeling flabby and poochy again and no, itâ€™s not my imagination. The new clothes I bought this summer for my newly emerged shape are not fitting!
\- I had a few berries with my breakfast this morning and I was bloated within 20 minutes of sitting at my desk.
\- My right hand tingles and gets ice cold within 30 minutes of typing (and I have an external keyboard so Iâ€™m not right on top of my laptop even)
\- I can feel the brain fog coming back and Iâ€™m losing my ability to focus and really pay attention and be present in each moment
\- My mouth gets extremely dry shortly after arriving to work. During my â€œbreakâ€ I barely drank anything at all. In fact, it was hard to force water down. I just didnâ€™t feel like I needed it. Now Iâ€™m back to easily drinking 1.5 gallons of water a day again. Iâ€™ve obviously got some intracellular dehydration going on. It just amazes me how quickly it happens.

Thereâ€™s so much more, but Iâ€™m working from home today and my wifi time is up for the morning, so Iâ€™ll leave it at that for now. Just wanted to share with everyone that itâ€™s so very important to do your own experiments and figure things out for yourself. I was so frustrated before when I truly believed I was doing everything right and getting nowhere. Itâ€™s painfully obvious to me now that what Jack says is true: itâ€™s not about the food like I previously thought â€“ itâ€™s all about the field! Your field determines how your body will react to the food, your field determines everything!

Now I just need to figure out how to win the lottery so I can live this life permanently

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-06T14:57:39+0300

Do you give in or up too easily? Have you surrendered? Surrender often emerges out of life’s losses, brokenness, failures, sufferings and a self-discovered deep disgust with your current life path and way of life. From the wreckage we learn, grow, and evolve to live a new way. In everything we face, we can choose to become better or bitter. How we view the failure with our attitude depends upon how we use it. Never surrender your dreams to life's circumstances; always count on your attitude and aptitude to create a new road to get to your destination in life.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The Link to Becker's infamous 60 minutes interview.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-link-to-beckers-infamous-60-minutes-interview.8158/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-06T01:21:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-link-to-beckers-infamous-60-minutes-interview.8158/post-94923>

[http://andrewamarino.com/blog/?page\_id=486](http://andrewamarino.com/blog/?page_id=486)

Dan Rather: You may have heard of itâ€”a Navy project called Seafarer. The original name was Sanguine. Itâ€™s a $700,000,000 submarine communications system that for the past decade has been a very expensive idea in search of a home. In every part of the country where the Navy has set foot talking Seafarer, there has been an uproar. Homefolks, politicians, scientistsâ€”they all turn out to have at it. Will Seafarer ruin the scenery? Will it be a major target for enemy warheads? And most important, what about reports that it could be harmful to the people who would have to live with it?â€¦ Captain Charles Pollack is the man in charge.

Capt. Pollack: The antenna would have about 2400 miles of antenna cable. If you draw a line around the extremities of that antenna-arrayed layout, it would encompass about 4000 square miles.

Dan Rather: So, somewhere in the good old U.S. of A., Pollack has to string out 2400 miles of antenna cable, buried a few feet underground. It would look something like thisâ€”a pattern resembling loose strings in a tennis racket. The intersecting lines would be about 3Â½ miles apart, and the whole thing would cover 4000 square miles of field and forestâ€”some of it along existing right-of-ways, like roads and powerlines, some of it through newly cleared paths. Is it safe?

Capt. Pollack: Yes, absolutely.

Dan Rather: Absolutely? Well not to people like this scientist. Are you telling me thereâ€™s a possibility that electric current, generated in a fashion such as this, could possibly cause heart disease and/or stroke?

Dr. Becker: Yes.

Dan Rather: You have to know that thatâ€™s a mind-blowing thought for a lot of people, including me?

Dr. Becker: Iâ€™m aware of that.

Dan Rather: Dr. Robert Becker is Chief of Orthopedic Surgery and a medical investigator for the Veterans Administration in Syracuse, New York. We have to pause here for a bit of explanation. Historically, the scientific community, almost in its entirety, has maintained that, to be harmed by electricity, you had to be shocked or burned; that the low-level doses surrounding us most of the timeâ€”from electrical appliances in the home, from power transmission lines or from the Navyâ€™s Seafarer projectâ€”could do us no harm. Thatâ€™s the Navyâ€™s argument. Now, are you telling me itâ€™s fair to say, accurate to say, that a housewife is exposed to more low frequencies in her home in the course of doing her day-to-day chores than she would be from Seafarer?

Capt. Pollack: Many, many times more.

Dan Rather: Youâ€™re certain that is a scientific fact?

Capt. Pollack: That is a scientific fact.

Dan Rather: Dr. Becker wouldnâ€™t disagree with that. What heâ€™d say is that you may not be safe, even in your kitchen. For twenty years, he and his staff have been experimenting on the effects, if any, of low-level radiation on living things. He is one of a small, but growing group of scientists around the world who are turning up information making them believe that low-level electrical fields do affect us. For instance, using very low voltage currents, he has made broken bones that wouldnâ€™t heal by themselves grow together again. And like most scientific discoveries, itâ€™s a double-edged sword. If those carefully controlled low-level currents can heal bones, well, it makes people like Becker wonder about uncontrolled electrical fields from household appliances, powerlines and Seafarer.

Dr. Becker: I was a member of the first ad hoc committee to evaluate the biological studies that were performed for Project Sanguine. And I most certainly sat there and listened to several studies that had very definite effects, yes. Animals that are exposed grow at a slower rate than control animals. A number of projects have shown this to be true. The second area in which definite effects do appear is that exposure to this type of field **seems to produce stress**. (This is where the PVN blog comes in. His work describe stress like pregnenolone steal syndrome, hormone 101 and 102 blogs when you read Becker's accounts)

Dan Rather: Is it true that the Navy repressed that report for better than two years?

Dr. Becker: The Navy did not disseminate the report widely.

Dan Rather: This is the report that heâ€™s talking about. It was done at the request of the Navy by a group of top scientists. They reviewed experiments performed on possible effects of ELF radiation on living things and raised some red flags. That was in 1973. The report finally got out a year ago.

Capt. Pollack: Now, in looking at some of the early experiments, there were effects noted. There were differences of opinion amongâ€”among the scientific people, as to whether those effects were significant. There were also differences of opinion as to whether they were deleterious. Itâ€™s our position now, and I fully support that position, that we have not seen any significant deleterious effects that can be attributed to a Seafarer systemâ€¦.

Dan Rather: Of most concern to people, though, were those reports of what the grid might do to them. The Navy countered: More scientists were following up the original studies. But the folks here had heard stories about what happened to some of the original scientists.

Dr. Becker: We know of, I believe itâ€™s five, specific projects in which positive results were obtained, when the projects were terminated and the money just disappeared. There was no more to continue the work.

Dan Rather: Now, is this a definite pattern?

Dr. Becker: It appears to be.

Dan Rather: That when a study begins to find that there may be adverse effects, that the money dries up?

Dr. Becker: Not even adverse effects. Any effect.

Capt. Pollack: What we had to do is try and determine which of the research efforts appeared to be the most fruitful, where we should apply the money and where we should apply the resources in order to get the best overall understanding.

Dan Rather: Meanwhile, the Navy has called the National Academy of Sciences to oversee and evaluate further experiments. And the NAS Committee has issued a one-sentence interim report saying that, so far, they think Seafarer is safe. But Dr. Becker isnâ€™t impressed. Some members of that NAS panel have previously testified publicly that radiation, similar to that of Seafarer, isnâ€™t harmful. And Becker maintains it would be awkward for them to change their minds in public.

Dr. Becker: For example, if a person has already publicly gone on record that the voltage field from a transmission line, a million times stronger than that from the Sanguine antenna, is harmless, then obviously he cannot do an about-face and say the Sanguine antenna may be harmful. So that a number of people on this committee, I would feel, have a pre-bias.

Dan Rather: Is what youâ€™re trying to say that weâ€™re playing with a stacked deck?

Dr. Becker: I think so, yes.

### cinnamon — 2013-09-06T04:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-link-to-beckers-infamous-60-minutes-interview.8158/post-94961>

Here's a very interesting summary of the participation of Robert O. Becker and Andrew Marino in the Hearings about New York State approval of high powered transmission lines in the early 1970s. It adds some context to the CBS interview. I haven't followed all the links yet, but looks like there are links to research done by Becker and Marino.
[http://andrewamarino.com/Testimonies/testimonymarino\_6.html](http://andrewamarino.com/Testimonies/testimonymarino_6.html)

Also, the site has a link to a [pdf file from 1998](http://www.rebprotocol.net/November2007/Marino%201998%20%20POWERLINE%20ELECTROMAGNETIC%20FIELDS%20AND%20HUMAN%20HEALTH%2080pp.pdf) that provides a lot more background about Dr. Marino and his involvement with Dr. Becker and with the hearings. It makes for an interesting read (and possibly might be a good source for a thriller screenplay!)

# Thread: What is an electro magnetic field?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-an-electro-magnetic-field.8160/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-06T02:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-an-electro-magnetic-field.8160/post-94936>

[http://www.who.int/peh-emf/about/WhatisEMF/en/](http://www.who.int/peh-emf/about/WhatisEMF/en/)

**Key points:**
The electromagnetic spectrum encompasses both natural and human-made sources of electromagnetic fields.
Frequency and wavelength characterise an electromagnetic field. In an electromagnetic wave, these two characteristics are directly related to each other: the higher the frequency the shorter the wavelength.
Ionizing radiation such as X-ray and gamma-rays consists of photons which carry sufficient energy to break molecular bonds. Photons of electromagnetic waves at power and radio frequencies have much lower energy that do not have this ability.
Electric fields exist whenever charge is present and are measured in volts per metre (V/m). Magnetic fields arise from current flow. Their flux densities are measured in microtesla (ÂµT) or millitesla (mT).
At radio and microwave frequencies, electric and magnetic fields are considered together as the two components of an electromagnetic wave. Power density, measured in watts per square metre (W/m2), describes the intensity of these fields.
Low frequency and high frequency electromagnetic waves affect the human body in different ways.
Electrical power supplies and appliances are the most common sources of low frequency electric and magnetic fields in our living environment. Everyday sources of radiofrequency electromagnetic fields are telecommunications, broadcasting antennas and microwave ovens.

### Arcole — 2013-09-10T01:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-an-electro-magnetic-field.8160/post-95364>

I finally bought a Trifield 1000 EX meter and found out that I have been working every single day for the past 5 years in a magnetic field of easily between 10-25 mG constantly, peaking to off the scale whenever I got too close to my adding machine calculator or the base station radio I use. Oh dear Heaven! I have moved those items as far from my work area as possible so that now my field reads in the "safe" zone. I also went through an elimination process of unplugging and replugging each item as well to be sure it was indeed those two pieces of equipment causing the trouble. I can limit my use of them but unfortunately I cannot do away with them entirely.

My question is two-fold. These items have very high magnetic fields but the electric field and the RF field are very low on them. Is that unusual?

Also, what kind of shielding is advisable to enable me to use the calculator without frying my circuitry? I remembered reading in one of the blogs that magnetic fields are the worst offenders. My laptop also read off the scale at home. Will a draping shield cloth or gloves help with the magnetic portion of using these items since the electric portion (at least on the calculator and radio) isn't bad?

I have been so on the bubble with getting my protocols to come together lately. I am sincerely hoping that the elimination of this magnetic field will be the tipping point that puts me squarely on the positive side!

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T01:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-an-electro-magnetic-field.8160/post-95365>

Hi Arcole. Can you ground them?

[http://www.lessemf.com/fabric.html](http://www.lessemf.com/fabric.html) (search for the word "prong".)

Perhaps the alligator clip can ground them? Hopefully it won't burst into flames or nuttin...
I use one of those to ground my laptop computer.

### Arcole — 2013-09-10T18:19:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-an-electro-magnetic-field.8160/post-95452>

Hey, @nonchalant! Just spoke to the guys at LessEMF and they said grounding would not help with the magnetic field issue. Only metal shielding stops magnetic fields. So I have moved the offending equipement far enough away so that my personal field is okay.

I do wonder just how much effect on my body that level of exposure to the higher magnetic field has had and how long it will take to know if moving these things has helped me. The rest of my home and workspace is pretty clear. I can't use my laptop at home without some major reworking in my workstation, but I do most of my typing on a battery powered plain text writer called an AlphaSmart 3000 which gives off NO offending readings. So the laptop hasn't been an issue. The biggest field influences on me have been the calculator and radio at work.

Anybody have any practical results with cleaning up their magnetic fields?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-09-06T19:08:08+0300

Any county highlighted in blue is at higher risk for a bad EMF field (and probably many other things).

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-06T19:14:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-us-population-lives-in-146-counties.8167/post-95047>

And look at TN......the one spot that lights up is where I found I had to leave.............Take a look at the Gulf coast along I 10........anyone see what I see? not a lot of fake light or Wifi........and you'll find a lot of conifer trees.........that is a big clue of where you might want to live.

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-06T19:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-us-population-lives-in-146-counties.8167/post-95050>

>

unfortunately - yes my county is on the map. though several counties to the south aren't - they also have access to Chesapeake Bay/Ocean, and good ground water....

Idaho looks great - and while it has conifers, it doesn't have access to the ocean, nor is it at sealevel....

### cantweight — 2013-09-06T19:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-us-population-lives-in-146-counties.8167/post-95052>

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-06T19:46:57+0300

### Gracelind — 2013-09-07T03:37:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/half-the-us-population-lives-in-146-counties.8167/post-95106>

A coast-to-coast flight will show you the vast amount of open space there is, especially West of the Mississippi.

The map is misleading for California. Except along the coastline, many of those blue counties also have a concentration of population in a few cities, with large tracts of open land.

Ironically, in some of those open areas the main signs of humankind are the silvery strands of wire stretching from tower to tower along the Grid.

# Thread: Adapting to the change of seasons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adapting-to-the-change-of-seasons.8168/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cinnamon — 2013-09-06T23:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adapting-to-the-change-of-seasons.8168/post-95079>

I was checking out the sun altitude tables [here](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php), and see that there is only about a week left when the sun will reach the 50 degree angle required for UVB rays to penetrate the atmosphere here in Toronto. I think that means I best hurry out to top up on Vitamin D synthesis in the next few days!

Thinking back to last year at this time (and this year in March or so for our friends in the Southern Hemisphere) lots of us started finding we weren't doing as well. So, what's your strategy going to be for this change in seasons?

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-06T23:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adapting-to-the-change-of-seasons.8168/post-95080>

I'm changing clients and I'll stop working in an emf death trap

Other than that I'm going to be spending as much time as feasibly possible outside

### Dextery — 2013-09-07T00:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adapting-to-the-change-of-seasons.8168/post-95084>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=95079)
>
> I was checking out the sun altitude tables [here](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php), and see that there is only about a week left when the sun will reach the 50 degree angle required for UVB rays to penetrate the atmosphere here in Toronto. I think that means I best hurry out to top up on Vitamin D synthesis in the next few days!

I have never looked into tanning beds as I am currently in Arizona...on the border with Mexico.

The value of sunshine D3 Sulfate as opposed to D3 supplementing. Dr Stephanie Seneff, MIT [http://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/swine\_flu\_obesity\_vitamin\_D.html](http://people.csail.mit.edu/seneff/swine_flu_obesity_vitamin_D.html) Lots of vitamin D3 and other things.

This Mercola article talks about UVA sunshine making Nitric Oxide for protecting the heart muscle. [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/07/15/sun-exposure.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/07/15/sun-exposure.aspx)

Louis J Ignarro won a Nobel in Medicine for his work in Nitric Oxide in the early 1990's. Wrote a book, NO more heart disease.

Other substances to make Nitric Oxide are Citruline and Argenine.

With what we now know about the photoelectric effect, it is a wonder that Canadians and Alaskans are alive without the photoelectric effect having much effect on those people.

Does the long day length of summer make up for the shortage in the winter?

My strategy is the same 2pm full sun half naked for 30 min regardless of the temperature.

# Thread: Determined to find a way
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 119
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Determined — 2013-09-08T01:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95167>

Ok, so I'm being brave & starting a journal on the forum cos u all look like a great bunch of ppl & I haven't seen any nasty stuff - looks like a v safe place for newbies like me to share our journey!
To try and summarize - I'm 52 & have tried oh so many ways to feel 'right' over these yrs. It all sort of started abt 14 yrs ago w fatigue. I tht I was gng thru menopause but found out I had Hashi's. & high homocysteine, low B12 & iron, so the Dr wanted to start me on thyroxine but having always been into ' natural' & a vegetarian for the previous 5 yrs, I wanted to avoid that. So I googled and looked and found all the good looking raw vegans exposing the wonders of raw veganism & so i was off (again, experimented w this in my 20,s).

I def felt & looked better for awhile.& also took a b12 & iron supp for awhile. And there were no antibodies by abt 6 mths later, so Thinking i was on a winner continued for another 18 mths until I felt like I was running on 3 cylinders once more, despite eating the best fuel I could put into the gas tank ( organic ,raw no wheat, green smoothies, cacao, all that stuff to try & improve the brain fog & energy slumps) & the thyroid Abs came back. And high BLOOD glucose levels w it- yikes - too many bananas? Felt thirsty all the time - no matter how much I drank - it all makes sense now Dr K! And those 3 cylinders sometimes lowed to 2.

Something was wrong so more research & reading abt raw paleo (Aajonus - but I can't eat raw meat - yet - sorry!) so have been pretty much cooked paleo now for the last 4 yrs & I knew it might take a while to restore the good fats in my depleted body - boy, did I wolf down the meat when I finally decided to let go of raw vegan! I remember my mums lamb roasts smelt oh so good & always loved my tiucker! In the early days It took a while to get over my conditioning & be convinced that high good fat wasn't going to make me enormous! That's taken a while too.

When I wasn't getting the results that others on Paleo said they were - wt loss / energy/ bounding out of bed in the morning - I internalized that & tht it was just me - ie ate too much of this or that, ate a gluten free cake for morning tea that week & so on. Went to a BHRT Dr, got on some missing hormones ( low Pg, low DHEA,LOW Vit D) / vitamins (progesterone 10%, DHEA cream, Vit D 10,000 q 2 days, iodine spray daily) (she also prescribed Estrogen cream but as I'd NEVER used OCP I flt like a pumped up balloon on this so stopped it- water retention/ tension) But even w these, which i tweaked in various ways (where/ when & how much) I still never felt right.

She then tried me on HGH but I also felt very wrong on this too ( & worried abt the mercury preservative in it!!) & whilst others reported wt loss, energy etc i felt like crap - like a wrung out dish rag - worse on it but tried it for a while 'might come good' so that was a few 800 buck vials down the drain!

Finding Dr Kruse's site feels like I have found the missing piece of my health puzzle! In ONE week I feel a lot better already - calmer, balanced, more centered, in bed by 8:30 , up at 6 , almost jumping out of bed, got 5 out of 6 cylinders gng some days which is amazing to me, who wld have tht that something as simple as a circadian rhythm could be so profound. Well i do now after reading Jacks blogs. SO interesting & though my brain hurts I absolutely love it!

I only started last Sunday (2/9/13) -bought the book on Kindle on Friday & went Epi Paleo on Saturday - turned out all the lights on Sunday & started trying to reduce EMF exposure ( this is going to be harder - live in an apartment block in Cns - pick up 5 Wifi's arnd me, plus my work entails catching a Dash 8 at 10,000 feet to get to/ from work twice a week (got the barefeet on the metal Dr - wld a Colantotte magnet help???) plus that dang donut hole over us & abt 1000 more bucks to get the Magnetico to Oz - more to that which I won't go on abt here).

Even a simple version of CT has been astounding for me. Been taking a cold shower to start- dont have a compression shirt or skin thermometer yet ( any recommendations for where to buy fellow Aus Krusers?) turning off the hot completely - the aversion to that was pretty strong to start off & I think cold Cairns tap water- whilst it takes your breath away is not the temp it shld be ( planning to head up to volcanic Lake Barrine one weekend - that will be FREEZING right now as its still chilly on the Tablelands).

But got to visit my DH who lives 1500 feet above sea level in Qld this weekend - we do long distance due to work -yes MORE air travel - not good! & def notice difference in my sleep quality after that but he does have a VERY COLD SHOWER water & red skin - takes your breath away!

Just a bit of resistance Day 1 w the lights out & no TV, but reading him bits n pieces to try & win him round. whilst a healthy dude who meditates twice a day - his stress levels always better than mine because of this - he is still leptin resistant evidenced by a big gut & a love of scones w jam & cream & cake & potatoes & porridge - reckons its his Scottish ancestry. He can see a difference in me tho so tryin to b supportive & hoping my effects will continue & convert him too.

Anyway that's my story so far, got a long way to go ( realizing my cortisol levels are upside down - still a bit buzzy at night & sleepy in AM, but SO much better already) and hope to see more results soon. Have never ever done this on net b4 so I must b v inspired! Thanks Dr Kruse for all ur amazing thoughts & look fwd to learning MORE & healing this body of mine (& hopefully helping others too - ESP the pregnant women I work w) xxxx

What a great start to a journal Determined .... and I love your handle!! There is so much great stuff here - so keep reading and asking questions! Please listen to Jack's Podcasts - they give you much needed information quickly.

If you can swing it ...become a silver or gold member. The webinars and Q&A's are fantastic.....but, of course, you don't have to. Dr. K has a very direct way of speaking and you can ask questions. There was a special - I don't know if it is still available tho.

once again - welcome to this great journey of a lifetime - we won't be getting a do over ...this is it....

### Determined — 2013-09-11T10:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-95581>

Not a great day - feeling a bit whacked right now! Had to travel Brisbane to Cape York - abt 4 hrs all up flying & no access to my normal paleo food etc here. I normally bring a pile of good stuff w me but couldn't because of the connecting flights. So whilst I grounded the whole way I'm still stuffed but prob not as bad as usual!
I did manage to eat my BAB & haven't been hungry all day just drinking ice water.

But boy I am looking fwd to my pub meal tnt - a 'man size' steak is in order.

I'm still mentally digesting Jacks blog as its pretty devastating for us Aussies to hear that & I don't feel I have much power to do more than I can at present! Heck I can't even access RO water & def not desalinated RO.

then I had a taxi driver telling me the 1 in 50 yr disaster is due and that it will be a cyclone bigger than Yasi ( terrifyingly giant) will prob hit Cairns this yr. OMG! My DH were talking abt buying property on the Sunshine Coast eventually - maybe sooner the better.

I haven't done CT for 2 days ( my cold shower version) but will tnt & looking fwd to getting home to my own bed on Friday so I can get into it well & proper w the ice.

Good stuff to report- I haven't had any indigestion for a week ( was thinking I had H Pylori) & I'm feeling much more aware & mood elevated w more energy for others - which hasn't been the case for some time - other than my job which I love.

This stuff really makes you re-think your priorities - I'd been studying (another) Masters ( Nurse Pract Women's Health) & working FT -w all this weekly air travel & now I'm wondering how to downsize & simplify life & thinking i may not continue this study.

cos I'm not sure i want to take on the full medical model in my work - Nurses ( including Nurse Practitioners in Aus where they r trying o break into a tight System controlled by AMA & big Pharma will def not be able to practice outside the box like JK is able to - you' d be burned at the stake!!!)

The truth be told I've never had faith in western medicine hence why I studied Acupuncture but working in the system has always given me a good income w some form of 'security' - ie regular money. and the way i work has allowed me to work in a wholistic and supportive way w the women I serve in pregnancy/ post partum - been a Homebirth midwife & remote work in Indigenous communities for 15 yrs. However do I want to continue to work in the western medical model?
Plus the stress of the study is shocking for my cortisol. Is it worth it ? So much to ponder!

I love that you are a homebirth midwife! Wow that is a rewarding career! I was a trained doula before I got very sick and could no longer work. I had wanted to become a midwife, but after coming back from such poor health I have a passion to help others do the same, to become whole again. I am in school to become a nurse practitioner and I understand what you mean about western medicine bullshit. I have a approached school with a mindset that I will regurgitate the crap they want me to and then go practice with a doc that gets it. I have found a few holistic practices and an integrative practice that I would be thrilled to work with. I refuse to dish out more of the shit that hurts people. Big pharma can kiss my ass! lol

You have a special place in my heart as a midwife....such amazing women....

The midwife that delivered all 3 of my children just saw my daughter for her first well woman visit....that was such an odd, yet beautiful feeling. My daughter hopes this woman will still be practicing when she has children of her own....at 13 yrs old that better be a long ass time from now...lol

Keep blogging and growing and healing

Flow can go anywhere in the CNS and PNS. If your function is not present in an area it is a sign to me either your electron splitting ability is low or your field sucks........ flow is directed toward your neocortex at night and away from it in day time........ and eventually to your bad places when add in enough energy by way of the 3 fundamental laws of nature. ...best way to fix it? Go Epi paleo and carefully read the last two blogs and the many more coming........CT is for all the time.

When we lose energy we emit black box radiation. Large transitions can take place sharply and rapidly in response to minute inputs or losses of energy of a coperative, coherent or metastable environment. When you have a lowered energy state, you are no longer stable.....so any other destabilizing force( think energy loss) has massive effects in many systems.

### caroline — 2013-09-28T02:17:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96336>

I don't seem to have a problem then ....but my ipad is a whole other kettle of fish! My feet were going numb when I was using wifi - I was tethering my laptop to my phone. Remember that Dr. K. is very easy to listen to and you get so much info so much faster. You haven't had the benefit of a skype informational consult.......he is very direct and easy to understand and kind and always listens ...nothing like the blogs. When you talk to him it is always a two-way conversation.

Everyone needs to listen to the sex webinar at least once in their life and also the one with Erwan Lecorre to get a much better understanding of this man.

The webinars are usually 60 min. - sometimes 90 minutes ....and you can listen to them whenever you have time.
if you do the live Q&A you can ask questions ...usually on anything.......but you don't have to listen live - you can listen at anytime you have time.

You can always become a gold member \[you have access to everything at any time\] and then if it doesn't suit you - you can drop it ....no problem at all...

I think if you reduce your EMF exposure throughout your house - you will be fine. Have we talked about your bedroom?

### Determined — 2013-09-29T00:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96411>

Youd be VERY welcome to do that Caroline - when i find that special place I could supply the land, but how about a TIPI? (assisted a women birth her baby in a tipi once, by candlelight, by a river, very beautiful, i was the only person clothed, prude that i am! She spent her post natal time there too, lotus birth, ie placenta still attached to bub, lucky it was a hot spring & the cord was off in 2 days! a record) He must be 20yrs old now that memorable tipi baby!

It was such a lovely 'space' to be in, I would LOVE a tipi, plus you can stand up in there & have a fire in the middle, how cool is that. The American Indians knew how to do it.

That brings up memories of the first time I went to work in the Australian desert with the Pitjantjatjarra people. As part of our cultural orientation we medical mob got to camp in the desert with a local Aboriginal family.

stayed in a 'wiltja' - just a simple roof shelter made of branches. Of course we also had the luxury of a 'swag' ( a mattress with canvas outer) & it was winter & there was ICE on the ground in the morning. I often wondered how the hell Aboriginal people could have lived in the desert in winter (or Summer!), where it is MINUS degrees o/n in winter, replete with ice and no clothing. They didnt use many skins in the desert because of their very nomadic lifestyle required for water/ hunting.

Of course fire, dogs and other human bodies helped, but now i know they were COLD adapted. When you see photos of desert family groups prior to white contact, they all had the most beautiful shiny skin & eyes, slim muscular bodies and a very proud stance- i saw the last remnants of these people when i first went out bush in the early nineties.

None left now & of course metabolic syndrome, T2DM and all the other neolithic diseases run rampantly through all the tribal groups (though there are always rumours of a few ppl who choose to still live traditionally out in the desert...spooky stories of sightings etc). But so Sad to see the destruction in families and lives i seems to be on a larger scale than what we see in the West. Very premature deaths.

Back to the fantasy you mentioned, might be able to supply most of your requirements but not sure about the handsome fish grillin man, you might have to BYO!

### caroline — 2013-09-29T01:43:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96419>

What an amazing story - I am going to read this again and again.....
I have never heard of a lotus birth .....why is that better? You have had amazing experiences - I hope you are keeping a diary and write a book some day.

I really hope that you listen to the webinar with Erwan Lecorre and Jack ......I know I seem obsessed or maybe possessed but I think that you will understand why when you see it.

Once, when my DH was in the hospital for an extended period of time I stayed in the lodge that the hospital provides.
Staying there as well was an aboriginal Lady and we talked daily as we were toing and froing to the hospital.

She left one morning very early before I was awake and I found a bag hanging on my door ... it contained a book she had written about the stolen generation - she was one .....she also wrote me a beautiful letter. She had the most kind and generous spirit - even tho she had suffered so much for various reasons .....she only tried to comfort me.......

### Determined — 2013-09-29T03:25:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96424>

thx Caroline, never tht abt writing a book, just cos my experience is like a lot of fellow midwives and remote area nurses (whom i know anyway). I do feel really privileged to have spent so much time w Aboriginal ppl and like your lovely experience, many have the most beautiful souls/ hearts that i have been very honoured to experience... I will tell you a story one day of one beautiful elder whom i went to a birth conference with & "spoke of the sacred duty of the midwife", all the audience was in tears! so was i! She told me a story of being sick in the desert prior to white contact, and of walking over 100 miles w her family to get some special herb to heal her. It was an AMAZING story!!!!

Lotus birth - just a snippet of its benefits..leaving the cord uncut & attached to the placenta till it drops off.... not everyone's cup of tea, but it does ensure baby gets ALL of its blood supply - about an extra 60-80mls after the birth (babys TOTAL blood volume is only 80 mls/kg e.g around 240mls all up in a 3kg baby), which is of enormous benefit (& the research is NOW affirming.. (mothers always instinctively knew - we havent cut the cord early at homebirths for as long as I have been around!), it has benefits for bonding ie cant move around much when a placenta is attached (the cord gets very dry, like a twig!) but some ppl believe that because its 'part' of the baby ie all came from the same beginnings (am sure Jack would be able to describe the embryology here!)- an extension of itself, that when the baby is 'ready' to let it go, ie its of no further use (some would argue its of no use once the blood flow has stopped) that its the 'right' time for it to separate, claiming baby is calmer, more secure, more whole as a consequence.

I don't really know about any of the so called 'spiritual' benefits, or of better human beings because of it, but i am supportive of parent's choices and if this is what they want to do, fully support them. What i do know, is that because of the 'appendage' of having a dry cord & placenta attached (specially wrapped/ salted etc), it does SLOW everything down. Ie Ppl cant come in & pass the newborn around, its just not practical, and so baby & mother often stay very cocooned in their own little blissful new world for a few days till the time comes when the cord separates & baby is free of the unwanted appendage! ie none of this going to shopping mall on Day 2 after birth if there is a placenta attached! Breaks my heart to see women up & about racing around straight after birth.

I think the Chinese are very right in nurturing the mothers 'Jing" (essence, vitality) very carefully after birth! Nurture your JING or will definitely come back to bite you (& take heed of Dr Jacks warnings about Circadian rhythms, good water & dietary proteins, & EMF as well - OF COURSE!).

### Determined — 2013-10-01T00:06:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/determined-to-find-a-way.8175/post-96542>

A little update as I sit and wait for my plane - stewing in EMF ....hmm
I'm feeling pretty GOOD - it's been 4 weeks on leptin reset and this is what I'm noticing...

Following circadian rhythms I'm usually asleep by 9pm - up naturally around 5:30am w the sun

Tolerating the BAB much more now - not 'gagging' on the protein - have upped the fat in morning though - raw cream in tea ( occasionally a coffee) eating mainly 2 meals a day w some Brazil's for my selenium around 2 pm & not a bit hungry

Doing CT twice a week - going up to 3x this week. Can bear it for around 35 mins - then I start getting shivery - but can keep going up to 45 - my max at present - I think my belly fat is shrinking but not sure - subtle - and no weight loss at all but my energy is fantastic so I'm going on that! Think the wt can wait - I'm about 10kgs more than I want to be.

Yesterday at work I got SO MUCH done - my mind clear & strong - I also have SO much more energy for others. I realise I used to clos myself off to all except the women I was seeing for care. Just didn't have any vitality to spare on anyone else - my goodness how I used to DRAG myself thru a day! Iam just absolutely AMAZED at how good I am feeling on more days than off now.

Seem to be sweating more ? Buts the 'build up' to the wet season is beginning here in the tropics - just the very start of it w increased humidity

Loving being more aware of nature - getting in the sun- touching lying rolling around on Mother Earth

Reading heaps more - fiction & non fiction - less on the computer and iPad ( wish I could read more of Jacks stuff though!!!) will have to kill trees and print the blogs out me thinks?- another book perhaps Dr K?

Don't watch any TV anymore so prob lots less stress that way too

Am convincing my DH who has seen a remarkable improvement in me - from daily adrenal fatigue to much more upbeat happy and energized person - he's even waiting for me to order my magnetico ( tax man be kind!)

All in all I ain't going back - only forward w this - most incredible thing ever.

Questions on my mind - when do u stop eating as per leptin reset? I'm not sure I'm sensitive & guess I'm NOT cos no wt loss tho other signs coming.

But hes still loyal to his Dr! This man lives on cream buns, chocolate & Coke (must have good genes or something!!)

In terms of wt loss -My diet is perfect Epi Paleo - no fruit - high fat - moderate protein ( xcpt for the BAB)

Example day
BAB: 2 lamb chops broiled in butter
3 eggs
Some organic broccoli & a little leek
Chai tea w coconut manna
Dandelion coffee w coconut manna

Lunch: usually nothing but occ a salad w prawns at the paleo cafe (fantastic to have this in Cairns)
Or a tin of Alaskan red salmon w Lemon juice & lettuce

Dinner : some protein ( ie a piece of snapper or a piece of GF beef) & steamed veges ( low carb but sometimes a bit of sweet potato - eg cauliflower, broccoli, zucchini) w raw butter of course!
2 pieces of dark cacao homemade ( YUMMY) chocolate

That's it !!!! Truly I shld be skinny!!! Nondairy no grains low low carb

### Dextery — 2013-09-08T21:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming-has-paused-at-least-in-the-arctic.8178/post-95266>

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/earth/en...o-actually-were-cooling-claim-scientists.html](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/earth/environment/climatechange/10294082/Global-warming-No-actually-were-cooling-claim-scientists.html)

Now we have this article that explains that the Arctic shelf of ice has grown to half the size of Europe in less than a year and the re-freeze time has not yet arrived for this year. Shipping lanes closed all the summer of 2013.

And we have the southern hemisphere with the ozone donut hole with more UVB getting through to the southern continents and oceans causing chaos in the food chain as well as more solar radiation causing illness.

The solar winds at work interacting with the Schuman Electro Magnetic Field.

I recall that Dr. Kruse said somewhere that the alarmists about global warming pointing to the Arctic sea ice receding for the last 15 years cannot explain the record amount of ice in Anarctica during the same period. I do not remember the reason though.

Just all confusion from the mainstream "experts".

"Eat like a shark, live like a epi-paleo polar bear" during the coming Ice Age.

BTW, when I visited Alaska, I learned that tan colored Grizzly Bears are primarily herbivores and live in the interior and really dark Grizzlies are mainly salmon eaters living along the coasts. Epi Paleo Grizzly Bears!

Soon I hope the breadcrumbs will come together into a loaf about climate change. LOL

# Thread: If the 1859 EMF event happens today the costs would be north of 2 trillion dollars
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T05:06:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/post-95310>

In 1859 known as the Carrington event was the most intense CME ejection in our modern history, creating auroras farther south than the Tropic of Cancer. Telegraph services experienced serious disruptions; random unreadable messages were coming through their instruments, and some operators even reported experiencing electrical fireworks sparking from their equipment.

Today NASA subcontractors estimate the risk of a similar CME is at 12% in the next ten yrs. If this were to happen today with the modern infrastructure all tied to the electric and semiconductor grids the damage to the world economy would be north of 2 trillion dollars, according to Homa Karimabadi.

Luckily, though, the Earth has a natural defense against the constant attack from the sun's CME by our magnetosphere. Earth's rapidly spinning metal core generates a magnetic field similar to that of an ordinary bar magnet. This magnetic field extends far into space at least 37,000 miles (roughly 60,000 kilometers). Most charged particles cannot easily cross magnetic field lines. Instead, something called Lorentz forces, created by the earth's magnetic field, deflect the particles' paths. The magnetosphere therefore acts as an invisible shield around our planet, barring the highly charged particles from the sun from plummeting through our atmosphere. There are holes in this shield with the largest one in the Southern hemisphere.

Despite its protective qualities, however, the magnetosphere also has vulnerabilities. Particles can still enter our magnetosphere by a process known as magnetic reconnection. Solar wind carries with it its own magnetic field, and when this magnetic field points in the opposite direction as Earth's magnetic field, the field lines connect in an explosive process that catapults charged particles into the magnetosphere.

Karimabadi and Loring worked with John Dorrelli, an expert on magnetospheric topological analysis of NASA's Goddard Space Center. Their work helps us find the location of the magnetic field lines from different sources, explains Karimabadi. Using NICS' supercomputing resources, they have become the first to create such a map to develop global kinetic simulations of the magnetosphere and space-weather effects. The team used Nautilus and Kraken to create their visualizations and simulations. A better understanding of how space weather affects our magnetosphere allows scientists to more accurately predict the impact of solar activity on our planet. With improved predictive capabilities, we can more effectively prepare for space-weather events.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T05:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-the-1859-emf-event-happens-today-the-costs-would-be-north-of-2-trillion-dollars.8179/post-95311>

The Earth’s magnetic field provides a protective cocoon, but it breaks during strong solar storms. These storms, also known as geomagnetic storms, can cause massive power outages, disrupt communications and navigation systems, damage Earth’s satellites and induce corrosive electrical currents through gas and oil pipelines that cause them to age faster and fail quicker. During periods of intense geomagnetic storms, flights must be rerouted around Earth’s magnetic poles to avoid the dangers of exposing staff and passengers to high levels of radiation. Astronauts especially are at risk of exposure to unhealthy or even lethal amounts of radiation, even when they are inside their spacecraft.

Because the sun can eject plasma in any random direction, the odds of a serious CME being aimed directly toward Earth are slim. However, the risks posed by dangerous space weather are not to be taken lightly . In 1989, a series of CMEs and solar flares caused a severe geomagnetic storm that left millions of people in Quebec without power for more than nine hours. Blackouts spread south across America’s northeastern coast. This geomagnetic storm was so intense that the northern lights, which are normally only visible in parts of Canada, could be seen as far south as Florida. The storm in 1859 was even more intense, creating auroras farther south than the Tropic of Cancer. Telegraph services experienced serious disruptions; random unreadable messages were coming through their instruments, and some operators even reported experiencing electrical fireworks sparking from their equipment.

CMEs pose a significantly greater risk to Earth than other potentially dangerous astronomical events. The likelihood of the sun emitting a CME depends on an 11-year solar cycle. We are currently in solar maximum 24 right now. During the peak of the solar cycle, the sun can emit several of them every day. At the minimum of the solar cycle, the sun may radiate only a couple per week. In the last 200 years, our planet has already experienced two geomagnetic storms intense enough to cost us billions, even trillions of dollars. There is an estimated 12-percent chance in the next 10 years of a solar storm of the magnitude of the 1859 Solar Superstorm hitting Earth. That would cause over $2 trillion in damage.” This amount is equivalent to a whopping 30 times the amount of damage caused by Hurricane Sandy in 2012, which cost the United States roughly $65 billion.

As our world becomes increasingly dependent on electricity, we also become more and more vulnerable to the consequences of severe geomagnetic storms. A storm that merely disrupted telegraph services 150 years ago would do a whole lot more damage today. “Imagine having no power and communication for over three to six months and over large regions of the United States. We are simply not prepared to deal with such a calamity,” says Karimabadi.

A geometric storm on the magnitude of the 1859 superstorm narrowly missed our planet in July of 2012. Some of you may remember that aurora were seen close to the equator last summer and many people in NASA did not believe it until picture came in to prove it. If this particular CME had occurred a few days sooner, our planet would have suffered devastating consequences similar to what happened 67 million years ago.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-06-17T06:25:31+0300

# Thread: Dehydration leads to Magnesium depletion by these drugs:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-leads-to-magnesium-depletion-by-these-drugs.8180/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
