### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-09T18:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration-leads-to-magnesium-depletion-by-these-drugs.8180/post-95340>

If you are losing water to your environment for any reason at all you will also lose magnesium......so what drugs can also exacerbate a field effect?

**What Drugs Deplete Your Body of Magnesium?**
Acid Blockers
Cimetidine (Tagamet)
Esomeprazole (Nexium)
Famotidine (Pepcid and Pepcid Complete)
Nizatidine (Axid)
Omeprazole (Prilosec OTC)
Pantoprazole (Protonix)
Ranitidine (Zantac)
Rabeprazole (Aciphex)

Antacids
Aluminum and magnesium hydroxide (Maalox, Mylanta)
Aluminum carbonate gel (Basaljel)
Aluminum hydroxide (Amphojel, AlternaGEL)
Calcium carbonate (Tums, Titralac, Rolaids)
Magnesium hydroxide (Phillipsâ€™ Milk of Magnesia)
Sodium bicarbonate (Alka-Seltzer, baking soda)
Antibiotics (a few examples)
Amoxicillin (Amoxil)
Azithromycin (Z-Pak)
Cefaclor (Ceclor)
Cefdinir (Omnicef)
Cephalexin (Keflex)
Ciprofloxacin (Cipro)
Clarithromycin (Biaxin)
Doxycycline (Doryx)
Erythromycin (E.E.S.)
Levofloxacin (Levaquin)
Minocycline (Minocin)
Sulfamethoxazole and trimethoprim (Bactrim, Septra)
Tetracycline (Sumycin)
Antiviral Agents
Delavirdine (Rescriptor)
Foscarnet (Foscavir)
Lamivudine (Epivir)
Nevirapine (Viramune)
Zidovudine, AZT (Retrovir)
Zidovudine and Lamivudine (Combivir)
Blood Pressure Drugs
Hydralazine (Apresoline)
ACE inhibitors:

Enalapril and HCTZ (Vaseretic)
Angiotensin II receptor blockers:

Valsartan and HCTZ (Diovan HCT)
Diuretics, loop:

Bumetanide (Bumex)
Ethacrynic acid (Edecrin)
Furosemide (Lasix)
Torsemide (Demadex)
Diuretics, thiazide (and any combination drug that contains HCTZ or hydrochlorothiazideâ€”dozens of drugs contain this)

Candesartan and HCTZ (Atacand HCT)
Chlorothiazide (Diuril)
Chlorthalidone (Hygroton)
Hydrochlorothiazide or HCTZ (Hydrodiuril)
Methyclothiazide (Enduron)
Metolazone (Zaroxolyn)
Diuretics, potassium-sparing:

Possibly the potassium-sparing diuretics, however this is not conclusive
Diuretics, sulfonamide:

Indapamide (Lozol)
Cardiac Glycoside
Digoxin (Digitek, Lanoxicaps, Lanoxin)
Central Nervous System (CNS) Stimulants
Methylphenidate (Metadate, Ritalin)
Cholesterol Agents
Cholestyramine (Questran)
Colestipol (Colestid)
Corticosteroids
Betamethasone (Diprolene, Luxiq)
Dexamethasone (Decadron)
Hydrocortisone (Cortef)
Methylprednisolone (Medrol)
Mometasone (Elocon)
Prednisolone (Pediapred Liquid)
Prednisone (Deltasone, Liquid Pred, Sterapred)
Triamcinolone (Aristocort cream)
Inhaled corticosteroids:

Flunisolide (Nasarel, Nasalide)
Futicasone (Flonase)
Triamcinolone (Azmacort inhaler)
Hormone Replacement Therapy / Oral Contraceptives
Diethylstilbestrol (DES)
Estradiol (Activella, Climara, Combipatch, Estrace, Estraderm, Estring, EstroGel, Femring, Menostar, and many others)
Estrogen-containing drugs (hormone replacement therapy and birth control)
Estrogens, conjugated (Premphase, Prempro)
Estrogens, esterified (Estratab)
Estropipate (Ogen)
Ethinyl estradiol (found in many birth control pills)
Levonorgstrel (found in many birth control pills)
Immunosuppressants
Cyclosporine (Sandimmune, Neoral)
Tacrolimus (Prograf)
Nonsteroidal Aromatase Inhibitors for breast cancer
Anastrozole (Arimidex)
Osteoporosis
Raloxifene (Evista)
SERMs (Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulatorsâ€”used for breast cancer)
Raloxifene (Evista)
Tamoxifen (Nolvadex)
Toremifene (Fareston)
Sulfonamides
Sulfa antibiotics, some diabetic medications
MISC:
Alcohol
Calcium supplementation (prolonged or in excess)
Coffee
Estrogen dominance
High cortisol levels
High-sugar diet (glucose, fructose, sucrose)
Malabsorption of any sort (celiac or Crohnâ€™s disease, pancreatitis, crash dieting, anorexia)
Mineral oil

Check your BUN/creat ratio if you are on any of these drugs......you will likely find that your level is greater than 7:1 and you will know you got a big water issue and a smaller magnesium one.

> Hydrocortisone (Cortef)
> Methylprednisolone (Medrol)
> Mometasone (Elocon)
> Prednisolone (Pediapred Liquid)
> Prednisone (Deltasone, Liquid Pred, Sterapred)
> Triamcinolone (Aristocort cream)
> Inhaled corticosteroids:
>
> Flunisolide (Nasarel, Nasalide)
> Futicasone (Flonase)
> Triamcinolone (Azmacort inhaler)
> Hormone Replacement Therapy / Oral Contraceptives
> Diethylstilbestrol (DES)
> Estradiol (Activella, Climara, Combipatch, Estrace, Estraderm, Estring, EstroGel, Femring, Menostar, and many others)
> Estrogen-containing drugs (hormone replacement therapy and birth control)
> Estrogens, conjugated (Premphase, Prempro)
> Estrogens, esterified (Estratab)
> Estropipate (Ogen)
> Ethinyl estradiol (found in many birth control pills)
> Levonorgstrel (found in many birth control pills)
> Immunosuppressants
> Cyclosporine (Sandimmune, Neoral)
> Tacrolimus (Prograf)
> Nonsteroidal Aromatase Inhibitors for breast cancer
> Anastrozole (Arimidex)
> Osteoporosis
> Raloxifene (Evista)
> SERMs (Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulatorsâ€”used for breast cancer)
> Raloxifene (Evista)
> Tamoxifen (Nolvadex)
> Toremifene (Fareston)
> Sulfonamides
> Sulfa antibiotics, some diabetic medications
> MISC:
> Alcohol
> Calcium supplementation (prolonged or in excess)
> Coffee
> Estrogen dominance
> High cortisol levels
> High-sugar diet (glucose, fructose, sucrose)
> Malabsorption of any sort (celiac or Crohnâ€™s disease, pancreatitis, crash dieting, anorexia)
> Mineral oil
>
> Check your BUN/creat ratio if you are on any of these drugs......you will likely find that your level is greater than 7:1 and you will know you got a big water issue and a smaller magnesium one.

Thank you for this!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### tellmisty — 2013-09-10T01:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-double-posting-in-forums-and-blog-comments.8184/post-95371>

Hi all,

Several of you have reported problems with the site and forum recently. We are working with our new web host technician to resolve these issues as quickly as possible. Feel free to discuss the issues you're having in this thread, but please also submit a support ticket with a detailed account of the problem you're having so we can provide the web host with as much information as possible. It will help us greatly if you provide your IP address, OS, and browser. You can do this by visiting [www.ipcow.com](http://www.ipcow.com), clicking "More Info", and copy-pasting all of the information that site provides into the support ticket. That way our web host can search the error log for your IP address so we can try to isolate the issues.

Thanks for your continued patience. There may be downtime later this evening (after 12am CST, 9/10) for some scheduled site updates, but we don't anticipate the site being offline for longer than a couple hours at most.

### cinnamon — 2013-09-10T05:34:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-double-posting-in-forums-and-blog-comments.8184/post-95404>

I find the format of the blog posts seems to change sometimes. It will look like it is formatted maybe for mobile devices or something (side columns disappear, and font and style are different) and then will seem to randomly switch back to the original style? And the number of comments will change - noticed this on the last blog especially, where in that "mobile format" it usually had just one comment.

When posting in the forum, sometimes "go advanced" button led to a page where the post preview was there, but the edit box was empty and there was no active "Submit Reply" button.

"Post Quick Reply" button seemed to work, but sometimes there would be a message saying not allowed to post again in less than 30 seconds and to try again. I did NOT "try again" and my posts were posted (always just once).

All of the above difficulties were new in the last couple of weeks and I thought maybe had to do with changing providers so figured someone was working on it. I will try to log these events if they continue to happen - with screenshots if possible.

Oh, one older problem that still doesn't look like it's sorted is the avatars one. Only a few members have avatars in the forum. I have one set up in my profile, but it does not show. This is nowhere near as annoying as not being able to access comments on the blog posts or the double posting problems, but thought I would mention it is still an issue.

# Thread: Ovarian Cancer & Epi-Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### digital — 2013-09-10T02:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95381>

Dear Jack Kruse-

I want to thank you so much for the consultant we did with you back earlier this year.

My mom in law was diagnosed with stage 4 ovarian cancer, spreaded from the ovaries up to the lungs and was given 2 months with surgery impossible (massive surgery that would spin 12+ hours requiring two teams, each with 4 surgeons, and a high probably of other risks associated).

After her diagnosis in Jan 2013, we did the consultant with Dr. Kruse. She converting to epi-paleo diet, walked barefooted, took advantage of the photoelectric effect and took a few of the recommend supplements. Her Aug CT scan showed a major reduction of cancer to less than 20% of what it was. She just completed her 5-hr surgery moments ago and is in recovery now.

Thank you so much for your help and service!

\-digital

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-10T03:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95383>

What an amazing success. thanks for sharing digital

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T03:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95387>

Just 8 months, Digital? Sounds wonderful! Hope she recovers well from the surgery.

### digital — 2013-09-10T03:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95389>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95387)
>
> Just 8 months, Digital? Sounds wonderful! Hope she recovers well from the surgery.

Truly amazing. We actually started seeing the effects 3 months after implementing Dr. Kruse's protocols... the CT showed 30% reduction then (~ Apr). By July/Aug CT's the masses (cancer/tumors) stabilized (to 20% of original) and hence proceeded to surgery.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-10T03:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95390>

This is my job......When a human is down I must extend my hand.

How many conquests mankind has had in exploring outer space and yet we fail to explore their own inner space. In my view, it is better to look ahead and explore what we do not know, than look behind and regret things we should have done. Stop wishing things were easier, and begin to wish you were better! Keep your edge by continuing to work and improve the skills and knowledge that are useful to you by exploring new horizons instead of repeating yesterdays fallacies. Exploring your inner masterpiece bring you wisdom because you begin to realize when you do not know the right decision to take in life, you begin to imagine what would be the worse decision I could make and you seek to avoid it. There is a magic in this action of personal exploration. I am very glad your mother chose to begin! No disease is impossible to beat with quantum biology.......the problem is no one believes it.................YET.

That will change too.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T04:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95391>

> [digital said:](/goto/post?id=95389)
>
> Truly amazing. We actually started seeing the effects 3 months after implementing Dr. Kruse's protocols... the CT showed 30% reduction then (~ Apr). By July/Aug CT's the masses (cancer/tumors) stabilized (to 20% of original) and hence proceeded to surgery.

Can we ask her age?
Tell her she has a bunch of people online rooting for her.

### Determined — 2013-09-10T05:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95405>

That's an inspiring story - thanks for sharing some of it digital

### Gracelind — 2013-09-11T06:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95549>

Hi digital

Your MIL's experience is very moving and inspiring for me as we lost our mother to OVCA in 2007.

Has she had any chemo? Do you know her CA-125 results?

Please do update on her condition. I got to know quite a few OVCA patients in a forum when my mother was ill. It's the kind of forum where, when members leave, it's usually because they have died. I can't tell you how much women with OVCA need hope and inspiration ... and a way of eating and living that improves quality of life.

So thank you for posting her experience reducing tumor size. I'm so happy for her and all of you who love her.

### Gracelind — 2013-09-11T07:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95555>

Hi digital

I hit "send" too soon!

I also want to send my best wishes for your MIL as she recovers from her surgery. It's invasive in the extreme and requires a long recovery. With the Kruse Protocol, though, I hope it will be faster and easier than otherwise.

Remember to take care of yourselves, and you, take care of your wife. This must be very hard on her. We learned very quickly that when one member gets cancer, the whole family "gets cancer" in different ways--but it affects everyone's lives physically and emotionally. We often felt that our lives had been put in a blender and swirled around till they were almost unrecognizable. If you all are eating Epi-paleo, barefooting, etc, you'll all be healthier, too.

### caroline — 2013-09-11T11:45:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-95587>

Thankyou for sharing your story ......my prayers are with all of you....

### sienaflin — 2013-09-25T08:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-96050>

This forum is really helpful. I already recommend this site to my friend who diagnose with stage 2 ovarian cancer.

### Frankie L — 2013-11-20T04:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ovarian-cancer-epi-paleo.8186/post-101080>

I too was diagnosed with NHL-B Cell follicular Stage 4, 2B and was told by Doctor's it was incurable. Well, I healed it with diet and all the tumours are gone! I made sure my PH was 7.5 or higher and ate plenty of greens, no dairy and some supplementation. It worked and it baffles my onc.

A healing experience like yours gives so much hope to others.

CT and water pick up where light leaves.........and the diet must be strongly ketotic when no sun is out......loaded with DHA and EPA so Maresins can change our Macrophages from M1 phenotypes in strong light levels to M2 in low light levels.......M2 macrophages do not produce any inflammation and this is how DHA gets its anti inflammatory effect in the brain when light is gone for 6 months.

I do not get how people want to live like this. Today morning I took the 3 yo guy with me in the forest, and we hugged trees.. found some berries, and it was so nice, so silent and beautiful, a sacred feeling. ### cantweight — 2013-09-10T03:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95386>

Audrey, I think you were paying as much attention as I was when we commented on Jedeye's post....it was an ask jack....oops.

Anyway, this big fat tweak I made has been amazing. I am seeing results from it like crazy. Today was 132 gr fat breakfast.....cant imagine the looks i will get from people when they ask me what I am doing.

I have had twinges of hunger mid day....my breakfast is at 6am since I am getting the kiddies out to school again these days....so by 2 or 3 I feel peckish. A glass of raw milk has been doing the trick, sometimes a piece or 2 of bacon if there is any leftover in the fridge.

Then dinner is just fish and veggie or meat and veggie.

I feel great...I am able to handle a much more intense CT with that much fat...my belly got so red this morning that my stretch marks stayed purple for hours....it was weird.

My hair and nails are unreal...having a bout of acne....whenever I lose fat I know my body is detoxing some nasty stuff so just doing my best to help it along.

Just printed the monster blog....going to read now

Sorry again Jedeye!!

### Hemming — 2013-09-10T09:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95420>

When I cut out grains a year ago it helped massively but I think the last step was taken on the epi-paleo diet. On top of that my skin is softer than ever and I don't burn in the sun.

ladies - Fat and I have this amazing love affair - I can no longer eat massive quantities of protein at breakfast. but fat. oh HELL YEAH.. There are points where the only thing that I can stomach is BPC and scoops of coconut oil. What's crazy is there are days that that's all I eat until 2pm or so. follow your instincts on this one ladies...

### AnnieW — 2013-09-10T15:47:06+0300

### cantweight — 2013-09-10T16:06:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-fat-bab.8189/post-95443>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=95440)
>
> So what are ya'll eating to get that much fat in the AM? I could overdose on bacon every morning but it gives me indigestion.

Annie, my fat is coming from unsalted grass fed butter, heavy cream, and coconut oil. I mix it in my coffee. Jack doesnt recommend coffee for those in an altered field, with high BUN/creatinine ratio as it is deyhdrating....have to do your own N=1 there. (see recent ask jack about bullet proof coffee)

Perhaps a decaf chai tea or some other tea would be good as well. I have 3 tablespoons of each split into 3 drinks. I dont drink drip coffee so it is just 1 espresso shot and one hot water shot (americano) per cup. Not 3 cups of drip coffee....I would explode from caffeine!

I also eat a pastured egg or 2, or some sort of meat....bacon, leftovers....whatever is there.

This mornings BAB was 126 gr fat and 6 gr protein.

I had a similar experience to Gretchen. I forced high protein BABs for a while but was developing a severe aversion to them. Got to the point I was gagging and dry heaving trying to get down massive quantities of protein. Took me months to get to where i am now....but i really think I found my sweet spot. Hunger is mild to none, like gretchen I do the AM fat load and Im set till 2- or 3 o'clock. I have a glass of raw milk then and dinner around 6 of meat or fish and vegetable.

Feeling great!

# Thread: New from Texas needs help~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LynnF — 2013-09-10T17:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95448>

Hi guys, glad I found this place. Believe me when I say my search has taken me all over the Internet and to more than one doctor. But I'm probably in great company there. You all know how many blogs and sites there are, and how much conflicting information, too.

I've had digestion and weight issues for my entire life. I've tried everything under the sun. Now I am getting new food sensitivities added all the time, extremes from constipation to diarrhea, pain in my right side that sent me to the emergency room (for the first time in almost thirty years), and after seven hours and two shots of morphine, sent home with a "We can't find anything wrong, follow up with your doctor." I did, he sent me to a Gastro who handed me some MiraLax and said my problem is all about constipation. Well ok, then.

I'm here because I guess I'm on my own. I'm on Medicare so I think a more natural practitioner is out of my pocketbook and out of the question.

I'll find my way around here, any suggestions are welcome.  Hello, everybody!!

### nonchalant — 2013-09-10T17:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95450>

Hi LynnF, welcome!
Do you mean the hospital let you keep your gall bladder?

### LynnF — 2013-09-10T18:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95453>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95450)
>
> Hi LynnF, welcome!
> Do you mean the hospital let you keep your gall bladder?

Yes. They did! I thought it must be that, but they said no.

### Huck — 2013-09-10T18:59:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95454>

Starting out, make sure you follow the prescription for what to eat and when to eat it, minimize the use of artificial light, spend time outside in the yard barefoot, get plenty of sun, and do cold baths. Minimize RF, but you will need to do some reading to more fully understand what that means.

And don't get discouraged when you are not fixed in 2 or 3 months. Many of us have seen lots of small changes that have resulted in huge improvements over a year or 2. Don't worry if you don't understand everything you read here. I don't and I have been at it for almost 2 years, but I do understand the big picture. For example, you need to eat a big protein breakfast 1st thing in the morning. The most important thing is that you do it. It is nice if you also understand why, but it works whether you understand the why or not.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-10T20:21:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95457>

Hi LynnF, welcome aboard! We do have some members in Dallas. Maybe they will chime in with some doctor recommendations. Huck is right, it does take time for some to find the right path and feel better. We are all so unique. This site and the blogs are a great source of information.Good luck with your search!

### LynnF — 2013-09-10T20:32:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95460>

Thanks guys. I appreciate all the input. I'm trying to read Getting Started.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-10T21:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95473>

Read the getting started...........then Read the Leptin Rx and CT protocol..............then pay heed to the fake light............when the sun sets it must set in your house. Look to the glasses and the folks here will help you in understanding that. Brain Gut 11 and 9 do those details..............then the biggie............EMF mitigation. Best advice.........if you want to go it alone...........Read EMF 1 onward to the current blog post...........after you get the basics above sorted in your head..............My adivice.........start a journal and begin to ask the starfish here questions..............many people will lend you a hand. I watch the advice closely so if I think it is not so good I will causually mention that too............the key is to begin in earnest and do not look back. Check you beliefs about diet and biology at the door...........they are useless here and we are all about using nature's laws of the universe to guide what is best for us.............

### caroline — 2013-09-11T00:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-texas-needs-help.8192/post-95495>

Welcome LynnF......keep reading and asking questions and start a journal as Dr. K. suggested...

### Huck — 2013-09-10T20:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/post-95464>

This makes 2 years in a row I noticed the same pattern with my blood pressure.

Blood pressure is high and requires medication in winter. In late Spring, it starts coming down and stays at or near normal over the Summer. Starting in September, I see a significant jump in blood pressure of about 20 points (systolic). Getting lots of sun brings it down some. I'm expecting by October, I will be back on a low dose of medication with increasing dosage over the winter.

### caroline — 2013-09-11T02:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/post-95512>

Would a tanning bed help this?

### Dextery — 2013-09-11T02:30:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/post-95515>

In this pattern of BP. are you always in the same location? Do you change location between summer and winter. Thinking that you might be going from a no emf area in the summer back to an EMF hell starting in September.

What in your environment can be making your arteries and veins less pliable and more stiff starting in Sept resulting in your heart having to work a little harder? I do know that cinnamon and fresh ginger have the ability to keep the arteries and veins pliable.

Just some things to consider.

Last year, someone posted a link to a study that said sun exposure lowers blood pressure. I seem to be extremely sensitive to the sun and my blood pressure goes down and I lose weight in the summer. And I was in same location. I'm hoping to start spending a month or 2 further south (fall and spring) and see if I can extend my summer.

### JohnR — 2014-02-03T22:31:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-pressure-back-up.8193/post-108168>

**A bit more info on Blood Pressure needed**

I noted a lot of posts addressing cholesterol, and very little regarding blood pressure. Are there any general protocols regarding lowering blood pressure, especially for a generally fit mid 50s male? My local docs insist I take lipitor to lower cholesterol, (despite improvements in weight and LDL/HDL ratio) and lisinopril for high BP (150/92). I've complied for about a month and feel not so great - tired, gassy, and a bit weak sometimes below the belt (without getting graphic.) Any general protocols to lower BP would be helpful. Also finding a cardiologist in Westchester County, New York willing to at least take a shot to work with me to get off meds has proved fruitless after much searching so far. Thank you John R.

### caroline — 2013-09-11T09:36:24+0300

Then they get a job, get married, have a family etc. and become very obese and don't get outside anywhere near as much ...so no photoelectric effect and no grounding.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-11T10:19:42+0300

### nonchalant — 2013-09-11T17:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-at-the-picture-everyone-is-outside-and-thin-why.8197/post-95626>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=95577)
>
> Today we have so many clues to make our own life working in a world we see as hostile.

"A world we see as hostile" -- that's it in a nutshell. We work all our lives to make money to cushion ourselves from hostile nature. We insulate ourselves from it at all times, at all costs. When camping in the wild, we pack lots of equipment/food to keep safe. When going on an outing with young kids, we need a large vehicle to carry all the supplies. We worry about eventual retirement and not having enough buffer. We fight against nature our entire lives.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-11T18:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-at-the-picture-everyone-is-outside-and-thin-why.8197/post-95630>

Thinking about my childhood (my first decade was in the 60s). Life certainly has changed.

We played OUTSIDE spring, summer, winter, and fall. Built "forts" in the fields and woods, rode bikes constantly when weather permitted, had to be called inside for dinner and bedtime, loved winter outside - as long as my snowmobile suit and boot liners weren't soaking wet, and ate fruit off the trees in summer (mmmmm mulberries!) & tomatoes and cukes right off the vine.

No air-conditioning...just a big ceiling fan in the hallway the pulled the cooler air in through the house at night (but it was very noisy!). In the evenings we spent time outside playing (where it was cooler) and on the bench swing under the black walnut tree.

My Mom would hang clothes on the line outside to dry for six months out of the year (saved $ on electric bill too). Dad was an avid hunter and fisherman, spending weekends outside if he wasn't working OT. My brother trapped muskrats all winter, walking a good many miles each day.

I feel old...

At least I didn't throw in the "I used to walk five miles in the snow to get to school each day" . That was one of my great-grandmother's stories. She used to carry a baked potato in her mittens to keep hands warm and then eat it for lunch!

### prAna303 — 2013-09-11T19:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-at-the-picture-everyone-is-outside-and-thin-why.8197/post-95632>

### Hemming — 2013-09-11T19:43:04+0300

Her diet consisted of dark chocolate covered macadamia nuts, fried chicken and cheap wine. Her cholesterol was through the roof! I always remember people taking about it being 680.

She never had AC. Sharp as a tack and spry till the day she died.

The great part is that you can reset yourself at any time and start doing these things again. It is up to everyone to choose path to walk on now... I can see clearly, two distinct ways in my society today where the big crowd goes into iPad / IT based living and a few take the opposit way. Most of us live in a world without to much danger today, not that many of us is direct hit by a war for example. What i mean is that we can aim for a living in more sync with nature and still do some modern things...

I am writing this right now on a computer and in the next couple of hours i connect with nature once again. # Thread: If you want to know why Omega 3 PUFA are the one real anti-inflammatory O3 read this
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-want-to-know-why-omega-3-pufa-are-the-one-real-anti-inflammatory-o3-read-this.8198/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-11T01:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-want-to-know-why-omega-3-pufa-are-the-one-real-anti-inflammatory-o3-read-this.8198/post-95508>

PUFA omega 3's are difficult to understand from PUFA 6 chemistry because how they work is based upon quantum principles in the CNS using their pi electron clouds with iodine and the water in CSF. In the human neuro-immune system omega 3 PUFA's are converted by macrophage's to an intermediate compound called 13S and 14 S epoxy-maresin. Maresins are produced by macrophages from DHA and they exert potent pro-resolvin and tissue homeostatic actions on the MHC1 proteins. These alter their action. Resolvins are compounds that are made by the human body from the omega-3 fatty acids eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). They are produced by the COX-2 pathway especially in the presence of aspirin. This happens to be why aspirin is helpful in heart disease. No other reason. The aspirin effect is greater in men than women because men absorb more EMF than women to do electrify there DNA and protein water micelles. Experimental evidence indicates that resolvins reduce cellular inflammation by inhibiting the production and transportation of inflammatory cells and chemicals to the sites of inflammation. Maresin's promote the conversion of macrophages from M1 phenotype to M2 phenotype that specifically does not stimulate inflammation in the human body.

1\. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21601924](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21601924)

2\. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23504711](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23504711)

# Thread: I am New and need some guidance on how to start taking the supplements?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### suzgaray — 2013-09-11T20:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95634>

Hello Everyone!

My name is Suzette and I am new here today . I have purchased all the Jack Kruse top 10 supplements and I would like to know if there is a protocol for how to take them (which is first and what times of the days to take them?). So far, I was following Dave Asprey s Bulletproof diet and lost 70lbs but I am still severely obese, diabetic T2 and with a host of other illnesses. In this case, I wanted to start over again and try to do the Leptin Rx reset and the CT, so I will need all the optimal supplements I can get . It is my understanding that all fat soluble vitanmins should be taken together. Here is what I put together:

At Breakfast:
1\. DHEA
2\. D3/K2
3\. Omega 3 (Fermented Cod Liver Oil/Krill Oil)
4\. MSM
5\. Selenium
6\. Lugold's 5% solution
7\. CoQ10

At Lunch:
8\. One-per-day Multi-Vitanmin tablet
9\. BioPQQ

At Dinner:
10\. Vitanmin C 2000mcg absorbic Acid in my Ice Tea

At Bedtime:
11: Magnesium 800mg
12\. Melatonin 30mg

Any advice or suggestion would be appreciated.

### Optimalbound — 2013-09-11T21:58:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95641>

Welcome, Suzette! Congratulations on losing the 70 lbs already. Well done!

If I understand Dr. Kruse correctly, exogenous melatonin is not recommended (except possibly when doing long flights across several time zones), and that you should work on fixing your circadian rhythm and get off the melatonin supplementation.

Good luck! I think you've come to the right place.

### caroline — 2013-09-12T00:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-new-and-need-some-guidance-on-how-to-start-taking-the-supplements.8204/post-95649>

Welcome Suzette - I love your name! It seems you have done very well so far - great news.

You really need to read the easy start guides very closely. Start the leptin reset \[RX\] with epi-paleo.

read the easy start guide for C/T

follow circadian light cycles

Most importantly - check your EMF exposure at home and at work ....extremely important

Drink spring water only

Do you have Dr. Kruse's book? THE EPI-PALEO RX \[from Amazon\].....it will give you a great overview for all of this in simpler terms.

Please listen to all of his podcasts - they are a simple and quick way to get this information too.

And .....just keep reading all the blogs - especially the ones that pertain to you - to start with. Read and reread and ask lots of questions.

Please start a journal - It shows commitment and involvement and we can get to know you and help...

This is an amazing journey of a lifetime .....

I forgot to say .... it is really important to get testing done ...There is a lot of great detail about what testing you need in his book.

About all the stuff you bought .....In my opinion, you put the cart before the horse. It is more helpful to test first and read and then see what you might need..... someone else will have some ideas for you - I am sure

Hi Caroline,

I agree with you I am just so OVERWHELMED with too much information and being DEAF the only information I can get my hands on is what I can READ. I am at standstill point and feel paralyzed on not able to move forward trying too many different things out desperation to get off complete insulin for T2D, but since I am poor (Medicare) there is limit ability to get all these test done except HbA1c every 3 months and once a year VitD3 test that my PCP will allow :-(. I did do the WellnessFX test but it was just basic. So how do I start all over again?? I have read Dr. K's epi-paleo book many times, I fully understand what T2D have to do (but he strongly recommends a full battery of test BEFORE you start his regime, which I cannot afford so I go with my hunch and what I already know) and I want to start CT with dunking your face first, the slowing moving into deeper levels of CT. I have stopped the IF and started eating BAB in the mornings with my BPC and continue to eat LC (more like 80/20 effective) I just refuse to go back on 12 different meds like before (last 15 or so years!). I only willing to take fasting insulin when my BG is greater 200mg which happens about 20% of the time but 80% of the time it is between 120-160 mg and just keep focusing on getting the numbers down through diet and exercise, I DO take my nighttime insulin Levimer 30 mg faithfully. Since I stopped most meds and have lost 70lbs on Paleo/Primal diet I feel so much alive and better energy, BUT now after 18 months something has to change or I am done :-(.

Just venting my frustration :-( sorry. Many of us Deafies are the last group of people to get proper help because of language barriers, but I AM an avid reader and I try my best to do things on my own by learning through trail and error so far its been good journey, but I want to move out of this rut to lose the last remaining 100lbs and be off the last diabetic meds before I am 50 (48 years old now) that is my ultimate goal :-(.

I would do Fatty Acids Analysis and figure out my DHA/EPA/AA status.

Then decide between steak (O6) and oysters (O3) as general category.

Without the benefit of analysis, with history of SAD or Paleo diet my guess is that 6 months of mostly shelfish and fish diet is in order to bring up likely lagging DHA.

How to procced further without guidance from Fatty Acid analysis, I am not sure.
Could use suggestions.

One, CT intolerance supposedly indicate O6 excess.

Supplementation with fish or krill oil is out in my book, because of overwhelming amounts of EPA in them.

\-----------
diabetic T2 is indication that body expects long winter to come now

in winter there is cold, carbohydrates are not available,

increase fat consumption from summer 60% to winter 70%
\-------------
Good vitamins are usually worthless.
They contain minimal amounts of items that you may actually need and you do not need the other items.

Spectracell micronutrient analysis is an excellent way to figure out your status.

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)
Price: $350.00
(Or less than $100 with doctors orders + insurance)
\-----------------------------
Magnesium is big item.

Magnesium threonate ------gets to brain & spinal cord
Magnesium Malate------gets to skeletal muscles and cardiac muscles
Magnesium orotate (big one) key for ATP synthesis,

...

### BJK77 — 2013-09-11T23:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-95644>

After reading the other thread about some of the ladies filling up on fat in the AM and craving it now, I realized that is so me right now. All I want is fat and TONS of it. I'm not sure which fat to use with seafood though. Bacon and I don't seem to get along too well  At least not in large quantities. I have been using coconut oil with my seafood off and on, but I can actually see a difference when I do this. My skin and hair both lose the softness and moisture they gained when I adopted epi-paleo.

I do eat a ton of fat for my BAB, but lunch and dinner are always seafood for me. I add an avocado for some fat, but I'm not sure what else to do. Ideas?

I'm honestly not feeling seafood these days at all unless it's wild salmon sashimi, which I feel like I could live on! I had some a few weeks ago on a trip to WA and came back and drove over an hour to a great fish market in Philly to stock up. I bought 20 lbs and it's almost gone now! It's an expensive habit I need to break . . . not to mention I'm not too sure about the radiation from Fukushima and the problems that's causing with Pacific seafood.

I eat tons of avocado. That and sea veggies are my only carbs over winter, but even the avocado just doesn't seem filling enough these days. All I want is FAT and lots of it!!

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95659)
>
> BJ, when you eat CO your skin and hair suffer?

Not from the CO, but I think because it's blocking the all the good stuff from the seafood (DHA and EPA). I used to use coconut oil all the time before epi-paleo and when I first started using CO I had improvements in my hair and skin, but when I went epi-paleo the improvements were even greater. Now if I cut back on seafood or eat it with a saturated fat I notice I lose a lot of the benefits I've seen from seafood. Honestly, my hair is just amazing these days. I hate to say that and sound braggy, but seafood makes my hair unbelievably shiny, smooth and soft. It feels like silk. My hair stylist can't get over it.

Saturated fats block the absorption of DHA and EPA from seafood.

### Gracelind — 2014-02-14T09:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-110131>

Hi BJK77.

You didn't specify your issue with bacon. I've always loved it and didn't notice any problem till a couple of years ago. It now makes me cough and choke. I thought it was the dry crunchy texture, but, no. Turns out that "naturally cured" bacon is made with lots of celery powder or salt. It adds as much or more nitrate to the bacon as the "non-natural" sodium nitrate used in standard curing but being a veggie can be called "natural." Same thing for many processed meats such as sausage, ham, etc.

Turns out I'm extremely sensitive to celerty and celery salt! Who knew?

You may well have a different issue--just thought I'd mention one possibility.

Lover of salmon sashimi here, too!

### Ken23 — 2014-06-05T02:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129698>

Is pecan or some other type of raw nut butter safe to use when having seafood?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-06-05T02:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129700>

> [Ken23 said:](/goto/post?id=129698)
>
> Is pecan or some other type of raw nut butter safe to use when having seafood?

Depends on your inflammation status. In general though:

1.  it's easy to get too much Omega-6 fats from nut butters because they are too easy to eat
2.  nut butters have higher probability of going rancid (bad O6 fat and possibly bad protein due to heat treatment)
3.  nut butters are inferior to actual nuts from the perspective of cultivating good gut flora (whole nuts contains the skin and other compounds that don't get digested until they hit the large intestine)

Personally, I don't use nuts with seafood, and if I did, I'd just roughly chop the nuts and not blend it into a butter; the last time I did some chopped hazelnuts lightly toasted in butter and poured over raw salmon was divine

### Ken23 — 2014-06-05T19:26:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-get-enough-fat-with-seafood.8205/post-129821>

I figured eating the whole nut was best. Just seeing if changing it up a bit was all that bad.

That does sound delicious

# Thread: How was your DC and RC today?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 130
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T21:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95716>

I think it is time to change gears now...

Not that i think enough is said about foods but we must get a more complete vision on how we can change ourself and others. Focus not only on the sides that are sick or ill but the ways how we do other things.

DC, how was your DisConnection today?

RC, how was your ReConnection today?

In this weird hyperspeed present we need to share how we do live in every aspect, so many clues we can share. We need to take back nature as a guide, find the masters and listen to that inner voice that we got from our parents and their parents.

So please join this trip and share what you think is important to become a difference in modern world today.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T21:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95718>

My RC today...

Went to the sea once again, it is getting colder every day. The air was about 12C and water 9C and sun shining from a clear blue sky. Did some freediving for a couple of hours mixed with some climbing. Not a single soul in my sight, a single sailboat but nothing more. I can really sense that i kind of live in a bubble, everyone is at work and i play... The most important thing is that during all this i can think for free, no distractions... This is pure luxury today.

Biked home, went to local coffeeshop to discuss art and taste with a close friend. Everyone is surfing their iPhones / iPads today, no one is returning your gaze, if they happends to see it they look down. So weird that everything that is human is erased... Read some books and did som painting, the connection between hand and brain is so important... I love the touch of my pen against paper, the smell of ink.

Went to the woods for some running, same thing here... The iPhone is king, nobody leaves it behind. Guess why training today is deadly, your gadgets kills everything. The sensation of wind against bare skin, the sun kissing your back, the wet grass against your feet, all this is packed into an app that beeps and send your data to your Facebook profile... Sometimes i would like to slap them in their face to try to make them understand but hello, can they change? Perhaps when their life is crashing down...

All of this sums up to a perfect day, so many hours of simple nature makes this boy happy.

### Hemming — 2013-09-12T21:44:34+0300

I have to say that I'm somewhat envious of the freedom and opportunities you have - something we should all strive for. My DC today only goes to hugging a tree in a natural reserve

### BJK77 — 2013-09-12T21:57:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95722>

I too am envious of your life prAna303. I dream about having that kind of freedom and time to spend in nature and with loved ones, but for now it's still just a dream. One day . . .

My RC today was simple, but wonderful nonetheless. I ate both breakfast and lunch outside under a huge oak tree and also did some yoga and meditation on the cool, wet grass.

Unfortunately I also had to spend several hours at work in an office environment and tonight I must attend a school event for my daughter. I am hoping to have time to get outside and do a little walking through my backyard woods after that though. That seems to help my sleep.

### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T22:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95723>

> I have to say that I'm somewhat envious of the freedom and opportunities you have - something we should all strive for. My DC today only goes to hugging a tree in a natural reserve

The biggest thing for me is when i found my meaning, my purpose everything synced up to the age of 15. This was the last year i was really true to myself and others and eight years ago i went back. I think we all can do the small steps and end up in big change, i did the other way. Changed everything from one day to another because i could not be true to myself otherwise.

Trees are so important to us, not just hugging but how the act, live and age. They are like living memories of the past. We have a saying amongst the old swedish lapps that when you really want to communicate with someone in distance, connect with a tree and you should feel how the person you "talks" to feels...

### prAna303 — 2013-09-12T22:25:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95724>

Simple is how one should do it. When you doubt, ask yourself how you would act in the wild as a wild human, forget all the add ons we have and be naked... I am a strong beliver that food should be consumed grounded, otherwise we do not get the correct currentflow in our system. So by doing this simple thing you are ahead of many, i think.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-12T22:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95726>

My RC today was pitifully small compared to yours.

Spent an hour at the park this morning before work with the sleeping ducks. The stars were out when I got there and so beautiful. When I left, the sun was not quite yet over the horizon. There was enough light to reflect on the lake and watch the fish jumping.

The high point was watching an eagle fly toward its nest at the far end of the lake. Still amazes me that I can hear the traffic on the I-45 corridor and at the same time see a bald eagle flying.

### Optimalbound — 2013-09-23T19:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95833>

Not even close to your league, prAna303, but here goes:

RC yesterday: Walked barefoot in the rain to an underground well to collect a few gallons of drinking water. Swam in the cold ocean with Inger.

DC today: After doing TriMeter measurements, I disconnected my clock radio, microwave oven and UPS at home. Disconnected the lift control box under my desk at work (made the TriMeter reading drop from 2.7 milligauss at edge of desk (100+ right next to the keyboard!) to <0.3.

Still have lots of work to do, but doing what I can with what I have, right now.

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-24T18:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-95955>

How am I RC today. I'm printing out a document I need to read for my new job/client and I'm going to go outside the house and sit on the grass in my front yard and "Work"

my DC will be working on line from home via a hard wired computer - no wifi.

Interesting thing happened yesterday - my first day back on the job out side the pentagon. I got a migraine and it took a conversation on Dan's Circadian biohacker's page to come to the following realization:

_"So I had what I think was a revelation last night around 3am. my migraine was linked to two things - forgetting to take my afternoon dose of thyroid meds and my first day not being under florescent lights. took my thyroid meds, had some sweet potato w/my ceviche along with CoQ10, mag and lots of water. So Jack changing the type of light you expose your self to during the day - directly impacts the photoperoid (not sure if I'm spelling that right) b/t it directly impacts the Vitamin A production. and conversion of T3 into your hormones. So when you alter Vitamin A production in some people as a result in changing or decreasing light - a migraine is a possible symptom of dropping Vitamin A levels? I'm not sure if this is even close to right on this but it makes sense. Obviously this isn't the whole picture... but I'm beginning to make more connections?"_

I was so used to artificial florescent lights (Even with the blueblockers all day) that not being under them yesterday - when my body expected it that I ended up w/a migraine. How crazy is that.

So my biggest disconnect is actually functioning in natural light! Which in itself is a reconnection...

### prAna303 — 2013-09-28T09:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96361>

Change...

DC: Something has to go and this time it will be computers. Been off the internet for some days now and thru this winter this will be the norm. Had some meetings with people who needed my help with their kids and this is a gamechanger for me. When i meet up with humans that can not walk or move in a natural way my head and heart hurts, this is a problem with many today and it is getting bigger day by day. The painful thing is that i see whatÂ´s wrong but not many will do as i say... For me this means isolation for a while to crack that nut, the way to get inside peoples head and make a change. To think i need the mountains and for most of my days i will be there, checking some stuff on my computer now and then.

RC: Analog, slowly over the past eight years i knew that this is what i long for... To be standing on the other side of the gap of insane technology usage. If one should make a stand you must make that gap bigger. Been on stage, shooting for a film for some times now and that resonates with me, the analog way of life with real humans of flesh and blood.

As said in Kodaks InCamera, about the usage of film for movies instead of digital cameras:

Cinelicious “likes digital” but “loves ﬁlm.” Why is ﬁlm so important to you?

I have great childhood memories involving Super 8 cameras, but mainly I just like looking at it better. I believe that humans respond to celluloid when they see it in motion. It resonates with them emotionally. We say, ‘respect the past and embrace the future.’ It’s important to know where we come from, so we don’t get lost on the path to where we are going. We strive to stay rooted in how digital decisions derive from a photochemical process. No one has ever asked us to make film look more like digital. As a creative community, it’s important to have a choice of tools.

That kind of sums it up for me...

### caroline — 2013-09-28T10:40:26+0300

Peter - you always blow me away with your thoughts...what an extraordinary young man you are.....you have so much depth....you are so wise........

### prAna303 — 2013-09-28T11:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96367>

> Peter - you always blow me away with your thoughts...what an extraordinary young man you are.....you have so much depth....you are so wise........

Thanks... Perhaps knowledge is global not local, when we study something today we try to get the details but forget about the whole picture. When we tap into something bigger we see how things works together as a whole, like watching a performance and focus on just that little part with that precise movement and then everything is lost. Sit back and widen your whole perspective, let go of focus and feel the world and its music.

### caroline — 2013-09-28T11:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96368>

Yo got me with ....."feel the world and its music.".......that brings tears to my eyes.......there is music everywhere you look...everything you see....everything in nature you touch ....every baby you see.....every child just sitting and day dreaming.....every older person who has a smile and a kind word and a pat on the back.....every wave you are lucky enough to see and feel .....and on and on......

### caroline — 2013-10-04T10:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-96856>

My RC for today.........I was getting into the rock pool at the ocean and a young girl said to me "it is cold - but once you are in it will be fine" I told her how cold was really good and she said "I love being cold"

We talked for about 30 minutes. She is this amazing, lovely 10 year old. I asked about school - if she is a good student......she said "yes I try really hard"
I asked what amazing things was she going to do when she got older......she thought for a moment and then told me she loves horses and has her own ...and she has been riding since she was little. She shows her horse and jumps and she said she is really good at it.

I said one day I will see you in the olympics .....she became quite shy and said "I don't think I would be good enough"

I told her that a thought can change her DNA ...nothing is impossible.....She really thought about that ....and then said ..."yes - I can do that"

At that point her Nan called her to get out of the water. Later she came to say goodbye and that she had enjoyed talking to me. What an extra ordinary young girl.... I was so lucky to spend time talking with her.....

Back for a short report...

DC: Irony at the highest level. Some of you know that i had a another life eight years ago, today is my day that i celebrate that i got away from everything. Irony, the same company asked me to come back and work for them again. The feeling of saying NO without any doubt, not of this world. So i continue my quest where work is only a small part of my ME.

RC: To celebrate my day, bought a nice bordeux... Going downtown and be a change, the only one without a smartphone. I will be human and connect.

### caroline — 2013-10-09T05:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97319>

RC/DC: We have live entertainment at lunch time in the summer - outside in a park - in front of our performing arts centre.

As I was waiting for a bus - the young man started playing - The Tennessee Waltz.
I was so overcome by emotion and was choking back tears ..... and I don't know why. It could be a connection to my Dad - but nothing comes to mind. I felt it so strongly .....but still don't have a clue why.... Obviously something really buried..... I am going to go and lay in the sand and stare at the ocean - and see if anything comes back..... I guess I had better take tissue!

That song is probably way before all of your time.......

### Inger — 2013-10-09T07:42:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97324>

Danco, we did almost the same..

Me too was swimming naked in the river twice.. then I was singing, and dancing, and walking the 2 sheep left, Emme and Mimosa, bare feet. I hold them in a line like dogs, but they follow me so sweetly anyways. I walk them to different places so they get to eat different herbs and grass. I sing for them too... I love those walks. They are my earthing-for-the-day walks. Emme and Mimosa are happy too

Those walks in the crispy air of the fall have something magic in them for sure

@ Audrey that song is beautiful. I hope you experienced some deep healing through it

### Awainer1 — 2013-10-11T02:14:22+0300

I'm really feeling the need to disconnect lately. I know its a process but I'm just sick of all the stress of life. I just want to live in nature and get unplugged from everyone/everything. There's too many pressures of modern society to be this and be that. In nature no one is trying to impress or be better its just about surviving. I feel like im making all these little changes to be optimal but only really getting farther away from it.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-11T11:32:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97518>

I feel that i must share another side of what we call life today, a world that do not depend on the latest version of iPhone, updates on Facebook or endless dramas of modern life. I want to prove that even some of the simplest actions in life can be exactly what energizes us, the small nuances that we miss with all external noice.

Since i do something that many consider weird, strange and not productive i face resistance everywhere i turn BUT that also gets me going. If you have been hearing for years that you must do this and do that to do what i do, then you grow thick skin. Just fighting against the crowd that you must wear shoes, eat carbs, wear warm clothes and NOT be in the ocean by yourself... The big security world that is around us, but wait, who is not ill today? They say i am extreme because of what i do BUT i am not the one who are having problems with sleep, getting a flu three times a year, crashing down like a 747. I often just smile and say that it is ok for them to think so, i mean, i would have died about seven times with their way of thinking about this way of living life.

It is sad but it is up to everyone to get a life and live, i do not move a single inch on this... Even if it cost me alot of possibilities of luxury, the big win for me is that i live, and live to tell a story, live to act in nature upon itÂ´s laws.

For many of us this is going to be a solo journey, but that is also part of life... Fix the unit called human and then you will not be alone anymore, when you are working more like a complete version of yourself you simply GET company, even if you will not want it.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-12T11:44:42+0300

DC: Since i do the above in a normal environment i will have to DC afterwards. Going to the sea, some statics and then my own "weirdo" thing... I often go lie under a tree, with my head connected (touching) the stem. I always choose trees that looks strong and beautiful... I prefer firs, they are strong and silent, not noicy like birch. Told you i was weird.

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-12T14:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97606>

Peter you said "For many of us this is going to be a solo journey, but that is also part of life... Fix the unit called human and then you will not be alone anymore, when you are working more like a complete version of yourself you simply GET company, even if you will not want it."

So true....from some perspectives I have become a loner or anti-social, but I have held the line against the kind of socializing that deadens me...Being with this more "complete verson" of myself I always feel connected to the natural world and I choose to be with people that I can feel connected to. The Non-violent Communication author Marshall Rosenburg calls this listening for what is alive in others and speaking from what is alive in yourself.....that's the only kind of talking I'm interested in these days.... My life is enriched by the people and animals that I'm drawn to now...which is why I feel attached to all of you guys....this searching is an enlivening state and all our discoveries and wonderment come through on this thread so clearly...xo to you all

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-12T16:08:35+0300

The Non-violent Communication author Marshall Rosenburg calls this listening for what is alive in others and speaking from what is alive in yourself.....that's the only kind of talking I'm interested in these days.... My life is enriched by the people and animals that I'm drawn to now...which is why I feel attached to all of you guys....this searching is an enlivening state and all our discoveries and wonderment come through on this thread so clearly...xo to you all

Beautifully said... you speak my mind.

It was a beautiful 12 degree C and no wind at all. I tanked sun power for hours in the yard in bikini, laying on the grass on a wool plaid with my bare feet touching the grass. I worked on a project, and watched the 2 sheep eating grass in the yard. Perfect work.  A little hard though.. Emme was way too interested to chew on my paper and pen, kissing me, and she wanted to drink my tea too. I had a nice rooibush vanilla tea... in the end I gave up.. and let her drink it. But man was it beautiful... those moments are the most beautiful to me. I feel so totally connected with everything around me it is pure magic

> you simply GET company, even if you will not want it."

Yes... I have experienced this
I always crave the loneliness.. IDK why. I am so happy then. I like to have the animals around.. and children for sure. And just a very few special people to me. But that is about it. I feel weird. But I cannot bear to have people too close in my life me that I do not feel drawn to. It is different at work, there I socialize with all kind of people and I love that! But I do not let them into my sacred place and home. I am getting so sensitive to energies.. peoples energies. I am a bit overwhelmed

Peter I too use internet only for reserach. Yes... this forum is educating, and very much so

### prAna303 — 2013-10-13T10:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97692>

DC: Took a morning ride, biking... three hours of wonderful sunrise. Biking is nice for your mind but not your body but i know how i reconnect. It is +1C this morning and it is smelling of snow now, winter coming. They promise snow on friday so i really hope they are right.

RC: Going out into the wild tonight, the sky will be clear i hope, stars everywhere. This is the time when i start to move outside again, more time in the cold and windy mountains. In the summer i do not feel this force pulling me out to sleep in the wild, to warm i guess. Going to search for a small cave i think will be in a spot that i climb, it is a small one and if i find it i will make it mine, it is a powerful place.

Think i posted this video before but it wakes the feeling of being active AND alive in the cold of winter...

A very alive feeling that I had forgotten all about. I was so thrilled for all the beautiful sunshine and warm breezes and much less clothes when we moved to Australia...but winter here is a non event I guess ...and oddly - I am always cold here in winter....

### prAna303 — 2013-10-13T11:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97706>

... If you look around today, everyone is saying that cold should be avoided at all times. Today they learn parents in Sweden that babys should always wear hats up until they are six years old, in other words 24/7/365.

We send away sick people to warm countries in the winters, everyone is going on trips around new year here, it is to cold and they need the warm weather, they say.

From day one we learn that cold is deadly, is it strange that many must let this change grow slowly? It is a big change for most of us.

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-13T15:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97710>

I also left Canada after thirty years.... if I knew then what I know now I could have lived better in Newfoundland....the lack of sun is a challenge that can only be met by getting super cold, grounded, hydrated and unplugged....which I didn't do. I lost too much energy and disrupted too many rhythms to recover without the sun, but I'm happy to be in a four season world in Vermont (Newfoundland had two seasons: cold rain/drizzle/fog and warm rain/drizzle/fog).....you are always welcome Audrey to have a "summer break" here and play in the snow.

The movie trailer almost gave me a case of virtual CT! Beautiful images.

### caroline — 2013-10-14T00:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97743>

The little boy in the video ....that is how I imagine Peter in my mind's eye ...always curious, always questioning and super cute
Thanks Seahorse .......a trip to Vermont some day would be awesome!

When I was in Kyrgyzstan awhile ago ....my very favorite day was when we were in the mountains after they had had their first big snowfall ...we were all like children - making snow angels, building a snowman, throwing snowballs .....we all has so much fun. There was so much spontaneity and laughing and just sheer joy.It was quite thrilling.....and at the end of a 30 day trip ..that one day was everyone's favorite ...far and away, and we had seen and done some amazing things. We, also weren't dressed for that day - only sneakers and lite jackets - not gloves or scarves or anything.

Our gorgeous young guide stripped down to his shorts and was paddling around in a stream - totally instinctive ....C/T....

### Lahelada — 2013-10-14T04:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-97756>

I took an unexpectedly long walk today. The sun was out,the city was quiet as this is a long holiday weekend .The paraiso trees are in full flower and they line almost every street in Buenos Aires. Their perfume permeates the city at this time of year and I felt so uplifted by the heady scent.The park was busy with people lounging on the grass , playing with their dogs or kids or both,old ladies were dancing outside the community centre in a spontaneously arranged dance. Kids were fascinated with the fire juggling clowns. No this was not an organised event, just Buenos Aires entertaining Buenos Aires. Right now there is a tango trio practising and the wind carries the music to our open window.
One of the reasons why I like living here , people with all their faults are still real. During my walk I spoke to three complete strangers.We don't know each other but we have community.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-17T16:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98144>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=98119)
>
> Just one word: S N O W

JEALOUS!

today RC was awesome so far. I walked Chaos to school barefoot, as usual. On the way back to the house to begin work- I was totally focused on the moment of what I was doing. Walking, and observing nature. as I turned the corner - a squirrel started running straight for me. I continued to walk towards him and he continued on until we met. i stopped, he stopped then he came up parallel with me looked up at me as I looked down, and he sniffed my ankle - I could feel his wiskers - and off he went about his business. I continued walking, turned to look back and he had stopped and just sat there looking at me... he had no fear of me. he wasn't worried about my actions at all, it was as if he sensed I was nature

### prAna303 — 2013-10-17T22:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98175>

Amazing weather today... Got up at 04:15 this morning and snow was falling... Up in the mountain and doing my first run in falling snow. About 0C so the snow chilled me quite nice. Later on some nice biking in snow blended rain and this sums it up to a wonderful day... Like i said before, i LOVE winters in Sweden.

### Inger — 2013-10-18T10:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98251>

I love the winters here too Peter. So crispy and fresh air.. and the shock I get every time I dive/dip in the river, I am addicted to it huh! I never get that in the summer, water is way too warm. Now it "climbs" closer to 0 degree C every day....

I so much more like to dip shortly many times / day instead of a long dip, because the nicest moment are the first ones  and I do not need to shiver after..lol

You got up so early? Wow! I sleep until 7 AM every day now, that is when it starts to get a tiny bit lighter outside.... but maybe the snow does it. Snow makes everything so bright  It might snow here today, no snow so far! Just a little rain mixed with some snowflakes.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-18T13:19:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98255>

Mostly because i had to check if the snow had started to fall and bingo, just a couple of centimeters...

RC: Early trip again, ended up sleeping by a tree on the frosty and snowy ground... The funny thing is that i scared the shit out of some people, they thought i was dead, lying under a tree in shorts only at -3C. Perhaps i will have that spot by my self from now on. Did some salesjob yesterday, selling crosscountry skiing equipment, crazy amount of customers, about 1400 in six hours. Biked home afterwards in -3C and snowblended rain, good reset after being inside with "normal" people with iPhones and loads of fake light.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-24T19:06:26+0300

RC: Been doing some testing on myself this week... Since i always felt like the natural way is the right way for me i have been eating outside every day and doing it sitting on the ground with my bare feet. It just felt like the way to go when i updated my own OS this week, my friends bought a new iPhone AND iPad Air. To early to tell if something is different but it feels better, in my mind any way. Been searching for a cave nearby but no good result yet, hope to find it before the real winter starts, planning to sleep many good nights there this winter.

DC: Bought a new fountain pen, just to minimize computer time.

### caroline — 2013-10-25T01:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-98932>

my R/C
I was in my apple store yesterday because my laptop is sick. We got talking about the new ipad soon to be released here and I, of course, mentions the high EMF output from them. I asked the young man where he keeps his iphone and of course you know what he said! I told him about the latest studies and Charlie Teo's concerns for many years now. He actually was listening very intently and said he would stop putting it there or next to his head. He said he spends a couple of hours a nite talking to his girlfriend and then puts his phone under his pillow. He was pretty worried. I told him to put it on airplane mode and fling it across the room and use speaker.

I asked him if he wanted to have kids .......he really was getting it! He said to me ....so basically I am frying my balls???
the answer is Yep!
So then I had the same discussion with the other young man - he, at least, doesn't keep his iphone in his pants pockets.
they really do get it on an intuitive level when you ask them if they want to have kids.......

### prAna303 — 2013-12-07T10:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102694>

At last the real winter is here...

Had some of the best starters for years now, been away from the digital world a lot during past month.

Slowly turning my world more analog by restoring and repairing my old turntable. It is alive now and kicking and music is art again. This is something that really gets me going now, listening to vinyl because many do not really know how good it sounds and feels. Had some friends over and we did some testing, cd vs vinyl vs digital (computer) and everyone was shocked how analog can build a room, a presence, like a timetravel.

Another funny thing is that i also restored my first real computer, a commodore 64 with an old tv as a monitor. And yes, my own gut feeling was right. When i use a modern computer with tft monitor i feel drained. Not at all the same feeling with this old thing. So many small details that makes difference, no mouse or trackpad, not as much artificial lights from that old tv and also the feeling of a real tool. New computers are filled with fun and joy and that oldie must be programmed and used as a machine, it does not junk code inside...

As i said, snow is here. Only 30cm but everything is white... My mind and senses are cleaned in just a week. Spent my thursday on a mountain top, looking at the view for about 4 hours, complete white out. Nothing else than a horizon with snow covered mountains. Just updated my sleeping skin, a completely new reindeer skin, redy for winter sleep in the woods.

Just a picture from last year since i do not use my camera anymore, it is exactly the same feeling now.

### Inger — 2013-12-07T11:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102696>

Wow.. how beautiful! We have just a mm snow here.. that is raining/melting away today. Hope for snow like you have too! So cool with the analog way and the vinyl plates. I want such a thing too now. I seldom listen to music anymore because it feels just so different than live. I found a old CD that had some very good songs so I listened to them a few times with ear phones dancing... super dance music, it was so fun... but later I measured the EMF coming out from the ear phones and it was.... crazy!!! I did not imagine it could be so bad... but my head does felt a little weird after listening with them.

I am way too little outside right now. But when I am, I always am amazed what a difference to being inside. It is crystal clear to me by now what a difference it have to make in our health... especially the colder months with that crazy fresh and crispy air and special feeling... very peaceful like everything is hibernating

### prAna303 — 2013-12-07T11:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102698>

> but later I measured the EMF coming out from the ear phones and it was.... crazy!!! I did not imagine it could be so bad... but my head does felt a little weird after listening with them.

I think it is also about how the music is presented. To just listen with the ears is not natural, all the details from the room, all the stuff that resonates with tunes is gone so we get a simulation of music. This is also how the modern world listen today, music, talk no difference.

Sound is waves and we feed on them...

### Inger — 2013-12-07T11:18:38+0200
