### SeaHorse — 2013-12-07T14:46:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102711>

Beautiful picture Peter...we're having a stop and start beginning to winter, it was so cold last week and then got up to rain/drizzle and fog the past few days. It's colder again this morning so the ice on the pond is flat and smooth....I'm hoping we can actually go skating...but now I'm thinking I just want to slide around in slippers....forget the metal blades on the skates...we'll see where that experiment leads me...

Last week we saw a live concert of the Irish traditional. group Lunasa....they have the most wonderful acoustic sound particularly because they have a string bass in the mix...flute, irish pipes, guitar, fiddle and bass....it's the beautiful balance of sounds and we were in the first few rows so I was soaking up the bass and soaring with the flute...fantastic!

I still have all my old records, but no player at the moment...good idea for a Christmas present for me from my kids....they will get magneticos from me and I'll ask for a stereo from them.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-07T18:59:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-102732>

It was 84 degrees at 230 PM yesterday here. In 1.5 hours arctic front came in and it was 33 degrees at 530. I went in the pool last night at 55 degrees. This AM the pool is 47. It is 36 degrees now and raining cold with 22 mph winds. CT baby.

### prAna303 — 2013-12-10T18:15:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-103068>

Winter, time where things happen.

As if i where thrown out of a plane, freefall thru sky, magic days are here.

On sunday, got the chance to see a dance performance once again. When i resonate like this i fall in love, this time also in one of the dancers... Okey, it is easy to look at someone and feel that they look good but sometimes you get something more, a level added. She was performing at a level beyond everyone else, fearless, weightless, silent and with powers of few. When i see something like this i get a rush that will last for months, for sure.

On monday, minus 16C, whiteout and many hours in the mountains... Mostly in shorts only, feeling more alive than ever.

Today, found a bordeaux wine that taste like heaven... Now of to the cafÃ©, moleskine and fountain pen, good old analog way.

Ciao

### prAna303 — 2014-01-03T17:34:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-104661>

> How do you survive brutal cold? If you gain the ability to generate an DC electrical field that matches the weather’s electric field, this electric field acts to repel the cold. CT magic.

Read this on the blog and must say it works... Been doing most of my running in the winter in shorts / thin shoes for years now, many cross country skiers think i am crazy because they have a ton of clothes on. Slowly i have pushed myself with comfort to -10C without any problems, it feels simply wonderful.

When i do sleep on the gound in the winters i feel like there is a sphere around me that makes me warm, i can lie almost naked on the ground under a tree for hours, down to -8C. In a strange way i feel more balanced around -5C and colder that around 0C, something is happening in the minus world, BIG time.

Cite: Nonchalant

> Holy mackeral! Do any of us do that, or would that be a more advanced trait of cold adaption?

I think most can do it right now, most of our fears about cold is teached for many generations now. I do it many days and nights and always feel good but then i have always loved cold and never thought of it as BAD.

### prAna303 — 2014-01-06T12:40:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-104865>

> Peter, do you feel a big difference between running during sunny days and overcast days?

I used to, for many years i would get a high from the sun but now i feel i get the same in every day...

The thing is that the crave for natural energy has changed, i need it every day now. With every challenge nature gives me i love it, sun in the summer, rain in the fall and snow and ice in the winters...

Last months, winter in the calendar but no snow is making me feel a little off, i long for some cold now but it do not show up... Solved it with more exposure in time out there.

So perhaps I can look forward to accessing water & cold & the earth for energy. I do seem to notice a small amount of energy in rainwater. And also from CT & grounding barefoot. Maybe that is the beginning. Thanks for giving us your experience.
I crave the sunshine now, and I expect and receive so much energy from it! Even DH has noticed and is starting to access a bit of it as well. At least he no longer thinks it's all in my head.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-01T11:36:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-107700>

DC: Off computer for some time now... This thing is quite weird i feel, the more others use their iGadgets i turn the other way. Only checking this place nowdays...

RC: Oh, so many things. Sleeping outside is great again, it is cold at last and i feel great. We had an epic storm before christmas and now the whole world around me is changed, the complete nature is changed, i got my forest back. Where i used to meet others, nobody dares to go, i feel like i won a million bucks. Gone another level with my music, started to do it myself, piano and guitar. Since i do not take classes i just do as i feel is right. Been asked to join a group of people to just sit and discuss the world, like the old school thinkers. Now many turn my way and ask tons of things, almost everyone here is sick or ill now, is it the suns activity that destroys them? I get asked what i do because the gap is widening... Funny, things that they thought was insane starts to attract them now.

I have a clear horizon and intend to keep it that way. Not many things can get into my life now, they have to match every connection i have with nature otherwise they go down the bin. My taste have changed to much the last four years, i only need and want the analog version, the fine details. It cost a lot, not just in money but also in how i realte to others, it is a price i am willing to take.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T02:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-107839>

Up until recently ... I feel like I don't relate to my old friends - nothing has changed there .... but holey cow - lately I seem to attract people where ever I go and every single time they want to know stuff. I am wondering what I am giving off? Obviously energy/electrons ...but why is it so much more noticeable?

There is so many people on a daily basis - I decided to stay in yesterday and recharge my battery! I was so content just staying home and sitting on my balcony and watching the ocean and the trees and birds etc...

huge changes are happening.... I have a front row seat to .... me

There is even this guy calling me that I met on a trip 2 years ago ...WTH...It is freaking me out because he has low energy and I don't want to hurt his feelings....I totally not used to this type of problem.

### prAna303 — 2014-02-05T21:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-108530>

When bad is good.

Got a chance to put all this at test. Was on one of my daily trips in the mountains, took a fall, hit a tree after a couple of meters and beat up my whole right side quite bad. Nothing broken, a little blood and bruises, but not really able to move in a good manor.

Took a while to collect my thoughts and make a plan, the basics for making the memory in my body work. So i took it to what others around me thinks is extreme, used nature in a manic way, almost like a addict and it worked...

\- Loads of natural water from a little river nearby
\- Loads of fat and raw salmon
\- Grounding at minus 5 C for a couple of hours a day
\- Slept outside (biggest effect in this one)
\- No digital, no technology just analog vinyls
\- Consumed music with my mouth wide open, vibrations
\- Played piano

After three days i got a intanse pain in my whole body, think like when you started to get your first teeth. I felt, this is good, i am growing again and after a day i woke up... No pain, my head was clear and almost feeling high, my bruises age gone and all that is left is the cuts.

With this i feel like new, so a really bad thing can maybe be needed to start up the currents in our semiconductors. With that overload it felt like restoring a battery, needed to clear up some sleeping components.

### caroline — 2014-02-06T00:57:08+0200

The universe just said "Hello" .......don't forget me - I am what makes you tick .....I am mother nature - don't forget me or you will suffer the consequences..... maybe not right now ....but you will

Just let go and feel, that was my theme last day...

If i look at the calendar, winter is still here and by that, parkas and mittens. Instead i follow what nature tells me, and if the streams start to brake up, snow melts like crazy, birds sings and for me the most true sign of spring, seagulls sings... This means i follow that trail instead of what others do.

With all of these signs, my sleeping spot had to change, from woods to the beach i felt. So, last night, instead of my pine i went to the beach to find all ice is gone, stars lights up my night and boom... Sleeping like a baby and waking up to the first sunshine in well over two months. Laid there just soaking up, waking up, like a bear from a den.

So even if it is one months early, for me and nature, it is spring.

### prAna303 — 2014-04-14T23:47:46+0300

Is this why?

When doing my freediving i get a feeling of deep pleasure, not just a goofball happines but something else.

If i simply let the element take me in, let me be part of it i adapt. I have no training of regular diving because i felt that it would stop me from acting in a proper manor. When i put myself in a position where i can not use my lungs for breathing, do i let the surface of my skin breath? If i let that element get to me and not use a wetsuit like many others do, can i get a balanced way to breath under water? Air is important to life for sure but when i am down there is it a thermodynamic problem i try to solve. When one part is missing and together with the cold of water, the air in water can i stay there longer than my friends? Yes i think but also the cold makes me work.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-15T02:03:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-121535>

An alternative "point of view" can be a dangerous luxury when substituted for insight and understanding. People only see what they want to see......or feel what they want to feel. When nature touches you deftly you become new.

### prAna303 — 2014-04-16T14:14:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-121814>

Had a date with a friend last day... She is like a perfect storm these days, slowly going into a eating disorder, trouble with her husband and slowly breaking down.

Thought a lot on the mountains today and perhaps she could solve the issues like this...

No doubt she is loosing energy... And with the error in eating, she is going down fast... But what to do if the can not stand to eat?

When we are in a state of that kind of panic, our systems has to apply and try to keep the unit alive. This means, sublime things goes first, like senses. She said she thought food smells like hell, to top notes is gone for her. We try to only get thru the days with our biggest limbs not the finest things that need tons of energy to work. This must be a problem to solve.

If we loose the ability to control food intake, the unit would adapt in the wild to use other sources of energy. And yes, she used to be a surfer so i told her she is missing that huge source of life, today she only surfs the iPhone. It is all about how we can use our whole unit to sense the elements. When i told her that this is perhaps everything i do just to survive today she kind of clicked.

She is on the road to hell but if you pay attention to small doors, you can escape, and she has. I got an e-mail that she is leaving her man and taking up her surfing again, she sat the whole night by the sea and like she said, it all became very clear. If she can not eat the energy, she must be in it for every second possible.

Is this natures way to solve that complex issue of eating? When times get rough we have a memory of that and starts a mode that eats the elements instead of man made foods, yes i do think that.

### Hemming — 2014-04-16T14:18:20+0300

### prAna303 — 2014-04-16T14:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-121818>

Exactly my idea... And perhaps the solution to my own health, every time i get some dips i just go away into the wild. When the entropy is modern world is to high i must reconnect with nature, to get the whole picture clean and sparkling.

I know that some of my friends would have ADHD if they did not climb, perhaps me also.

When you expose yourself to the things that created us they welcome us and we remember. Nothing has to be pushed because we are a creation based on these rules.

### prAna303 — 2014-04-24T00:36:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-dc-and-rc-today.8210/post-123241>

Did some new stuff today... A discussion between unknown humans with only one connection, in a soft place with wine.

Not a single subject that was based on rules but the focus became my point of view... In the end, every single thing can be talked about with quantum physics in mind and as a guide. We talked life, love, work, illness and other stuff. I was the odd one once again, not having a regular work, regular family, regular way of thinking but it was nice...

The shift is going on and i feel hope for the future, even if i feel that i must live most of my time off-grid there is an attraction to life and other people... Now i understand that i survive because of these things and makes me see the world in a much more clear vision...

So, try it for yourself, gather up in groups of five to seven, share a bottle of wine and talk... Just like we used to before internet and likes on cyberspace.

And in memory of someone i lost but maybe will find again...

# Thread: In the mood to hack? Tissue Mineral Analysis

### cantweight — 2013-09-12T23:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-mood-to-hack-tissue-mineral-analysis.8214/post-95730>

Thanks for taking the time to look at this....lots of lows, no highs, very little toxic metal which is awesome because that was not always the case for me

Really curious about the significant mineral ratios....dont quite understand them and my calcium to potassium and calcium to magnesium are way off.

\*When I preview this post all the info runs together and i cant get it spaced. My number is first and the ideal number is second. In the ratios the third number is % of ideal\*

MINE IDEAL

Calcium 22 40 Below range

Magnesium 3 6 Below range

Sodium 1 25 Way below

Potassium 1 10 Below range

Iron .6 3.5 Below range

Copper 2.7 2.5 In range

Manganese .008 .04 Below range

Zinc 17 20 In range

Chromium .051 .12 Below range

Selenium .045 .18 Below range

Phosphorus 13 16 In range

Zinc, copper, and Phosphorus look great. Sodium is crazy low. I have craved salt since my 20'sâ€¦cant get enough. Fainted in public once and was taken to hospital, only thing they found was low sodium.

Toxic metalsâ€¦barely registered on chart. Woo hoo!

Lead .017
Mercury .038
Cadmium .001
Arsenic .013
Aluminum .30

Nickel, Cobalt, Molybdenum, Lithium, Boronâ€¦.all below range

Significant Mineral Ratios

MINE IDEAL % of IDEAL
CA/MG 7.33 6.67 110

CA/K 22 4 550

NA/MG .33 4.17 8

NA/K 1 2.5 40

ZN/CU 6.3 8 79

CA/P 1.69 2.5 68

Thanks for any input

### cantweight — 2013-10-06T16:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-mood-to-hack-tissue-mineral-analysis.8214/post-97034>

More labs.....

T4 5.5 (4.5-12ug/dl)
T3 uptake 32 (24-39%)
Free Thyroxine 1.8 (1.2-4.9)
TSH .329 (.450-4.5 uIU.ml)

So TSH is low...thinking I will back down my dose, taking 150mg armour. Maybe CT and iodine are kicking in?

Tested positive for single mutation C282Y hemochromatosis. No surprise. My dad has hemochromatosis and my daughter tested positive as carrier, my sons negative.

BUN/Creatinine ratio went from 11 in spring to 15 in fall!!! ugh!!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T16:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-mood-to-hack-tissue-mineral-analysis.8214/post-97036>

Toxic metals go down in tissues as energy levels go up........just sayin. What happens on a dying star is very instructive here. A dying star loses energy and begins to create transition metals in its core. Here is another example how a change in energy changes the structure of matter. The same process that happens in a star happens in you because the laws of physics apply across the board.........biology is just waking up to this foundational fact.

### Hope — 2013-10-25T22:38:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-the-mood-to-hack-tissue-mineral-analysis.8214/post-99041>

So - you had an amazing reservoir of disparate information ....and then, one day, the penny dropped.....and here we are.

I can see with my two daughters - The oldest is very street smart ...but she always had difficulty learning because of dyslexia which no one ever picked up on \[me included\] until she was about 17 ....so she learned how to cope in a very real way. My youngest daughter is very book smart - but when it comes to life - I sometimes think she got left at the station.

So - we all have our own unique abilities.........I think I have gone off the rails here....

The whole description of electron resonances, brain vortices, properties of water, pi-electron cloud densities, fractal patterning,...the list goes on--is just stunning in its breadth of science. And yet, ultimately, the integration of these various parts leads to better understanding of how we all work. Knowing that can only lead to a realization that living congruent to the laws of physics is the only real path to health. I feel like we are all like the frog in a slowly heated pot. Ultimately, we will boil to death without taking any action. That is a fractal patterning of CT! ) Think about it. We get hotter (keep warm) because it makes us feel good, yet we should be getting colder so that we can live better.

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-24T21:33:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-95965>

So, Q'Rad Randy

how are you applying some of what you've learned to your current situation given your local???? Inquiring minds want to know. I know you've been CTing for a while but interested in what other things you've pursued.

### QuantumRadical — 2013-09-24T23:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-95984>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=95965)
>
> So, Q'Rad Randy
>
> how are you applying some of what you've learned to your current situation given your local???? Inquiring minds want to know. I know you've been CTing for a while but interested in what other things you've pursued.

Shijin13. Good questions. To tell you the truth, I have been doing some experimentation. I skipped CTing for about 6 weeks to see if the benefits were long lasting. Well, no they are not. I went right back into it, and after just one session, many of the benefits returned. Interesting. I felt I had a lot more energy, less aches and pains, and overall better mood. I also eat a lot of seafood--raw oysters, sashimi, salmon, etc. I also sleep on a ChiliTechnology cooling pad. I set it to the lowest setting, 46 degrees. It really helps to get to a deep sleep. I just purchased the ChiliTechnology cooling mattress and hope DW will use it to sleep better. She doesn't sleep well at all and refuses to even read The Book that I got here, Jack's Book. The mattress should arrive today via UPS!! Jack's last couple of blogs also put me over the edge and convinced me to purchase the Magnetico pads to go under the cooling mattress. I hope that is a winning combination. I am really excited about the magnetico pad. It is so congruent with the quantum biology that Jack is describing in his blogs. I hope the effects are more than subtle.

To celebrate the beginning of Fall, I have also started doing two ice baths a day. My ice baths (my CT sessions) usually are around 50-55 degrees and last about 30 minutes. Every once in a while I get the water down into the high 30's--brutal but effective (I can only last about 15 minutes, but I am starting to tolerate the colder water better). I hope to do two 1 hour CT sessions by New Year's. I am also going total ketosis with a heavy emphasis on the right fats. Three and a half years ago I weighed exactly 200lbs. This morning I was 179lbs and have been at this weight for about a year now without even trying. I am testing the winter theory of weight loss and think my ideal, ketosis, CT'd, epic-paleo weight should be around 165, maybe 160. We shall see. I am very fair-skinned and avoided the sun like the plague, but over the last couple of years, I get out in the sun as much as possible. I can now sunbathe for about 45 minutes without burning (that's 45 minutes each side ). This has certainly helped my Vit D levels not to mention my energy level.

I also supplement Vit D, Vit K2, iodine, magnesium, etc., etc. My cold baths are also enriched with epsom salt, magnesium chloride flakes, hydrogen peroxide, and baking soda.

I frequently wear orange-tinted glasses at night to mitigate the blue light issues. It definitely helps me to sleep better.

I also stay pretty active, and lift heavy things frequently.

One of the interesting things I have noticed is that I wake up in the morning without an alarm clock almost to the minute each morning. It is like that rush of cortisol is on a timer. I am asleep, then within seconds I am wide awake from the cortisol rush. It is like a switch, not gradual at all. No wakeful grogginess anymore. I wonder if this is what optimal feels like or is it a cortisol issue? My bet is that it is a measure of optimal. I do sleep a lot better than I used to.

The science of all of this is absolutely fascinating. But the recipe for health is pretty simple--get cold, eat a lot of seafood, do things to mitigate the emf problem, get some sun, sleep well, and don't sweat the small stuff. And get as much oxytocin as possible!

Let me put it bluntly. When I follow Jack's protocols, I feel better. When I drop off the wagon for even a couple of weeks, I can feel the difference in every dimension. I just wish more of my family would follow my lead, but you just can't make the horses drink. Jack's stuff works!!

Q'Rad Randy

### cantweight — 2013-09-25T17:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-5.8226/post-96079>

I feel like a huge weight has been lifted....this blog was the one that finally slapped me upside the head and shook me saying wake up dumb ass and pay attention this is your answer.

How much time I have wasted worrying about food and supplements, damning grains etc. Never has this been an issue of being fat, of battling various illnesses, its simply been about what I have denied my brain for years and the impact that has had on the body as a whole. From 2006 through 2008 I ran 104-105 degree fevers almost everyday between the hours of 4 and 6 PM with a corresponding cortisol spike. That is some crazy shit for a brain to endure...I am grateful to have recovered from that at all!

I have regained the ability to speak, write, read...etc. So i know it is healing. I am so excited to apply what i have learned from this blog. So excited!!!

Going to continue with lots of fat, some protein, tons of well water and add my new protocol of jumping on the trampoline in the cold temps with the sun shining on as much skin as I can expose without startling the neighboring cows, horses, and goats

I will continue to ground at least and hour a day and lay on my sons magnetico while I do my school work.

Man I have such a sense of peace from this...I see my whole family becoming oxygenated, hydrated, and energized with much more simplicity than i had been able to see before.

Thanks Jack!

Audrey I am honestly blown away by what is happening with my little guy. In spite of all i have learned I was still skeptical of what the magnetico would do. I expected to see nothing and hope something on some level was happening. It has not even been 2 weeks and he is doing really well. Exploded in a strange rash after night 1...rash is worse in morning after being on pad, his attitude and behavior freaked night 2/3. Since then he has been calmer than ever, speech is excellent and I saw zero involuntary movement today or OCD crap. Zero. I went whole hog and got their mattress ([http://www.chilitechnology.com](http://www.chilitechnology.com)), California King, plus the Magnetico's Super Sleep System (Cal King). So I have a 14 inch mattress on top of 2 layers of magnets that weigh 360 lbs. Great workout carrying all of this in boxes up a flight of stairs. Tonight is the first night I will be sleeping on the combination. I had a Chilitechnology cooling pad before and loved it but DW sleeps poorly so upgraded the whole bed. Kind of an experiment in Quantum Sleeping!! The cooling pad can be set as low as 46 degrees which results in about a 55 degree bed conducive to very deep sleep.

Yes, I have been pretty healthy all of my life, but there is a reason why I follow Dr. Jack's protocols. I was just not feeling right about 3 years ago and modern medicine's only recommendation was to take Statins because my cholesterol was a little high. I had also put on a few pounds and couldn't lose the weight even with going very deep Paleo. I wanted more discussion about all of this with my doctor and instead just got a prescription. So I fired the Doctor on the spot in front of his staff and have never looked back. My current Doctor is the same one that Gretchen visits and she is great. I discovered Jack's blogs in August of 2011 just a couple of months after he started posting and now read just about everything religiously, plus I try to read his cites so I can evaluate the actual scientific research. Nothing like an N=1 to validate what you read. But I started embracing the cold and eating a lot more seafood, and feel lightyears better. Still think I can go deeper into this and I suspect that I will see even greater improvements--thus the magnetico and the chilitechnology mattress. I tell people that I feel better now at 60 than I did at 35. I have every intention and desire to be randy when I am 110 years old.

Yes, I listened to Jack's sex webinar--twice. I think he was right on in every respect. It is too bad that our schools don't teach sex education like that. They should.

One can't have too many friends!! )

People tend to buy only the beliefs they have had seared in their head over life, not the benefits of seeing life adapt before their eyes. Our ability to adapt and change will set us apart from the majority of people who are unwilling to change or slow to change. Some times the only difference between winning and losing is one inch of an attitude. Your attitude will determine your altitude in life. You are absolutely unique. Completely designed to stand out from the crowds, the norm and even the self doubt. When you carry around self doubt it is akin to riding your bike with brake engaged. Be exactly who you are. Be peculiar. Be authentic. Be special. Be unique.

Very interesting idea ..... but as I question everything, I have several concerns.

a) Seems like the electrical component of the bed and/or pad would be a significant source of EMF. Dr. Kruse has previously stated his dislike for water beds. Does Chilitechnology have some of the same risks of a heated water bed?

b) I have personally discovered the benefits of sleeping w/ my bedroom circuit breaker switched off. Since this device is an electrical device, it would require the bedroom circuit breaker turned on ....... or a really long extension cord to another room.

c) Have you tested your bed with a body voltage (BV) meter? Would be very interesting to see if the Chilitechnology adds to the BV reading. In my case, I found the BV reading on my bed dropped by 80% w/ the breaker off.

# Thread: What may electrify DNA/RNA?  A clue to the future of the blog.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-23T05:59:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/post-95811>

In a cell or in an organ, under appropriate conditions a phenomenon quite similar to a Bose condensation may occur in substances which possess longitudinal electric modes. If energy, from any source is fed into these electric modes and thence transferred to other degrees of freedom of the substance (semi conductor), then a stationary state will be reached in which the energy content of the electric modes is larger than in the thermal equilibrium (zero entropy system) . This excess energy is found to be channelled into a single modeâ€”exactly as in Bose condensationâ€”provided the energy supply exceeds a critical value (this critical value is C2 in C2M= E for biology). Under these specific circumstances a random supply of energy is thus not completely thermalized, but partly used in maintaining a coherent electric wave in the substance so that it may carry huge amount of information in energy formats.

### QuantumRadical — 2013-09-25T06:03:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-may-electrify-dna-rna-a-clue-to-the-future-of-the-blog.8227/post-96042>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95811)
>
> In a cell or in an organ, under appropriate conditions a phenomenon quite similar to a Bose condensation may occur in substances which possess longitudinal electric modes. If energy, from any source is fed into these electric modes and thence transferred to other degrees of freedom of the substance (semi conductor), then a stationary state will be reached in which the energy content of the electric modes is larger than in the thermal equilibrium (zero entropy system) . This excess energy is found to be channelled into a single mode—exactly as in Bose condensation—provided the energy supply exceeds a critical value (this critical value is C2 in C2M= E for biology). Under these specific circumstances a random supply of energy is thus not completely thermalized, but partly used in maintaining a coherent electric wave in the substance so that it may carry huge amount of information in energy formats.

Jack, I think I totally understood what you just said.  I think!? The energy doesn't get dissipated as heat but is instead captured in the electromagnetic field which can then be used to pass along information in that particular electric mode. it is all about energy capture and productive use of that energy rather than chaotic heat dissipation.

Q'Rad Randy

# Thread: Options for People Who Can't Be in the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### calishaye — 2013-09-23T22:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95837>

Hi Everyone,

This is my first time posting. I have been busy reading Dr Kruse's blogs and have really been enjoying every morsel of information. It is apparent that sun exposure is critical for health. I am wondering what some options are for people who cannot be in the sun. My daughter has a mild form of EPP, which does not allow her to be in the sun when the UV rays are 3 or higher. As far as I know, your body cannot make Vit D when the rays are that low. There are some people who can't even stand indoor lighting without painful reactions. At the moment, there really isn't anything conventional doctors are able to do for them, so other than D3 supplementation, are there other options available for these people, and is there a toxic dose of D3 supplementation?

Here is a website for the EPP Foundation if anyone is interested in learning more about it. It's a tough disease because even a little sun exposure can cause extremely painful reactions, and imagine having to go through life without being able to be in the sun.

I'm curious about the cold thermogenesis as well. Is that something that could be used to regenerate a faulty liver perhaps? That is the cause of the EPP, a breakdown in liver function where the porphyria enzyme is not being flushed out of the liver so it builds up. I do not know why the sun exposure is related to the liver, I would love to hear Dr. Kruse's ideas about that since he seems to be a master at the sun and how it affects our bodies.

Any thoughts would be so appreciated.

Thank you.
Jill

[http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP](http://www.porphyriafoundation.com/about-porphyria/types-of-porphyria/EPP)

### caroline — 2013-09-24T04:12:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95890>

Welcome Jill. I have to say I know nothing about your daughter's problem ..that would be so tough.

Have you read the C/T blogs? I would say that is definitely the way to go. C/T lowers inflammation and that is what you want to do. Have you seen the C/T easy start guide? There are a ton of people here having huge success because of C/T.
It is our primordial state of existence.

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? they are an easy way to get a lot of information quickly. Do you have his book? The leptin RX - It gives you a great overview of all of this and of course the webinars and follow up Q&A and you can ask questions in "ask Jack"

Please start a journal in the "journal section" and we can get to know you and help out when we can. You can also help us/ others by telling us about your daughter.

Have a look at some of the other journals - people here are dealing with all kinds of stuff and getting great results.
Please have a look at a journal by "Mr. Pinkies" and "canweight"

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-24T06:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/options-for-people-who-cant-be-in-the-sun.8230/post-95900>

CT and deep ketosis is huge for her..........CT especially......she can use the visible spectrum but she can use red light phototherapy. This is something you will have to look into.......and you need to realize the way to deliver it with someone with EPP is also unusual......thru the nose into the base of the brain into the olfactory bulb.
Protoporphyrin IX is a biochemically widely used carrier molecule for **divalent cations** that are very commonly found in the transition metal portion of the periodic table. This is where their reaction to EMF becomes interesting. People forget visible light is part of the EMF spectrum. I bet after the Sept 2103 webianr you become a lot more interested in what I just said here above. Together with iron (Fe 2+) the body of the heme- group of hemoglobin, myoglobin and many other heme-containing enzymes like cytochrome c in mitochondria and catalase are formed. Complexed with magnesium-ions (Mg 2+) the main part of the Chlorophylls are formed. This chemicals are used to make energy from sunlight. Complexed with zinc-ions (Zn 2+) it forms Zinc protoporphyrin. The key is the hydroxyl free radical............and the loss of polarized macromolecules in cell membranes can not polarize nearby water molecule in the plasma in these kids.....when you add light to the mix you generate a specific type of reaction that drives a massive inflammatory reaction by generating the hydroxyl free radical.....this is where the burning and itching come from......if left long enough T2D, cancer, or liver failure come fast because of the obstruction of the biliary tree.......light sucks energy from your childs plasma (water) .....This disallows coherent oscillations on RBC membranes because of a lack of optimal water chemistry.........One of the keys here is to realize it is not a UBV spectral issue.........where Vitamin D is made. It is the visible spectrum that is the problem. This implies that the frequency of the EMF corresponds to the altered protein........there is a specificity..........Trust me.......I am glad you asked this.......it sets the stage for Sept webinar........The point I am making to you in a circular fashion is the wavelength of the EMF matters to your daughter........alter it and you alter her life. She can make Vitamin D with a Vitamin D light. Most kids with EPP get burned thru glass. UV light usually can not go through glass. Fake light is even worse for your daughter I bet. Fluorescent lighting specifically.......because it s loaded with blue light and is associated with a massive Electromagnetic action........certain light cause protein conformational changes caused by the excitations of the frequency of the EMF.........very few studies have been done on this in EPP because no one realizes that frequency of EMF has a specificity for protein folding and this alters the action of these proteins in water..........

# Thread: The Psychiatric Drug Crisis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-09-23T22:46:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/post-95839>

Very interesting article ... below are a few selected passages.

It’s been just over twenty-five years since Prozac came to market, and more than twenty per cent of Americans now regularly take mind-altering drugs prescribed by their doctors. Almost as familiar as brands like Zoloft and Lexapro is the worry about what it means that the daily routine in many households, for parents and children alike, includes a dose of medications that are poorly understood and whose long-term effects on the body are unknown. Despite our ambivalence, sales of psychiatric drugs amounted to more than seventy billion dollars in 2010. They have become yet another commodity that consumers have learned to live with or even enjoy, like S.U.V.s or Cheetos.

Yet the psychiatric-drug industry is in trouble. “We are facing a crisis,” the Cornell psychiatrist and New York Times contributor Richard Friedman warned last week. In the past few years, one pharmaceutical giant after another—GlaxoSmithKline, AstraZeneca, Novartis, Pfizer, Merck, Sanofi—has shrunk or shuttered its neuroscience research facilities. Clinical trials have been halted, lines of research abandoned, and the new drug pipeline has been allowed to run dry.

In the nineteen-forties and fifties, schizophrenic patients in some asylums were treated with cold-induced “hibernation”—a state from which they often emerged lucid and calm. In one French hospital, the protocol also called for chlorpromazine, a new drug thought to increase the hibernation effect. One day, some nurses ran out of ice and administered the drug on its own. When it calmed the patients, chlorpromazine, later named Thorazine, was recognized in 1952 as the first drug treatment for schizophrenia—a development that encouraged doctors to believe that they could use drugs to manage patients outside the asylum, and thus shutter their institutions.

[http://www.newyorker.com/online/blo...ac-ssri-mental-health-theory-discredited.html](http://www.newyorker.com/online/blogs/elements/2013/09/psychiatry-prozac-ssri-mental-health-theory-discredited.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-24T00:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-psychiatric-drug-crisis.8231/post-95850>

No one has a clue...........but the details of how the cortex works is in EE5. And more is to come...........

I am not an expert but this is what I see. Your cortisol is lowish in the morning and spikes at nighttime - it should go the opposite way. The hs-CRP needs some work. You are dehydrated based on your Bun/c ratio. Your liver is under a stress. Your iron and ferritin is high for a woman, I believe this can indicate inflammation in your body. You are hypothyroid based on your Reverse T3 but you really need more labs - Free T3 and T4 so you can get your ratio with the Reverse T3. Your testosterone is obviously high but I don't know much about hormones. You also need to work on your blood glucose level.

The sex hormones, I am working with a naturopath to fix. It's been an adventure ever since my hysterectomy, let me tell you, so I am not worried about those. I think we almost have it right. gall bladder out
size 14 when I got pregnant
Gave birth to a 10+ pound baby girl who after 3 months found out that there was something genetically wrong with her.
Right now she is 15 in chronological years but 1 year developmental.
was morbidly obese (260 pounds)
started going to the gym before classes. I think this is when the hormones all fell apart
working out at the gym at least 1 hour 4-5 times a week plus some on the weekends at home and developed a severe case of sacral ileitis in the hips and bursitis in them as well as the knees, pretty sure I tore something in my shoulder doing a bench press and that’s when the bottom dropped out.
doc finally diagnosed me with celiac and have been wheat free ever since.

March 2010 I finally got my engineering job but it was 45 minutes one way from our house. But I do really like my job and I am good at it.

bit insulin resistant

July 2011 LBM 140, actual weight was 193, height 68 inches.
2011 had a TSH done and it was 2.5
my T3 was 2.5, fairly low. I convinced him to let me start Armour in January 2012 and am up to 90 mg per day.
turned 40
08-13-2013, 03:39 PM----------I am due to start my next cycle maybe on Friday
08-20-2013, 11:58 AM---------follicular time of my cycle
08-31-2013, 01:56 PM--------I am in the second half of the cycle and that seems to be worse for me.
09-04-2013, 09:43 PM-------I am due to start my cycle on the 9th

Espousing for moderation or normality is not something to aspire to, it's something to get away from.

I left my iodine and other half of cytomel on the kitchen table
09-05-2013, 09:12 PM----------I am on 100 mg/gm Pg 2X a day.
09-08-2013, 06:12 PM----------Tomorrow the next cycle is suppose to start but I am hoping that with being on Pg, it might wait a bit but either way, doc said to take it to day 28 (tomorrow is 25)

but to do that you need to do preparatory work first.

I am now on Leptin reset since August 15 so I don't consume anything that would elevate my insulin. I have no idea what more to do to lower it, there is no diabetes in my family even though they are all overweight. Any suggestions?
>
> Thanks again for all the current suggestion and to all that are participating in this community. I feel so blessed to be here.

I am not a doctor. I am a 73yo man.

There are at least two problems with cortisol.

1, to have it in just right amount on average (over 24 hr period)

2, to have that cortisol in right amounts in the right time (good Circadian Rhythm).

Following dr Kruse's protocols should help in both.

\---------------------------------

How did you come to conclusion that your 7keto-DHEA is low?
I see:
DHEA-Sulfate 180.1 ug/dL
LDL Cholesterol Calc 105 HIGH mg/dL 0-99
Progesterone 3.6 ng/mL
Estradiol 22.0 pg/mL
49 y/o and had a total hysterectomy at the age of 36----------->menopausal
Cortisol (saliva) 6.7 ng/ml 3.7-9.5 (morning)
Cortisol (saliva) 2.7 ng/ml 1.2-3.0 (noon)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.1 ng/ml 0.6-1.9 (evening)
Cortisol (saliva) 1.0 ng/ml 0.4-1.0 (night)
Testosterone, Serum 121 HIGH ng/dL 8-48
Free Testosterone(Direct) 0.3 pg/mL 0.0-2.2
\-----------------------

I am assuming that you would get improvement with better levels of:
DHEAs=(275-400 μg/dL)
progesterone=(15-20)ng/mL
estradiol=(30-40)pg/mL

DHEAs & estradiol via direct supplementation
progesterone (and cortisol) are likely to see improvement if you check and correct your likely low pregnenolone.
Myself, for that purpose, I use
Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology (over internet)

With higher DHEAs levels you are likely to see higher Total Testosterone, so you may need to change your testosterone supplementation.

You have very high SHBG (Sex Hormone Binding Globulin) (High TT but low FreeT)
There is some chance at correcting that by working on your thyroid.
You are supplementing with thyroid hormones and getting very high RT3.
High RT3 is a way body dumps T4:

TSH <0.006 LOW uIU/mL 0.450-4.500
Thyroxine (T4) 8.0 ug/dL 4.5-12.0
T3 Uptake 45 HIGH % 24-39
Free Thyroxine Index 3.6 1.2-4.9
Reverse T3, Serum 28.4 HIGH ng/dL 9.2-24.1

You also have a good chance at identifying many of your deficiencies using this two tests:

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel
and
FAPCP Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

////////////////

post your fasting insulin level

# Thread: Not So Silent Newbie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Reset 2013
**Posts:** 65

### suzgaray — 2013-09-24T04:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-95892>

Hello My name is Suzette and I am DEAF (proud to be!) and this is my first attempt to write a journal here. Even though I have Ph.D. and the first Hispanic Deaf to get it in USA, written English is NOT my primary first language. I much rather prefer ASL or what we call VLOG (video signing!). However, I decided to embarked on this journey and would like to try doing a written journal here. I guess I should apologize if my English written is not clear or doesn't follow proper grammar. I just want to be able to write my mind freely so if I don't make any sense oh well at least it beats talking to myself .

Where do I begin?? Hmmm, to keep a long story short. I am T2D, and was on 12 different meds for the past 15 years and I felt like I was getting sicker, more tired, and in pain all the time. I finally decided to do something about it because my healthcare was more interested in feeding me PILLS then helping me find a way to better health. I believe this is true for many of us who are Govt./medicare dependents. I think we are the "gennie pigs" for trying new meds and collecting data whether its successful or not they don't care. So with that in mind, I started the Paleo/Primal diet under Mark's Apple, then moved to Tim Ferriss 4 hour body, then moved to Dave's Asprey's Bulletproof diet. BF diet worked the best for me with BPC in the mornings and IF protocol following eating 2x a day be done before 8PM. After 9 months I lost my first 70lbs (I am obese about 150lbs overweight so almost half way there!) I must say that I did not follow my doctor's advise to continue those meds, I threw them all away and went cold turkey had BG so out of whack for awhile (200-300 mg) but after the horrible storm and sickness (the usual WD and symptoms of letting go of gluten, sugar, following low carbs, etc..., about 6 weeks I finally was able to breath and see the light at the end of the tunnel and I keep focus on that. My sugars are better (maybe not to norm, but at least workable for me!) averaging in 120-145 mg range, headaches, body pains, night sweats, fatigue, all were gone. I could now focus more on the diet and the new energy gave me the desire to work out, see it all was worth it. The only meds I DO take is Levirmer at bedtime because I have that critical Dawn phenomena effect where my fasting sugars are still too high in the mornings (140-160 mg) but after mid morning it drops below 120 mg. I believe after I get a better grip on my eating habits and lose more weight it will better. I finally found Dr. Kruse stuff by accident in the forum of Dave's Asprey website. I was curious to know who he was and what his views were. I immediately bought his book EPI-Leptin Rx book and read it several times and decided to give it a try which means eating BAB at least 50g of protein in the morning, continue to follow paleo diet but little or no starches, AND soon will embark on CT protocol in hopes this will be the right direction to move forward. I have been stuck and regained 10 lbs in the past 4 months (not bad considering what others who have similar backgrounds-wink!). Soooo, this is where I begin.

Thanks for "listening" .

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T01:38:35+0300

That makes sense........Rubella is what I was betting on.......it explains perfectly well why you have the other problems. Rubella affliction at the time the temporal lobe and cochlea are growing are also tied to the exact same times the P 450 system is in the liver.......rubella cause massive losses of energy on the developing neocortex. Hearing develops adjacent to teeth gums jaw and gut on the somatosensory neocortex........so if you got rubella it means it affected the SQUID over the neocortex of the superior temporal lobe........this will affect thalamocortical pathways to and from your brain and your gut.......to give you T2D and obesity.......here is another irony.......Ammon's horn involved in acoustic pathways are the most energy intensive on the cortex......so when it is affected it will try to steal energy from adjacent areas.......and the adjacent areas in embryology would predict what happened to you........based upon my model of the quantum brain in EE5.

> Hi Caroline,
>
> Unfortunately, I do not tolerate meat fats well ( I barf!) so the only fats I seem to do well is MCT oils, avocado, bacon, grassfed beef, goat, elk, lamb, tallow, grassfed lard, etc... Can I continue to use these??

Except for MCT and avocado, all above have preponderance of omega6 no matter how organic or grass fed it is.

Proteins come with fat.
There are also separate fats.

The idea is to divide fat sources into three categories.

Souce of Omega 6
Source of Omega 3
Source of energy

Without benefit of testing, the chances are that you (and most everybody else) have way too much of omega 6.

I suggest that for the next six months you stay away from foods that contain lots of omega 6.
Omega 6 is everywhere, it is very hard to avoid it, you will not be 100% successful, do not worry you will not starve via lack of omega 6.

Additionally, unless found otherwise by testing, do not use fish oils or krill oils. They have unhealthy high levels of EPA.

......................
from salt sea waters

oysters
clams
crabs
octopus
snails
sardines
anchovies
mackerels
herrings
\----
\----
salmon
tuna

...
Eating can food watch for any oils they come with, often it is oil with high omega 6 content, drain it out or get other brand.

...

/

### caroline — 2013-09-26T08:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96172>

I can see that you are a worry wart! I understand completely that you want to do everything you can to help yourself.

Please check out other journals. Lots of people here are dealing with some very tough issues. Have a look at Cartweight's journal and Mr Pinkies journal. Mr. Pinkies (Kate) has lost way over 100 pounds this past year and is dealing with autism.

The really important points about the leptin reset are....tell me if I am boring you to tears - ok?

Eat a BAB within thirty minutes of rising. We also need to have the oxytocin talk!

Leave at least 4 hours between meals and before bed. Absolutely no snacking! If you are having coffee or tea - just black

Drink spring water - lots

Eat seafood as much as possible and lots of fat...

Get outside and walk in the grass or dirt or ocean. (where are you btw). Lay under a tree

C/T

Avoid fake light ..,.. When the sun sets - so should you

What is your EMF exposure? Are you limiting/avoiding wifi? ...this maybe the most important thing for you.

I am going to go back and re read what Dr. K. Said to you and get back to you....

About C/T.....I put a bag of ice on my tummy when watching tv. I just fill a large Baggie with water and freeze.
You can start by wearing a tight compression shirt and putting the ice on top. The compression shirt helps to collapse the fat cells.
I now just wear a light t- shirt and then after awhile put the ice right on my tummy.

But again - just do what you can for now. I promise that in a few weeks you will have it all down pat. And then it comes down to figuring out what works for you - what you Need to do for your own n=1

Sorry - I was just trying to make it simpler and maybe I have confused you more?

### caroline — 2013-09-26T10:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96181>

Suze ....I was going to ask you about your earthing mat. They can be a real problem and make things much worse for you.
You plug it in? are your outlets grounded? you need to be sure of that. I don't know much about the mats but they can put you in the wrong field. You could PM nonchalant and ask her ....she knows a lot about magnetics and stuff.

The most important thing is to check EMF's. do you use a microwave? \[throw it away!\] are you able to cut the power to your bedroom at night? Do you have a smart meter? Does your neighbour have a smart meter?

Take everything out of your bedroom......no TV, no clock radio etc. and then cut the breaker. \[you can leave the bed  \]

Is your computer wired in? get rid of wifi.....

### suzgaray — 2013-09-26T23:42:08+0300

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=96217)

### nonchalant — 2013-09-27T02:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96245>

This is the one I bought. I was surprised at the low cost. It is made of brass, with a copper layer on the outside. But it is 8 feet long, and quite difficult to pound into the ground. You may be able to find a shorter one somewhere. I absolutely love mine, the grounding sheet is so good for my back. The fact that the rod goes so deep means it gets down to the moist soil (an issue here in Texas) for better conduction. You would also have to buy a grounding rod clamp to attach the wire.

I also tried grounding with a short metal tent stake, while on vacation. I wasn't impressed, but it is much easier to just push a short spike into the dirt.

[http://www.homedepot.com/p/ERITECH-1-2-in-x-8-ft-Copper-Ground-Rod-611380UPC/202195737](http://www.homedepot.com/p/ERITECH-1-2-in-x-8-ft-Copper-Ground-Rod-611380UPC/202195737)

2\. Added tuna, shrimp, and scallops to my diet (at least each day I ate one of them!).
3\. Walked or hiked 5 plus miles each day
4\. Did my first ever CT in the swimming pool!! Had my partner watch me to make sure I didn't have a heart attack or pass out, but the water temp in the pool today was 60 degrees (ok, I know its not 55 degrees, but still was a challenge!!) I was able to stay in it for 20 minutes and afterwards the following had happened:
a. My skin felt great and soft!!!
b. my BG before was 131 dropped to 118!
c. All of my usual body pain either disappeared or I felt so "light" and calm
d. decreased hunger/cravings was noticeable later in the evening???

5\. I lost 5lbs over the weekend!
6\. Started to wear orange glasses earlier in the evenings when the sun went down instead of 2 hours before bed.

Hmmmm, so far good (though not perfect with sticking to Paleo 100% but not bad either). Tmw, Monday I will start doing CT 3x a week to see what noticeable differences it will make by the end of the week . It will start to get cold at night 50 degrees or so. I will try the evening time to do CT and see if it further aids with sleep?? I still taking 800mg Mag Malate during the day and Mag Threonate & Melatonin 10mg before bed. ### caroline — 2013-09-30T07:10:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96485>

I think you are doing just great! I am very proud of you. The pool sounds great and you will get used to it as the temp drops.

"everything in context". Is one
of JK's very favorite phrases. It doesn't matter what works for him or me or anyone else...yoy have to find your own n=1. So if you were to ask him "should I take more thyroid meds" how could he possibly answer you unless he was your Doc and could lay eyes on you and know all your labs?

Dr.K always tells us ....we need to have a partner Doc to get to optimal health.

Remember he is just the guy with the flashlight......he can't fix anyone....even his own family. He speaks about his wife on another thread about how he was 5 years into this before she jumped on board!

Please keep asking questions and especially if you don't quite understand what we say.

Congrats on the 5lbs ...and it seems C/T is already working its magic! If you ever feel like giving up always remember that you just have to start all over again.....Not worth it!! Always come here for support if needed.

If you have a choice buy other than tuna....but don't worry about it just yet...once again .you are doing so well - trust what Dr. K is saying ...But continue to question everything including him.

### caroline — 2013-10-02T01:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-so-silent-newbie.8238/post-96661>

Please don't be disheartened .... a lot of people here have made amazing progress - but it can take awhile. Remember - you didn't get broken overnite so give this process time to work ...it isn't a linear journey......

You are already seeing the positive benefits of C/T .......hang in there! It is daunting - but it is doable and we will support you.

Break it down a little....

How hard is a BAB? you get to eat bacon and eggs.... or a roast leg of lamb for breakfast and have a BPC

50 gr. of carbs or less .... just saute some greens or mushrooms in butter or CO

C/T - you said you enjoyed being in the pool ..that is really good for now and, actually it is great because the water temp will drop and you will become accustomed to it. Get your partner to put on some compression gear \[if they need it\] and get in with you.

I don't mean to minimise your feelings in any way - but if you want to get better you need to do better. This has been the case for all of us. We need to have absolute and 100% commitment on this journey .....we don't get a do over.

This doesn't mean that we don't slip up sometimes and lose focus ...of course we do ......but we all want a happy and healthier life and it takes our head in the right space to get there....and we have this amazing guy with his flashlight

You are a very intelligent woman and you deserve so much credit for all that you have achieved ....don't stop now..your future is only as bright as you want to make it. You are the limiting factor....

### caroline — 2013-10-11T05:24:19+0300

# Thread: Hshi's and high RT3 - best way forward?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flora — 2013-09-24T18:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-95951>

Have very recently found out I have Hashi's from thyroid lab results:
TSH-2.55
FT4-9.8
FT3-2.52
RT3-240
FT3/RT3 ratio- 10.5
TGAB-20.69
TPO- 17.97

Have also had thyroid ultrasound which didn't find anything unusual - no nodules or enlargement.

I've spent days reading everything I can find on this and StopTTM site to help me work out the best way forward.
My Dr says she doesn't treat unless ABs are above 50 but I have an appt. tomorrow to show her results of scan and T3/RT3 results (which I got myself) and I want to know whether to press for treatment.

My main symptoms are fatigue and lack of stamina plus aches and pains (mostly in legs) and inability to lose weight over the last year.
I have followed Jack for all that time and eaten epi-paleo. I'm now (for last 3 weeks) doing leptin rx and daily cold showers (will soon progress to bathtub) and re-started with blue blockers/ minimal lights after sunset and early bed/early rise, more oxytocin, wet grass grounding first thing in morning plus more when I can. Also have trimeter and have cut out excess EMFs where poss. in the house and also live way out in French countryside so virtually no external EMF pollution. Sleep has improved a bit with this (less waking, easier to go back to sleep, no bathroom breaks) but still never really feel refreshed in morning.

My other labs have all improved since last lot nearly a year ago, but only thyroid test then was for TSH (which has reduced from 3.35). But LDL (176) and Trigs (67) both reduced and HDL (83) up. HSCRP (0.9), DHEA (375), Vit D (61) and Ferritin (227) (all not previously tested) all seem pretty good.

However Pg /E2 ratio only appears to be 50 (if I've done the calc correctly) but I have used trans-dermal progesterone cream for around 10 years now! so would have thought it would be at a good level. Would the trans-dermal still show up correctly in a serum test? Or am I just not absorbing it?
Have also taken Mg malate for months but recently added D-ribose and DHEA (was 50, now 25mg after labs).

What confuses me is that Jack says AI almost always shows up with poor Vit D and trashed DHEA and mine aren't. So would I be right in thinking that I am mostly on the right track and what I've been doing for the last 12 months has actually improved the Hashi's? Has anyone with Hashi's actually managed to reduce ABs this way, with no drugs?

What I'm really wondering is how do I get that RT3 number down? i.e. do I need to press Dr tomorrow for either NDT or T3 only to address it, or do I wait until the next labs (6 months unless I pay for them myself) and see how they are then?
But I still have symptoms and I guess that's what's important - so do I go with meds to improve them or persist with what I'm doing?

I feel I should be able to work this out for myself but can't...so any input / help / discussion very gratefully received!

Many thanks

As to the field I'm in - well I'm literally surrounded by fields and forest with no large towns and no industry anywhere remotely near - no pylons - just a single power line that stops at our house entrance (doesn't even go over the house as we are the end of the line).
Yes we have wifi but my laptop has ethernet cx and we turn wifi off at night. Phone line is corded and I rarely use mobile.
I've wracked my brain to think of other sources (no microwave and have reduced vacuum cleaner use (such a sacrifice!), never use hairdrier now) but nothing else shows up on trimeter.
What could I be missing?
I did spend hours on the laptop with wifi (before I knew about EMFs) last winter reading all your blogs -could I still be dealing with that?

### Flora — 2013-09-26T16:35:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96203>

**Hashi's and high RT3 - best way forward?**

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=95954)
>
> When D and DHEA are not trashed it means one of two things.......your taking exogenous supplements or your environment is a really bad field..........

Many thanks for response Dr K, but am very confused as I thought a really bad field WOULD trash those levels.
I supplemented some D last winter - but not all summer - and only started DHEA recently, so am surprised it would get to optimal level so quickly - but great if that's the case!

My field is definitely not terrible - am in deepest rural France with no neighbours, no near power lines (just our own), no fake light at night and have checked inside EMF levels with trimeter to mitigate as much as poss.

So, if I have reasonable confidence in the above, and working through step by step, I'm thinking that it's likely adrenal fatigue causing the high RT3? (I know I need an ASI but can't find anywhere yet that does that in France)

So first step in following the Adrenal Rx is to restore Pg levels to increase BDNF and improve brain signalling.
My Pg /E2 ratio (I have calculated, so could be wrong) is 50 (E2 - 41pg/ml, Pg - 2,5 ng/ml (n=0.1-0.2 for menopause))- despite having supplemented trans dermal nat progest. cream for about 10 years. Does this mean I am not actually absorbing the cream / not taking enough / should try oral tablets instead? Can I do anything to change this?
I am doing all the other steps in that protocol, so do I just continue and hope that several more weeks will improve things?

Saw my Dr. yesterday and she'd never heard of RT3 and said all she could give me was some synthroid - but didn't want to (nor do I want to!) as she wouldn't normally treat until TSH reaches above 7 and my thyroid is 'burnt out'!!! AAAGH
However when I mentioned NDT (she hadn't heard of that either), she did say she could refer me to a naturopathic doc - so have an appt there in a couple of weeks.

I just don't want to do any further harm and get back to normal - it really scares me when I read all the horrible things that can happen from having Hash's - and although I'm taking responsibility for my own health I don't yet have the confidence that I'm interpreting the blogs correctly and doing it right.

However, I am loving my morning barefoot walks on wet grass in my PJs with the dog, followed by Bab, followed by long cold shower (which I actually look forward to now!). I yearn for the ocean though, (3.5 hour trip  ) Hoping to get there for a week before too long...

Can anyone who' s 'been there and done it' give me any help / encouragement / ideas.

Many thanks

### Grizz — 2013-10-02T01:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96657>

Flora,

You have a complicated explanation for a simple iodine deficiency. Iodine & Selenium are essential for the thyroid and it gets VERY sickly without it. Start with selenium to get your Hashi under control, then add the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

Dr. Dach Explains

> As you know, the problem with Iodoral (iodine) relates to the patient with Hashimoto's thyroiditis with elevated TPO and/or thyroglobulin antibodies. These patients may go into Hashitoxicosis after supplementing with iodine, exhibiting hyperthyroid symptoms possibly requiring hospitalization for thyroid storm.
>
> We are finding Hashimoto's thyroiditis to be more common than originally thought, almost epidemic, and it seems to be increasing. On a routine basis, initial evaluation includes thyroid antibody levels. In addition, all patients routinely must have a serum selenium level drawn. I have found that in almost all patients with elevated antibodies, selenium supplementation will normalize and drive down antibody levels on serial lab studies.
>
> In order to prevent the thyroid storm and other adverse effects from iodine in Hashimoto's patients, as you know, these patients must be supplemented with selenium first. This is why we first routinely draw a serum selenium, and for those cases below 135 ng/ml, we give 200-400 meg of selenomethionune for three weeks before starting the iodine supplementation at 6.25 mg (half the 12.5 mg tab) every other day.
>
> Starting with a lower dose of iodine avoids the various adverse effects of skin itching and metallic taste and GI symptoms that can be reported at first. These are usually transient, and after a week or so, the dose can be safely increased to a full tablet daily.
>
> Occasionally, we see a typical Hashimoto's patient with an elevated TSH around 5 or 6, and also an upper range free T3 around 350 to 400. These patients respond to selenium supplementation well, and follow-up labs usually show free T3 coming down to the 280-300 level, which in retrospect indicates the patient initially exhibited a slight thyrotoxic effect of the Hashimoto's thyroiditis, which then cooled down after the selenium. At this point it is safe to start the iodoral. If a Hashi's patient with a slightly overfunctioning thyroid and coexisting low selenium level is then started on iodine without first optimizing the selenium level, this will aggravate the thyroiditis, and possibly throw the patient into thyroid storm.
>
> Leaving the area of Hashimoto's and going to the general patient (with normal antibody levels without Hashi's), another big issue with iodine supplementation is that the TSH may go up, which is then interpreted by the family physician as a sign of hypothyroidism. This interpretation is an error, as the elevated TSH is cosmetic and unrelated to underlying thyroid function in these cases.
>
> A good example of this is a 55-year-old woman who came to see me after taking an iodine supplement from a health food store, hoping to shrink her goiter. The high school kid at the health food store gave her the wrong dosage instructions, and she had been taking a huge overdose of iodine for months. She felt fine, and a routine thyroid lab panel at her primary care physician showed a TSH of 65! The doctor was very concerned and told her that she "blew out" her thyroid with the health food store iodine, that she was severely hypothyroid, and gave her a prescription for Synthroid for her "hypothyroid" state.
>
> Here was a lady sitting in front of me who felt fine and had no symptoms of hypothyroidism. I sent her to the lab for a repeat panel and her Free T3 was normal at 330, indicating that she had normal thyroid function and did not need Synthroid. I explained to the patient that the elevated TSH was an "artifact" of the iodine supplementation, and that nothing else needed to be done other than stop the iodine and retest the TSH every 6 weeks or so. Six weeks later her TSH had come down to 10 and six weeks after that it was down to 4. The lady said thank you and I never saw her again.
>
> All of this information is David Brownstein's Iodine book, which I credit for helping me immensely to figure out the day-to-day practicalities of how to actually do iodine supplementation in our practice. In order to safely use iodine, the biochemistry and physiology of the thyroid gland must be reviewed and understood by the practitioner. Much of this information is newly uncovered, and is beautifully presented in Dr Brownstein's Iodine book.
> [http://jeffreydach.com/2011/04/06/iodine-is-safe-and-effective-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx](http://jeffreydach.com/2011/04/06/iodine-is-safe-and-effective-by-jeffrey-dach-md.aspx)

Please see more on Hashi here:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets)

Also avoid drinking water with fluoride. The fluoride totally distorts your thyroid testing numbers.
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/fluoride-and-your-thyroid/)

Grizz

### JanSz — 2013-10-02T04:31:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hshis-and-high-rt3-best-way-forward.8245/post-96671>

Hi Flora;

specifically:

recalculate dose if using other iodine/iodide source.
\-------------------

(E2 - 41pg/ml, Pg - 2,5 ng/ml (n=0.1-0.2 for menopause))

Your E2 looks good
progesterone aim at 15-20ng/mL
You are supplementing with progesterone cream and that is all that you have?
I think that you have low pregnenolone, do that test.

# Thread: Anyone else watch the show "Curiosity"? Specifically, Season 2 Episode 4?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-09-25T01:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96003>

The show itself is pretty cool, see more background here if you're unfamiliar: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curiosity\_(TV\_series)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curiosity_\(TV_series\))

But the episode I am referring to is called "Brainwashed". The goal of the episode was to see if it would be possible to "program" someone to kill "Manchurian Candidate" style (i.e. so they completed the killing as if it were a program in their brain that they could not control nor remember afterwards). They had a hypnotist evaluate a whole bunch of people to narrow it down to those most susceptible to hyponosis.

The part I found extremely interesting was the final test they used on the last 4 subjects: using an ice bath. At this point, they had already weeded out all of the actors, and those with weaker susceptibility, and they were down to the folks who could easily be hyponotized. In order to determine that they were deeply compatible, they hyponotized them and told them they'd be getting into a warm jacuzzi.

The first three were unsuccessful, and weren't hyponotized deep enough for this to work... but the 4th guy sat in the ice water for 2 minutes with almost no change in vitals!!

Think about the potential there... if ice baths could be framed like this for everyone, then they could be used as regular therapy with little to no impact on the individual's conscious mind.

Or, this could just be something to keep in mind in your personal endeavors. Mind over matter my friends, our brains and bodies are capable of far more than we give them credit for.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T01:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96005>

or maybe the the cold allows you to control the current generated in layer one of your cortex?

### nonchalant — 2013-09-25T05:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96036>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=96005)
>
> or maybe the the cold allows you to control the current generated in layer one of your cortex?

Hmmm... maybe I should continue to reread Jack's blogs while sitting in ice water.

### Marie — 2013-09-25T09:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-watch-the-show-curiosity-specifically-season-2-episode-4.8250/post-96051>

> [sjoshua said:](/goto/post?id=96003)
>
> Mind over matter my friends, our brains and bodies are capable of far more than we give them credit for.

YES!

Just an FYI about hypnosis...

ALL hypnosis is SELF hypnosis which allows you to access the 90% of your brain that has no "rational" filter. However, it does have a moral filter, so you can never be hypnotized to commit crimes.

Marie
(Who went to hypnotherapy school to learn to deal with some serious physical pain issues. My docs can't believe how high my paint tolerance is...as I've learned how to selectively turn off specific nerves.)

Good Afternoon this is my first post and I am excited to find this forum.

I did search for an answer but did not find one.

I'm looking for someone to help me understand why it is preferable NOT to exercise during the Leptin Reset program. I've seen where it says if you must exercise, do so after 5. That statement confirms that NOT exercising is the best.

Thanks in advance for your insight!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T21:00:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96096>

If you have inflammation present......you want to avoid exercise until you mitigate that risk using CT and the Epi-paleo Rx eating by Leptin Rx rules.......once your HS cRP, DHEA and D levels stop falling and stabilize then exercise can begin. If you read EE 5 closely the answer is buried between the words.......any amount of inflammation decrease electron flow to the neocortex and therefore less O2 is delivered to neurons. The less O2 delivered cause free radical formation and makes you more susceptible to electro magnetic radiation above the Schumann resonance.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-25T21:01:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96097>

Exercise works best when inflammtion is not present. It has to do with how the blood vessels on the surface cortex is aligned with the current of flow.......

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-25T22:25:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96109>

I've seen more success w/o exercising than I did when I was exercising. And Now I understand WHY. EE5 is connecting things together... the most I do is walk with my kids, enjoy nature and play with them or do yard work. that's it.

### caroline — 2013-09-26T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/importance-of-not-exercising-during-leptin-reset.8260/post-96144>

Welcome to the forum ....I totally agree .... the only things I do are walking and a bit of swimming and some kettle bell swings now and I have never looked healthier or been in better shape.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I do not like it cooked or fried as much at all, somehow it feels weird to my digestion, IDK why. I just love the fresh taste of it raw!

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=98831)
>
> This morning I tried around 150g of lean fish, 100g olive oil and an avocado. The protein/fat ratio is probably still a little too high but its lower than other days and I actually feel better today.

Do you have a good quality EVOO Hemming? I think when you eat that much of it it should better be great quality... I use an organic brand from Italy that you can track where and when it was made, no older that 6 months. It is divine! I think there are many not good olive oils out there, rancid... etc.

I am doing a way higher fat WOE now too! and way less protein. Not perfect yet because I run out of fat! Takes some time to research great sources for sure. By the end of this week I will have lots of grassfed beef fat again. I wanted to quit olive oil, avocado and coconut oil because we go into winter now. I will try to use only raw GF beef fat, fatty fish / fish heads, and grassfed raw butter for my fat sources this winter
