Out of the past 8 nights, I have spent 4 completely awake, and this is how it has been for me. I have just spent all last night awake, cannot relax or feel remotely sleepy. and on the nights I have slept it ranges from 1 1/2 hrs to 3 hrs, so it is SEVERE, once I'm awake that's it I cannot fall asleep again, although sometimes I may, just may be able t dream a bit, but find they are disturbing, so not sure if that is down to the medication or Serine? ,it is 6am in the uk, and feeling very emotional with not sleeping, ( a complete wreck in fact) it is badly affecting my mental and physical state. All my hormones are not working properly like they should. I just cannot function. It upsets me what it has done to my body.

I will reread CT7 thanks, is there a particular time of day thats best to do this?

i have been doing cold showers (no facility to do ice), for the past 5 weeks.

# Thread: Want to be optimal but....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sweetie86 — 2013-10-01T09:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96588>

Hello. I want to start the Leptin Rx, as eat small meals often and I don't think its optimal. I am tired all the time, my diet is clean, chicken, eggs, fruit (maybe too much fruit) and veg and 2-3 litres of water a day. I don't have anything seriously wrong with me that I need to take regular medication for except I have recently had CMV and EBV within two months of each other. However I was always tired before that. I do have sinus and allergies to dust mites (from a recent RAST test), and sleep issues, I am a poor sleeper, with bruxism so I wear a mouth guard at night. My eye is severely swollen right now from an allergic reaction to dust. I used to have chronic urticaria as a child but it has cleared up since age 14. Also extremely painful menstruation with causes, vomiting, migraines, IBS C and D reactions too. And I always have constant headache but it changes places, sometimes in my C-spine, sometimes at the front of my head, sometimes at the back of my head.

I also have IBS-C which I treat with magnesium citrate so as long as I take that then I'm fine but I do get scared one day I will run out of it or companies will stop making it. My digestive system gets worse (i.e. clogged) with things like prunes or dates or fiber supplements which is also strange as these help most people.
So my issues are just little annoying health issues but they add up. My new Dr says he might be saying its chronic fatigue. As I have to ration my activities for the day. Often as I am studying full time, this means studying for 7 hours then resting in an uncomfortable half awake stupor for a few hours til bed time which then takes me 4 hours to settle to sleep. I do get things done and get good grades I just have to plan for my energy levels on what I can do every day. I try and walk 45mins each day and get sunlight in the mornings too. I sometimes do more intense workouts but on more intense exercise programs I need afternoon naps and take a long time to recover once I've two weeks into the programs.

I have noticed this epi paleo requires fish and good fat. When I ate fish and prawns the other day I broke out in a big rash on my stomach and thighs and had an itchy throat. I never ate seafood as a child or until recently I've tried prawns only a handful of time. And if I eat high fat, i.e. olive oil, red meat (grass fed t-bones etc) with fat on, I break out in terrible acne. Deep stuff that hurts and just looks ugly. I stopped eating the fats and the acne goes away. Nuts generally hurt my stomach too but I stay away from them usually and I keep away from dairy because of acne issues. I did have a coffee addiction starting but Im done to one or zero cups a day, a few black teas.
I'm not overweight but would like to change body fat composition and drop 6lbs. And mainly get optimal of course! I think I'm having too much omega 6. Frustrating as I eat better than most people my age (27 years old), well I should say I eat 'cleaner' than most people my age but have way less energy. Obviously something is preventing me from being optimal, but each time I try and eat seafoods or good fat, I have these physical presentations of rashes or acne. Anyone with a similar experiences with fat and acne? Or seafood allergy? Or low energy levels that require small frequent meals? What did you do to overcome this? And going from a frequent small meals to 3 large ones how did your digestive system cope? I think mine may shut down from the BAB due to the IBS-C? I cant stand feeling clogged and will do almost anything to avoid it.

Sorry this post is so long thank you for reading. I wouldn't really dare talk to people in my life about all this, as all these little niggling illness aren't actually that serious but I am miserable (I know there are people that are worse off than me and I am grateful for what I do have. I'm generally pretty positive though it sounds like Im a whinger sorry!). I see Dr Kruse and the Leptin Rx as the way out but I feel like I can't tolerate increased acne or feeling clogged up. Thank you for reading my post. Hoping to hear any suggestions or similar experiences?

### Dali Dula — 2013-10-01T15:19:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96598>

Try this sweetie: do the leptin rx as directed. Incorporate bone broths from grassfed animals, fish bone broth, duck broth. Drop the chicken and don't eat shellfish right now you will tolerate it better after some detox. The broths are the miracle food try to include them in every meal. they are also inexpensive and delicious. Drop all sugars starches, and dairy. this includes fruit. Don,t worry about exercising right now. Walk and swim in cool water if it available. A few weeks of following these simple guidelines should make you feel better. If you smoke or drink alcohol that should stop or you are wasting your time. Then you can keep digging. It is a deep journey. Don't fear the fat, run from the low-fat.

### nicld — 2013-10-01T16:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-be-optimal-but.8314/post-96600>

Look into Ludol Iodine (check out the iodine thread). I had IBS-C and since taking that, it has gone. I was on Mirilax sometimes 2X a day because, like you, I feared getting blocked up. Once the system stopped, the pain started. Also started the disabled DD on it and we no longer have to give her Mirilax. Due to some of the meds she is on she had terrible constipation. I am only doing the 3 meals a day and I think it has helped the GI system. Just my N=1.

You should find alot of help and suggestions here. Good luck.

Nicole

Seanb4....you could be getting a lot of dirty electricity which is basically what travels on your grounding wire if the house electrical wires aren't done well, or are low grade wire and cable. There was a lot of activity around this topic during the EMF series. Grounding into the ground is great (as long as you aren't in a high voltage area) grounding through your building wiring is very iffy. AND it isn't a matter of just checking if you are plugged into the right hole in the outlet.

There was a huge difference in having a grounding sheet go out to the earth versus plugging into an outlet for me. I'm relying on the magnetico and a schumann resonator to provide a good field while I sleep now, but I used to use the grounding sheet with the wire out the window down to the ground. I do have my computer grounded through a wire out my window attached to a ground rod.
Good luck with all this!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-happens-when-water-is-consumed-on-an-empty-stomach.8316/post-96931>

If you want to get healthy fast I bet it will help huge. I did not even ground in that tent, but every morning I went for my morning toilet into the forest bare feet and got some nice grounding in.
Sean maybe you have a beach nearby, you could sleep on the beach? I bet there are many possibilities, when you look around  there always is. Maybe the solution is right in front of your nose you just have not seen it yet

### seanb4 — 2013-10-06T09:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-exactly-happens-when-water-is-consumed-on-an-empty-stomach.8316/post-97005>

I'm not currently doing that, it's on my todo list.

The orange juice is for potassium and carbs. I've found I experience less stomach symptoms with the least volume of food I have. Some months ago I found I could drink a cup of this "soylent" drink twice a day, with water in-between, and it would produce less bloating overall.
I stopped doing this as I assume I was missing quite a bit of good stuff found in real food. But yea so far that's produced the best results dietary wise than anything else I have tried and I am sure it's volume related.

# Thread: Oliveview's journal

### oliveview — 2013-10-01T14:30:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96597>

Hi everyone! Here's my recap from Day 1

Got up and had my BAB with steak and eggs. I had a horrible day at work but stayed full until lunch. Normally I would have done some stress eating but I didn't! YAY! I ran home around lunch because the kids chameleon died (it was a bad day all around) and had ground beef for lunch. Supper was pork tenderloin and a bit of broccoli with butter. I had a nice icy cold shower in the morning and got the house dark by, well...dark.

I am still waiting on my labs to be posted. I had to pick and chose and not get all of my labs at once. I will be going for a physical next week and get some more of them done. I went ahead and got the ones I knew I would have to pay for. I won't get my female hormones checked until early December. I know it's not perfect but it's where I am.

My optimal goals
1\. Feel better
2\. Live longer

Those really are my ultimate goals. I am 46, almost 47, female and morbidly obese. In the last year, for the first time ever, I can't keep up with my family. We just got back from a trip to Disney and I was miserable. I have tried everything to lose weight and nothing works! I lose the first week or two and then that is it. I've even had lapband surgery a number of years ago and it's been a disaster. I am coming to Epi-Paleo with the mindset that it may take me months to start to lose weight, that this is more reset than it is a traditional "diet". Diets don't work and unfortunately I've been shamed and put down by the medical profession saying that I just need to eat less. I know that I have leptin and metabolism issues, maybe hormones as well. I've been talked into antidepressants as well, anything to deal with the pain and the shame of obesity. But I have know from the beginning that there was something wrong with me. It's just nice to find somewhere where people seem to understand!

### oliveview — 2013-10-02T00:39:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96654>

Day 2 is going well so far but more on that later.

I live in the US, actually on the coast of Georgia.

Lab tests that I am waiting on are Leptin, HS CRP, Hgb A1C, rT3, T4, TSH, TPO, Thyroid Antithyroglobulin, Vit D. I am planning to get more labs next week when I go in for my physical: CMP, CBC, Ferratin, and others. I have not ordered the saliva test yet. Is there somewhere better to order it from?

I was always a active kid and enjoyed sports. When I was a teenager I got injured playing soccer and ended up having 14 knee surgeries in 2.5 years. At that point I gained 100# and my life has never been the same. I always struggled to get the weight off, but in my late 20's I went on phen-fen. I lost 100# in 3 months without even trying. That was the point that I knew there was something very wrong with me. When they pulled it from the market, I gained the weight back...plus some.

I had my 3 children over the course of 3.5 years in my mid to late 30's. I was very obese but did well with my first pregnancy. My second I was sicker and had lots of nausea and vomiting. I actually had a mild case of pneumonia and was on antibiotics when I delivered him. By the time we were pregnant with our third, I think my body was just depleted. I was hospitalized numerous times for dehydration, pre term labor, and I was profoundly anemic. Fortunately, I had great labors and delivered 3 healthy children.

The weight loss only got more and more difficult. We tried every diet there was to try and I just continued to gain weight. I gave in to frustration and had a LapBand done about 5 years ago...that's a whole other post but to sum it up...disaster! I am now up about 35# from the time that I and the LapBand. sigh.

Anyway, I have never been a sleeper. Even as a child I never slept well. I still don't sleep well.

We have very few computers at work and no wifi. We have both at home. I haven't read too much about the EMF exposure but I'm getting there. There is just lots of info to absorb.

If you're still reading, thank you! Comments and suggestions are welcome.

It sounds like you have made a great start on testing. I get my ASI done thru Canary club - ZRT labs. Dr. K.used to recommend them - I don't think that has changed.

I feel very excited for you ..you have a great attitude!

### oliveview — 2013-10-03T17:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-96795>

We drove last night to our place here in the mountains. We got to bed very late and I didn't sleep we'll being in a new place. We were able to open the windows and sleep in the cool air. Also, we could hear the trickling of a little creek by our window. Very nice. I've had to eat out dinner last night and breakfast in this morning but I was able to keep on track. It was more like an hour before I had my BAB this morning but that was the best I could do. I had eggs, steak and avocado. Vacation is nice but I look forward to being home and having control over my food.

We (my very supportive husband and I) have been reading about supplements. As I get a handle on the eating, they will be next. Well, that and CT. Lots to do but I'm still feeling motivated! I don't have lots of energy but I certainly don't have carb flu, thankfully. I was lc paleo for a month and then went off for two weeks before starting the leptin rx/epi-paleo. Maybe that's why I'm not feeling too bad.

So today starts Day 4...

### oliveview — 2013-10-13T17:44:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-97725>

So, today is Day 14 on the leptin reset. My husband and my cousin (who lives with us) are also on the reset. I am feeling somewhat better than I did previously. I think my energy levels are still low but I am working on that. I'm trying not to be frustrated but its hard. I weigh significantly more than my cousin, we are roughly the same age, we eat the exact same thing every day and she is dropping weight fast. I know the reset is not about losing weight but this really stings. I'm trying not to focus on it but it brings me back to the same ole thing of not being able to lose weight. I know that I just have to keep moving forward.

I am going to focus on the positive now that I'm done whining! So, today I am thankful that I have no cravings. What a huge relief it is to not be white knuckling it though those sugar and carb cravings!!

I have had to switch to only doing it 2X at night during the week since it seems to take me so long to rewarm that it stops sleep and right now, sleep in more important. I have been taking a warmer shower afterwards for a couple of minutes and that helps with the rewarming and probably is the wrong thing to do but I need SLEEP. I do take really long ones on the weekend when I have all day to move around and rewarm. It was really nice in the summer when I could go outside and let the sun rewarm but when the temp is only 55, it will not really be warming. Oh and it is really nice when the hubby lets be snuggle up next to him to rewarm and get some oxytocin release too

Good luck and we can do this.

### freesia — 2013-10-16T23:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98045>

Just a suggestion for managing the later baths. Maybe experiment with a shorter time in the bath since you can't do it earlier, then as rewarming gets faster (it does happen!) gradually increase the time in the water again...to tolerance. Tolerance = to where it doesn't impact sleep negatively.
I had to use a hot water bottle on my feet a few times last winter so I could get to sleep. That was a transitional tool and it didn't hurt!

### oliveview — 2013-10-24T16:09:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98842>

Thank you everyone for the advice on the CT. I will try and see if it helps. I've been a bit discouraged as my weight was down and now its back up. BUT--I'm still here and still following the program!

### caroline — 2013-10-25T01:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oliveviews-journal.8317/post-98938>

please hang in there! keep a diary of what you eating .... account for everything.......Dr. K. calls it the "Hawthorne affect"

### nicld — 2013-10-02T20:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-96716>

### Justfourme — 2013-10-03T04:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-96759>

The livestock started getting sick, had miscarriages, and their milk supply decreased to almost nothing. The cows went from being healthy looking to gaunt looking. Which when most of your income is from the milk cows, basically shuts down your business. I don't recall exactly how my brother tied the problems to electricity, but they were basically the last house at the end of that electric line. Experts from the college came out to run tests which showed higher than normal levels of electricity in the milking barn. The electric utility company came out and said according to their tests, the levels were within the "acceptable range". At that time I asked if this electricity problem is causing all of these health issues with the livestock, what is it doing to you? They moved within a couple years.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-11T23:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97561>

Neighbor boy across the street from us in the early 1970s had a brain tumor in his teens. Probably was benign though I don't know for sure. There was a transmission tower on the hill just a few hundred yards above their house. On damp foggy nights we could hear a "sizzle" and humming sounds from the wires. Even back then we wondered whether living directly under those wires contributed to this tumor. His surgery was successful and as far as I know he's still fine.

The book is an eye-opener and it calls into question all the research that tells us chemicals are safe that have been banned in Europe and Russia. Makes you wonder about climate change research and the overwhelming campaign to belittle those who call for a healthy skepticism.

It's clearly an economic decision that chemicals need to be proven conclusively that they are not safe to be removed from the market. Shouldn't a society concerned about health first require _proof of safety_ before a chemical could be introduced? And then there's the much more complicated issue of interaction among multiple chemicals . . .

We are at the mercy of certain manufacturers and bureaucrats--a sometimes deadly combination.

my thoughts on this at least from an American POV - trace back to Manifest Destiny. This has a HUGE impact in how we do things as Americans, as does our dysfunctional gov't. ### JoeBranca — 2013-10-12T00:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97569>

"my thoughts on this at least from an American POV - trace back to Manifest Destiny. This has a HUGE impact in how we do things as Americans, as does our dysfunctional gov't. "

which ties into Marino's account of his conversations at the international conference with all the power company bigwigs he was invited to speak at (about halfway through the book). It's actually difficult for me to accept the things were said by a couple of those folks as he reports in the book... they sound like the scripted lines of cheesy robber baron villains in old movies. But I suppose with enough drink in someone, they're liable to reveal anything...

Marino's reflections on what he heard and experienced are more interesting... that the push for above ground powerlines can't be fully explained by the pursuit of profit in context of the power companies. Rather they would want their visible monuments, to remind everyone how they maintain and make possible modern society. In other societies one instead might have numerous inescapable statues and images of their "benevolent" dictators for a similar reminder.

### Hope — 2013-10-12T00:59:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-ultimate-emf-book-that-is-a-must-read.8321/post-97570>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=97566)
>
> Why is it exactly that Europe and Russia are much more tuned in? and seem to put people first?

Audrey..just my humble $0.02 as someone who spent 21 years growing up over in Europe (not Russia).....and the #1 clear reason to me is that they are NOT quite AS crazy greedy as they are in the US. Hands down. It's so sad but so so true, in my book. I see it so much in so many examples.

Just one teeny tiny example....over there, many employees by law get 4 weeks vacation a year. Paid. Have for decades. Employees and stuff is more expensive there, but they are saving money on healthcare expenses for sure. Plus people have about HALF the chronic disease rate they do here. Maybe that will change with the whole EMF thing etc. but it's all.about.money. and government not by the 'government' but a few select large corporations in the US. And it stinks to high heaven. It's also why lots of Americans call lots of Europe 'socialist', which I hate...I call it SMART. And not bad for the size....one of the countries is no bigger than the size of Wisconsin, but it manages to have the 3rd or 4th largest economy. In the world. Not bad for a place with 'socialist' healthcare, retirement etc. It's far from perfect too of course.....but to me this is simple and clear as mud.

More so than ever before.

They will stop and eat everything on the way to fuel up for the journeys to South America. All that said....I'm sure the conifers feel like a good place to be even without the berries.

# Thread: doing leptin reset and c.t for chronic pain, need help with labs please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/>

**Posts:** 21

### ash — 2013-10-03T00:01:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96743>

hello everyone, I am 53yr old male and have suffered chronic pain in my side since having total hip replacement in 2000. also caught hep c at this time. was told the hep c had cleared up by hospital in 2002. pain is constant in my side, like someone sticking a red hot poker in my body. also have had unexplained aches and pains around chest area, neck, shoulders for a few years. these are easing off now since starting leptin reset and ice baths alternate days. the pain in my side is still bad and have been placing ice on the area in the bath. am reading jacks book and realise I need some labs but would like to know which ones to prioritise. I am 5' 6 and weigh between 125lbs-130lbs now. I have dropped over 10lbs in the 3 weeks I have changed to epi paleo and leptin reset. also I have horrible sleep, barely managing more than 3 hours at a time. I have been on 80mg-100mg oxycontin daily for approx 2years. the 6 years previously it was tramadol x 8 daily. at the moment im only taking 12mls methadone daily to help me come off oxycontin as the withdrawals are horrible. i cant get under 10mls daily as the pain in my side kicks in then and i cant go without any pain relief. i am finding all this stuff im learning really amazing and am praying this is going to be my saviour as have felt suicidal in the past with this pain that will never leave me. i hope that someone can give me some info as to where i can get labs done and how do i go about having a consult with dr kruse. thanks, ash. i don't know if this matters but i live in Southwales UK ( does the uk do these labs anywhere etc). am looking forward to meeting others on this forum. thanks ,ash. p.s there is other medical history before my hip replacement as i had a massive motorbike accident in 1983 and smashed pelvis, femur, radius. also have had crazy chaotic life taking many many class a drugs and totally screwing my circadian rhythms and sending my cortisol, dopamine etc all over the place. xxx

### caroline — 2013-10-03T00:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96744>

Welcome Ash to the forum. It must just be so awful to be in that much pain on a daily basis. Jack's book has a great chapter on labs. I am in Oz and get saliva/ASI testing done thru ZRT labs in the U.S. Some one else will come along with more suggestions on labs for you.

Please start a journal in the journal section - maybe transfer the above to it. Journals are great for commitment and everyone here can follow your progress and offer help and support and Dr. Kruse likes to know more about you to help if he can.

Please have a look at Dr. K's podcasts - they are a quick and easy way to get information. Also, if you can manage it.....become a gold member and then you have access to all the webinars and live Q&A. There is a webinar every month and then a live Q&A a few days later where you can ask questions. Jack is starting a massive series now that is mind bending.....the first one was a week ago and he promises the next one \[October\] will be off the charts! He is very easy to listen to and very direct as well and he is leaving no stone unturned. Think of this as an investment in your health and your journey to optimal. That all being said ..... of course you don't have to ....we will support you any way we can.

That is great that your are eating epi-paleo and started the reset and doing C/T. C/T will be a game changer for you - can you manage it every day?

You need to be very, very aware of your EMF exposure - how is your home and work place for wifi etc.? You need to limit these as much as possible - as soon as possible.
There was an EMF bootcamp that I think you can still purchase if you are interested.

Observing light cycles is critical for you ....Go outside and look at the sun upon arising ...and when the sun sets - no fake light. Do you have blue blocker glasses? extremely important for you too. Especially limit wifi in your bedroom - no clock radio/TV etc. and cut the power if you can.

Get outside as much as possible - no shoes and as little clothes as possible. Do you have a river, lake, ocean near by?

Only drink spring water - you need to eliminate fluorides etc. pronto

Please read about the magnetico sleep system - I would think that would be critical for you as well.

Keep reading Dr.K.'s blogs and make sure you read the comments after each blog .....there is gold in the comments.

You often have to read the blogs many times to start connecting all the dots - please keep asking questions.

We are all on this Journey together and Dr. K. is shining his flashlght for us.

There are lots of great stories in the journals of folks working on there road to optimal - maybe you can offer some help to them too.......

### caroline — 2013-10-03T00:57:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96745>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T13:26:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doing-leptin-reset-and-c-t-for-chronic-pain-need-help-with-labs-please.8325/post-96779>

They key with Hep C is you must eat a much higher fat diet especially if this is associated with chronic pain. The Hep C has major effects at cytochrome 1 and on superoxide dismutase in the mitochondria. Hep C will destroy sleep and this destroys the DC current below the myelin level that resides outside the axons.......when you can not sleep well you can not regenerate proteins well and the result is increased pain as time elapses. The Epi paleo RX acts directly as a resetter of epigenetic signaling by directly affecting DNA methylation and histone de acetylation. Anything that acts an HDAC inhibitor in our cells, blocks mTOR signaling by increasing the process of autophagy. Autophagy happens in sleep normally. Sleep has the exact same effect as the Epi-paleo Rx. Autophagy occurs maximally in humans during sleep when we are regenerating. October 2013 webinar is about this very issue.

### caroline — 2013-10-04T00:48:10+0300

Hep C single-stranded, positive-sense RNA virus. Positive-strand RNA virus replication complexes are universally associated with intracellular membranes like that on the inner mitochondrial membrane. This is why fat is best for Hep C. beta oxidation limits mTOr activation. HCV only infects the liver because all the receptors and proteins it acts upon are there. Once in it ruins hepatocyte mitochondria. HCV virion enters uninfected liver cells via LDL-c receptor. LDL-c receptor remains the same even after fats have been delivered to the liver's mitochondria. The more triglycerides are delivered the more HCV virus gets up taken to the liver mitochondrial to trash it. This is why high carb diets and HCV are toxic. Same thing with booze. Booze increases TG like mad.
This is why bad diets and Hep C show other disease caused by ROS. If you have hep C you need to eat a boat load of saturated fats that enter the ETC at cytochrome 2. Hepatitis C is also associated with SjÃ¶gren's syndrome (an autoimmune disorder); thrombocytopenia; lichen planus; porphyria cutanea tarda; necrolytic acral erythema; insulin resistance; diabetes mellitus; diabetic nephropathy; autoimmune thyroiditis and B-cell lymphoproliferative disorders.

# Thread: Creating Clarity ~ Clarissa's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ClarissaK — 2013-10-03T03:59:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/post-96754>

Hello everyone,

Just found this site and this group recently and have been reading and listening to Jack's advice and knowledge voraciously!

I have been on/off "Paleo" for a few years, though it never helped me for long and now I am understanding why it didn't answer my health issues in itself. The info I've been reading here is like an awakening! I feel that Jack has tapped into the very things that make us Human and I for one am ready to finally start feeling like one.

I wanted to start this journal today because I believe that MY NEW LIFE is beginning for me on this date. I met my husband 14 years ago today and instead of celebrating it we are in the midst of a divorce. I have been struggling with that, not wanting to do it, but realized I simply must live for myself now, LET GO of the things that are "no longer serving me." So today is my day to start falling in love with ME, caring for myself as if my life depends on it (which it does).

I was "lucky" enough to read this journal entry by Inger last night and it was so life-affirming to me, I plan to thank her sometime. I do believe that things can and will align to assist us in our journey if we allow them to. [http://www.jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/](http://www.jackkruse.com/winter-solstice-special-blog-the-inger-effect/)

I also listened to one of Jack's blogs today, the one from March 2013. In it he says "Sometimes a Goodbye is a second chance for Life." ITA, and am saying goodbye to my old way of living, being co-dependent and lost, hungry yet overweight, tired and bored with life, ready to find some MAGIC.

A little bit about my road to ill-health:

I was raised on a pseudo-healthy SAD diet in the 70's/80's. I had chaotic, divorced parents (one of which was very controlling, one was a drug addict). As a little girl I was very thin, always had colds and painful legs, had energy but craved weird things like rock salt and dry dog food.

As a teen I was thin enough, though felt fat and started the diet-roller-coaster that got me where I am now, age 44. My 20's were simply not healthy, didn't exercise much (didn't think I needed to as I was still thin enough), ate out a lot, went from being a junk-food-junkie to a Vegetarian and back again. In my mid-20's I was addicted to cola, too, and the low-fat nonsense kept me from ever feeling very good. By my late 20's I had become hyper-vigilant and everything woke me up in the night. By the time I had my son, age 33, my sleep failed to restore me at all and I sank into anxiety/depression/insomnia followed Chronic Fatigue, IBS, and Fibromyalgia. And it hasn't ever gotten better.

The past 10 years have been a near-nightmare. I couldn't work. Lost friends. My marriage suffered. I crawled into a shell. My body felt like it was 80, not near 40. My moods were all over the place, and I tried EVERYTHING I could do "fix" my diet (fasting, raw veganism, the GAPS diet, juicing, supplementation, cleanses, low-carb, etc.). I found the only thing that helped was doing Paleo, but after a couple of months that would fail me too. However I kept going, kept trying, hoping something would click. Doctors were no help. I became despondent at times, what with my aching body and my foggy head not allowing me to have a life... BUT I am pretty tenacious and stubborn, wouldn't give up. I come from Pioneer stock and we keep on keeping on...

So... after digging down VERY deep into the depths during my divorce and rediscovering my strength I have decided that I am NOT going to stand for living in the "shell" of my life anymore. I want to be a Starfish! I want to live again!!

I live in Portland, Oregon, not too far from the ocean and my dream is to move nearer to it. I have always loved the ocean, won't ever live far from it (I lived in England for 7 years after getting married, just near the Kent coast). I feel that my urge to be near the coast is instinctual, and Jack is just adding to my conviction about that!

So, what am I doing now? Making my diet as Epi-Paleo as possible. Fish daily (ate little before), grass-fed meats (though less beef now and more pork and organ meats), good butter/ghee, eggs, seasonal veg/fruits, and I'm weaning off the sugar/starches. I like sweet potatoes and also allow myself grapefruit and apples. Sugar in my tea is very hard to give up though, ugh! I tried cold-turkey on the sugars/starches, but my son has been sick and I've been up for 2 nights and I have been a zombie (fell back on comfort foods, though much less than before)...

I am turning off the TV/wi-fi early in the evening, have low lights on from dusk, and am going to bed at 9:30 (I had been staying awake watching crime dramas and/or reading until I about fainted from tiredness). I am taking walks most days, and doing yoga before bed. And...so far there were 2 days when it all came together (sleep, food, lower stress, lower EMF's) and I FINALLY woke up with some energy and some hope. I plan to keep doing my best, be on this website every day, keep a journal, get off the sugars, learn the protocols, and love myself again.

Any and all advice is appreciated--I look forward to reading more of your journals, too (when I am not so tired).

I'm so excited about this journey, feel so blessed to be finding the CLARITY I have been seeking. XOXOXOXO

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T04:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-clarity-clarissas-journal.8327/post-96755>

welcome........you have joined at a time where the blogs will be foundational and key for wellness

# Thread: Want to see how current my info is?  10/2/2013 WSJ article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T17:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/post-96798>

By LAURA LANDRO CONNECT
Critically ill patients who survive a stay in an intensive-care unit, where they are often heavily sedated and ventilated, can find themselves mentally impaired long after release. A new study says the problem is far more common and lasting than previously believed.

Nearly 80% of patients with prolonged ICU stays showed cognitive problems a year or more later, and more than half exhibited effects similar to Alzheimer's disease and traumatic brain injury, according to a report Wednesday in the New England Journal of Medicine.

The researchers, at Vanderbilt University in Nashville, Tenn., and the Tennessee Valley Veterans Affairs Geriatric Research Education and Clinical Center, said the findings highlight the need to modify ICU practices that put patients at risk of cognitive problems, and to offer rehabilitation programs to restore concentration, thinking and memory function after patients leave the hospital.

Enlarge Image

Jason Andrew for The Wall Street Journal
Joan Healy said that after a stay in an ICU, she suffered 'brownouts' during conversations, when she couldn't fully grasp what was being said.

Intensive-care units traditionally keep patients heavily sedated, immobilized and on mechanical ventilation to keep them free of pain, anxiety and agitation as they heal or undergo invasive procedures. But prolonged sedation can trigger or exacerbate delirium, a temporary state of acute brain injury that can also be caused by conditions such as septic shock, an infection that spreads through the body and is a common reason for being admitted to an ICU.

Delirium, long linked to higher rates of death, is also associated with long-term mental impairment in ICU survivors, the study found. E. Wesley Ely, senior author of the study and founder of Vanderbilt's ICU Delirium and Cognitive Impairment Study Group, said some ICU-related brain injury could be prevented if the duration of delirium in the ICU is shortened. A new protocol Vanderbilt and others are following includes weaning patients from sedatives carefully, waking them regularly to see if they can breathe on their own sooner, and getting them out of bed and moving as soon as possible.

The study included 821 patients with respiratory failure or septic shock who had a median ICU stay of 5 days. Their ages ranged from 18 to 99. Only 6% had pre-existing cognitive impairment, and 75% developed delirium during their hospital stay. Assessed for cognitive function at one year after discharge, 34% still had scores similar to moderate traumatic brain injury, and 24% had scores similar to patients with mild Alzheimer's disease. In total, nearly 80% scored lower than predicted by age and education.

"Whether you are young or old and even if you are previously healthy, if you go into an ICU with significant breathing problems, are on a breathing machine or in shock, you may not be coming out with an intact brain," said Dr. Ely. And after discharge, he adds, "so many people are living in their own private hell of mental fog and no one around them knows about it, tells them it is going to happen, or tries to prevent or treat it."

Joan Healy, a 48-year-old who lives in Huntington, N.Y., spent a week in an ICU three years ago suffering from septic shock due to an infection. For two months after discharge, she said all she could do was sleep, and for the first year after recovery she would experience what she described as "brownouts," where she could do little more than nod and smile during conversations without grasping what was being said.

Her family was just happy she was alive, making every effort to help, but "they can't fathom that I can't follow directions, that a simple recipe to cook for dinner is torture," Ms. Healy said. "It's like being in a dim room at sunset, you can navigate but you don't feel you have a grasp on everything going on around you."

Ms. Healy, who does administrative work for her husband's orthopedics practice, reached out to experts at Vanderbilt, who referred her to a computer-based program for attention and memory problems, which she said has been helpful. "I am not the person I was, but from last year to now, I am doing better, and I am still working hard at it," she said.

Malaz Boustani, chief operating officer of the Indiana University Center for Innovation and Implementation Science, who wasn't involved in the study, said its findings add to the evidence about brain-health concerns for ICU survivors and highlight the need for recovery programs that "coordinate the complex cognitive, functional and psychological recovery needs of Americans who survived a critical illness."

Write to Laura Landro at [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
[http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052702303492504579111361055134336.html](http://online.wsj.com/article/SB10001424052702303492504579111361055134336.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-03T17:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/post-96799>

now go re read the EMF smoking gun for biology geeks link in the Sept 9, 2013 EE4 blog post............

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-see-how-current-my-info-is-10-2-2013-wsj-article.8333/post-96833>

yep.......From a recent blog:

WHAT DOES THIS RESEARCH IMPLY?

When you lose your DC regenerative current in Schwann cells and the brain’s microglia and oligodendroglia for any reason at all, you can not regenerate the normal arms of neuro immunity or of cellular immunity from your thymus gland where the T Helper cells go to mature; This means you can’t activate T-Helper-3 cells so T-Helper-17 cells begin to dominate. When it TH3 dominates long enough what happens? Calcium leaves the pineal gland and circadian biology is altered, thus, we lose the yoking of the Vitamin A and D cycle in the brain, and the result is a spike in NF kappa beta and IL- 6 in the brain and peripheral tissues. These are the main inflammatory cytokines in the brain. These are the same effects seen experimentally in Blackman’s work mentioned earlier in the blog. The result is that antibodies are made from constantly activated B cells because the T cells can not turn them off and the subsequently ”tag” the different tissues in which this altered field occurs in.

When this happens in the thymus at a certain time of embryologic development, the result can Hashimoto’s disease or celiac disease. If it occurs at after the 8 embroylogic week of human development it will affect the developing hind brain and dentate nucleus in the cerebellum. This affects the posterior hind brain the result can be autism and its spectrum. Guess what the most common finding is in autistic brains on functional MRI? The answer is: Altered cerebellar development.

What happens to the neuro-immune system in the brain microglia and oligodendroglia in an altered field?

Once the calcium is lost from these cells it changes the resting membrane potential of cells. The resting membrane potentials of cell membranes of the microglia and supporting cells of neurons are altered immediately. It is in these cells where the brain’s hormone cascade is made from LDL cholesterol. This causes us not to make or secrete neuro-hormones on time or at optimal levels because of the calcium efflux. We get a chronic pregnenolone steal syndrome if we look at an ASI or salivary melatonin level. When calcium is released from cells we see a dramatic change in free T3 levels and vitamin A. Remember from the hormone 101 and 102 blog posts that pregnenolone steal syndrome that occurs when free T3 and vitamin A cycles are disrupted in the brain. When any stress response occurs in the brain the response it to raise LDL cholesterol, but we need to convert it to pregnenolone to make all hormones. We need T3 and vitamin A to do it. When these physiologic alterations occur the result is a cytokine storm from an the massive release of NFkappa beta and IL-6 from our cells. When these chemicals are present you can not make this conversion, nor can you activate T-helper-17 cells (supressor cells to stop the process) or T-helper- 3 cells because they are in arrested development in the thymus. Why?………because your electromagnetic field has been altered across your brainstem! The field is what causes these cells to mature and become expressed after they visit the thymus.
CIRCADIAN GEEKS: The entire outflow of the cerebral cortex must pass through the brain stem as it heads toward the spinal cord. At the bottom of the brainstem is the ponto-medullary reticular formation (PMRF) where the parasympathetic outflow of the brain is located. This allows is to calm down and digest our food naturally. Neither one works well in autoimmunity. This area is directly connected to the hindbrain in humans. The vagus nerve that connects this part of the brainstem to the gut as the major player to signal our body to rest and our gut to digest properly. This is why the SCN is entrained to light cycles alone but the gut organs which are entrained to feeding behaviors only. They communicate to the hypothalamus via the incretin system and bring their information to a different part of the hypothalamus. This may begin to explain why the Leptin Rx is built the way it is. When you eat is more important than what you eat in autoimmunity. In the upper brainstem is where the sympathetic outflow is located in a place called the midbrain reticular formation (MRF).

The MRF connects to the PVN to give us the sympathetic outflow to the adrenal glands you learned about here. This is the site of where adrenal fatigue really happens. When the PMRF is activated it automatically down regulates the PVN. THE MRF and PVN are where the stress response of cortisol, Nor Epinephrine, and epinephrine are felt body wide via the IMLCC of the thoracic spinal cord which connects to the peripheral nerves to access the the rest of the body. **Alterations of the IMLCC is why GERD occurs as the neurons in the esophageal plexus have their voltage gaited channels altered. This plexus controls the GE sphincter to acid backwash in the lower esophagus.**

It is not that hard..........your cells are giant semiconductors and you must pre load them all with energy.......if your gas tank is low your brain steal energy from other systems and when this happens it is called diseases............if it lasts too long and you never fill up with gas you die early.......and your Ferrari fails because of the part of your car that requires the most energy............It is the age old story of Robin Hood except biology style............It is not that hard.

Get curious. We all have few special talents in life, but I am passionately curious about what animates life. The only difference between me and a madman is that I’m not crazy! I never give up the quest to know why.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-05T05:22:38+0300

The master in the art of living makes little distinction between his work and his play, his labor and his leisure, his mind and his body, his information and his recreation, his love and his religion, his books or his blogs. He hardly knows which is which. He simply pursues his vision of excellence at whatever he does, leaving others to decide whether he is working or playing. To him he’s always doing both..........

# Thread: Reversing non-traditional nerve damage
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-non-traditional-nerve-damage.8342/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### balor123 — 2013-10-05T01:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reversing-non-traditional-nerve-damage.8342/post-96897>

I had severe obstructive sleep apnea (AHI 50) that went undisagnosed most of my 20s. I've been on a CPAP for a few years now and I'm somewhat better but a lot of the damage is done. To name a few: groin pain (come and go), allodynia in one spot (come and go), varicocele recurrence (come and go, after surgery), eyelids that won't fully close (seems fixed), parathesia (seems fixed), gastroparesis (seems fixed), BFS (come and go), etc. Despite treatment, nocturnal clenching remains a big problem and I have a messed up bite (anterior open bite). Anyway, I seem to have hit a plateau in my recovery. I eat a solid Palo diet but that's it. I don't take any supplements. What dietary modifications should I be looking at that I might be missing? Should I be reintroducing supplements and if so which ones? I see Dr Kruse has some nice recommendations for diabetic neuropathy but in my case I have oxidative stress neuropathy from simple lack of oxygen - that is, years of fluctuating oxygen levels while I slept. At least on my sleep studies the nadir seemed to only be about 92%.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-05T01:43:52+0300

**Participation of leptin in the determination of the macrophage phenotype: an additional role in adipocyte and macrophage crosstalk**

Macrophages exposed to leptin in culture expressed surface markers that were more similar to the M2 phenotype, but they were able to produce TNF-α, IL-6, IL-1β, IL-1ra, IL-10, MCP-1, and MIP-1α, as observed for M1 cells. Results suggest that leptin strongly contributes to the phenotype observed in macrophages found in adipose tissue.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23708919](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23708919)

### yewwei.tan — 2013-10-08T05:00:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-that-have-been-bugging-me.8345/post-97246>

Cold basically increases the DC current within cells (and hopefully we see increased current in our Schwann Cells). ie: Cold creates more usable energy for your body.

Cold adaptation is part of the process of becoming more energetically metastable (the mechanisms of which are described thoroughly in the CT series). If rT3 remains high during this period, it is due to latent leptin resistance and inflammation, which has nothing to do with cold.

Still, some people subjectively "react badly" to Cold Thermogenesis. Some reasons may be:

(a) a higher perceived level of stress due to the discomfort of being cold. When cold, you are in a more reduced state, your body has more energy, but that doesn't mean that it necessary "feels good", especially when you're just starting out

(b) toxin dump. Which is generally a good thing, though in some circumstances it may be undesirable (eg: when nursing a newborn)

(c) genetic. eg: you have a SNP in the [COMT gene](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catechol-O-methyl_transferase), and cold adaptation will be drastically slowed

(d) medications. eg: you are using beta blockers (beta 1,2,3 receptors are activated for the conversion of white fat to brown fat), and your adaptation is drastically slower.

(e) pre-existing illness. eg: you have hashimoto's (which also increases the likelihood that you're on offending medications), and your body doesn't have adequate levels of the right hormones to cold adapt

(f) other hormonal imbalances. eg: menopause, heavy antibiotic use, birth control pills, etc ... all of which can cause issues

All of these make withstanding the cold subjectively harder, but that doesn't mean that cold isn't already doing its thing and increasing your Schwann cell DC current.

Hope I got most of that right ....

### yewwei.tan — 2013-10-08T05:11:15+0300

On one hand, I'm pretty sure Jack has mentioned that when you are cold-adapted and leptin sensitive, your mitochondria have the ability to tap into uncoupling proteins to liberate calories as free heat. In theory, this means that the body can defend a specific level of fatness very well, since excess calories can be gotten rid off.

My personal experience has been that cold-adapting has allowed me to eat more (now in the 3000-3500kcal/day range, as a 6'2'' 187lbs male at 12-13% body fat. Fully ketogenic epi-paleo diet I should add).

I'm assuming that I'm cold adapted now, since body temperature rises a little after CT in sub 10C/50F water.

I should also note that I started my cold-adaptation only this June, and through the winter here in Australia. These are great conditions for cold-adaptation to take place. I'd be curious to see the effects as I approach summer time.

Maybe I started in an energy-deficient state, and after cold-adapting my body is now able to use more of the food that I consume to restore the optimal state of energy? I'm not sure, and will definitely continue to monitor my response.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-that-have-been-bugging-me.8345/post-97263>

Okay thanks for the replies.

When you say some people react badly to a higher perceived level of stress. Do you mean they feel uncomfortable/unwell OR do you mean their body reacts badly (by raising T3)?

So cold increases your DC current creating more usable energy in the body however you need more energy to burn as heat to keep yourself warm. So which one is stronger? Is the increase in DC current so good that it overcomes the bodies need for extra energy by making the current energy that more efficient. OR does the DC current only slightly increase usable energy meaning the person will have to consume more to keep them selves heated?

# Thread: Red light therapy - tanning bed?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 75
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-10-05T20:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-96957>

Anyone have experience using a tanning bed for red light therapy? I came across this Mercola bed ([http://tanningbeds.mercola.com/](http://tanningbeds.mercola.com/)) - the Vitality D-Lite bed is the one I'm considering.

Just curious if anyone has any experience/opinions on this. I emailed Mercola and they said all of their beds have electronic ballasts so the EMF levels are much lower than those found in beds with magnetic ballasts, but how do we determine if the harm from laying in this thing is worth getting the benefit from the light?! I've been reading the blogs and trying to understand as much as I can. I get the basics, but not all the underlying science . . . YET - I will get it eventually though. I'm stubborn like that.

I do want to be sure this is the way to go though before I flop down $3k!

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-05T21:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-96958>

I'm considering doing a plan with a local tanning company - we just don't have the room for a tanning bed in the house...

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-05T21:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-96960>

Regarding artificial photo-electric effect, Dr. K has stated a preference for red light over tanning beds. And regarding red light, his preference is red light up the nose over red light in a tanning bed.

About one month ago, I ordered this intranasal red light device ... [http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/](http://www.vielight.com/product/633-red/)

Each session lasts 25 minutes. Recently I moved the timing of my sessions to the during last hour or two before bed time. My sleep has improved over the last month. Definitely can't give all the credit to the red light device ... it could be the change of seasons other people have mentioned. Either way, better sleep is definitely a win.

In summary, for red light therapy, I would recommend this device. I previously used red light in a salon ... after some time, I got busy and my visits became less frequent. The convenience of having a battery powered device at home is great. And I suspect, one AA battery emits way less EMF than red light in a tanning bed.

Paleo,

Any lab result improvements from your Vielight? I was looking at a Mercola Red light tanning bed but decided to try one of these for the reasons you mention instead. Then due to my high daytime Melatonin levels and after listening to the Sept. Webinar I also bought a blue light therapy box to jumpstart my body on winter mornings up here in Canada.

I use the bluelight box in the morning when I'm getting ready to start the day then I use nasal red light device for my commute to and from work. After two weeks I find myself waking up 30-60 minutes earlier without the alarm and looking back on the last few weeks I've been more productive than normal. So qualitatively it seems to be working. The real test of course will be if I see any changes to my labs after a couple months of use.

### RickD — 2013-11-01T02:51:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-99593>

Hope,

We feed them a raw food diet called Legacy made in Alberta. It comes as a meat patty that is 80-90% human grade with some veggies mixed in it. After losing our last two Vizslas to the same form of a supposed rare cancer I decided Kibble was part of the problem so we put the new pups on it immediately with great results. We also have two older dogs that I dragged back from the Middle East years ago when I was working there and with the diet shift they both started acting years younger. Needless to say I'm a total convert. And while I'm talking about the dogs on this forum I should also mention that one of the older mutts loves the Magnetico pad, I guess she can tell it's good for her.

Our cat though is a entirely different story. 15 yrs old and down to three teeth refuses to eat anything but Fancy Feast.

BJ a few months ago on a ME/CFS forum people (most people who tried it) were running into problems using the vielight.
I settled for using it 3 mins every 3 days and had no problems but I recently got out of the habit.
I should start again and increase slowly.

I'm not sure how, but something was making people with ME worse...possibly before they might have turned a corner and made some improvement (??) it's hard to know. Most of the people trying it had moderate to severe ME/CFS and many like me had it for decades.

My advice would be to take it very slow and work up gradually. x

### bionaut — 2013-12-29T07:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-therapy-tanning-bed.8346/post-104386>

I should get some labwork done and see how these lights affect me. I own an e health nose light. I wanted a vielight for a while but the cost was prohibitive. This is surprising since it seems to simply be either a small laser or LED attached to a circuit board. The E health light shines at 650 NM and clears my nose when it is stuffy and I think very clearly after I use it. For less than a 50$ you can get a product like this that I would expect to be similar. However, the manual is translated from chinese and makes no sense... it is pretty funny actually. I don't know how they stack up against each other but I will say that this little device has a positive effect. BTW if you buy from China, expect to wait a while, shipping took about a month. I got mine off of ebay, not manafront but below is a link so you can see what I am talking about.

[http://www.manafont.com/product\_inf...eatment-for-nose-unconfort-relief-2aaa-p-7081](http://www.manafont.com/product_info.php/ehealth-superlight-therapeutic-apparatus-treatment-for-nose-unconfort-relief-2aaa-p-7081)

You can find an American marketed rhinitis stim called Bionase on amazon or ebay that seems to be the same thing. I haven't tried this one but it seems to be in the same vein.
[http://www.ebay.com/itm/BioNase-All...312?pt=LH\_DefaultDomain\_0&hash=item2585591ef8](http://www.ebay.com/itm/BioNase-Allergic-Rhinitis-Hay-Fever-Nasal-Light-Treatment-Device-Therapy-NEW-/161151000312?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item2585591ef8)

Also I own a red 660/880 LED matrix (I don't use those 2 wavelengths at the same time used at the same time) which I apply to my spine or stomach after icing to for an extra boost. I work a night shift for half the year, so I resort to some biohacks to help out. Gotta get mah photons! It has been a lifesaver.

[http://www.elixa.com/light/index.htm](http://www.elixa.com/light/index.htm)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T04:09:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-96982>

it will........it is a biggie in importance.

Here is a clue..........PER 2 directly effects the cell cycle in mitosis in the cell cycle. Mitosis is the phase in the cell that occurs just before cell division to generate an offspring. The mammalian period 2 gene plays a key role in tumor growth in mice; mice with a mPER2 knockout show a significant increase in tumor development because of significant decrease in apoptosis. Here we can see the altered circadian signaling is tied directly to oncogenesis and epigenetic modifications made in response to environmental triggers. We also now know that expression of DNA is directly tied to methylation patterns and histone acetylation patterns in DNA/RNA also directly tied to abnormal circadian signaling in both systems in mammals. **This implies the analogue and digital systems you just learned about in circadian control are massively important in brain function**. This is why most of my protocols make a big deal about timing in many fashions.

This is all caused by mPER2 circadian de-regulation of common tumor suppression and cell cycle regulation genes, such as Cyclin D1, Cyclin A, Mdm-2, and Gadd45, as well as the transcription factor c-myc, which is directly controlled by circadian cycle regulators through E box-mediated reactions.

In cases like this, **we see large changes in the cortisol, melatonin, and Vitamin D cycles** in humans epithelial cancers. This complex quantum dance implies that sleep is tied directly into to cell cycle functioning, neurologic function and directly into cell mediated immunity at a very **fundamental level in neurogenesis** at a very early stage of development for all life forms. You learned about how the **neuro-immune system is linked to brain myelination** in Energy and Epigenetics 3. There is another deeper physiologic and anatomic tie of neurogenesis and the development of immunity in humans you will learn about as the series goes on. It appears from recent research that **sleep directly effects the chronic diseases of aging, and likely plays a role in cancer, autoimmune, and neuro-degeneration development at a very fundamental level.**

### caroline — 2013-10-07T03:34:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97117>

I was thinking more about sleep last nite - mostly because I couldn't - when will I learn to stay off my ipad......

When I get drowsy - I think my heart rate slows down and my body cools down and signifies that I am ready for sleep...so then I go to bed - but then I start getting hot.\[not for the right reasons btw\] and my heart rate is speeding up \[again - not for the right reasons\] It is like I am feeling an electrical charge thru my body. I have been feeling it in my head - but last nite I realised that I feel it slightly everywhere.

I just don't understand why I would become drowsy - and then loose it.

I changed things up yesterday - I stayed at the ocean until 7:30. When I came home I poured a small glass of wine and sat outside on my balcony for about an hour. I started to yawn - so I thought great - time for bed.
The same thing happened - within 10 minutes I was very hot and there was no hope of falling asleep. I finally got up and drank a cold glass of water and took a cold shower ....my body temp came down and seemed to stay down - but still, not a chance of sleep. I then got up and had some brandy - back to bed - still wide awake... bad night.

This worries me alot now - obviously bad things are happening - losing energy......

I need to work out a new game plan for today.....

> I changed things up yesterday - I stayed at the ocean until 7:30. When I came home I poured a small glass of wine and sat outside on my balcony for about an hour. I started to yawn - so I thought great - time for bed.
> The same thing happened - within 10 minutes I was very hot and there was no hope of falling asleep. I finally got up and drank a cold glass of water and took a cold shower ....my body temp came down and seemed to stay down - but still, not a chance of sleep. I then got up and had some brandy - back to bed - still wide awake... bad night.

In my personal n=1 ... alcohol and quality sleep don't mix. .... For that reason, I stopped drinking alcohol years ago.

My party of one oxy always helped...put me right out, and for quite a while i couldnt fall asleep without it. I called it masturbation meditation...lol Now it seems I can get to sleep without that magic bullet. I think that is a good thing.

Hubby and i are actually in separate beds. We get along better when we sleep and it seems one of us is always either restless or snoring and disturbing the other so he bought me a queen memory foam bed and I sleep across the room from him. Our goal is to get back together eventually but we are better parents and partners if we dont spend the night shushing each other.

I really think it is that memory foam. I am thinking of buying this cool gel pad thing and see if it helps. Will let you know

### Amy — 2013-10-08T16:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97281>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=97253)
>
> I think Ketosis is going to be huge - but not really sure why exactly ....I need to think about this.....

well , we know that ketones are a great brain fuel: more atp, clean burn, possibly the preferred fuel, etc..... we know that ketosis works for refractory seizures and there are case reports and a few small studies showing benefit in autism, Alzheimer's and possibly neurodegenerative disease and most everyone reports sharper cognition and sense of energy/well-being. the confusing part for me is that many people, changing no other variables other than starting a ketogenic diet, often report sleep going to crap. It is a common complaint and i can't yet make sense of it.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-08T17:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97284>

Ok, this is just me...

I think everyone has to rethink how we sleep. Today we spend one of the most important time of day sleeping inside a building with soft nice beds and way to little natural resonance. Even if we try to do it with magnets and grounding tools we crave the powers of natural. Every time i feel off sync i sleep in the wild, on the ground with as little stuff as possible. I think we are not made to lie in a bed that forms it shape after us, i think we should sleep on the ground with all the "nasty" stuff because then we compensate with our bodies.

One big thing is also that we should not sleep in the exact same place day after day, we should shift places. When you do this we eliminate bad signals from the environment, think nomad. That way of sleeping today is killing us when it should rebuild us.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-08T19:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97292>

> [Amy said:](/goto/post?id=97281)
>
> well , we know that ketones are a great brain fuel: more atp, clean burn, possibly the preferred fuel, etc..... we know that ketosis works for refractory seizures and there are case reports and a few small studies showing benefit in autism, Alzheimer's and possibly neurodegenerative disease and most everyone reports sharper cognition and sense of energy/well-being. the confusing part for me is that many people, changing no other variables other than starting a ketogenic diet, often report sleep going to crap. It is a common complaint and i can't yet make sense of it.

The answer to this is easy yet complex but buried in the science of EE6. When you eat ketogenic you make more ATP.......36 vs 147 (glucose to fat) ATP function in a zero entropy quantum cell theory opens proteins structure up to expose more water binding sites in proteins.......the amount of ATP is stochastically linked to potassium concentration inside the cell. This is why potassium is inside the cell and sodium is not. It is a function of protein and ATP levels. So if you all of sudden eat ketotic, but you are dehydrated (high BUN/creat ratio) you can not fully take advantage of the energy surge.......see water is designed to bind to very specific parts of proteins (coo- groups)........potassium to other sites (beta and gamma carbonyl and imino groups). This make an "intracellular gelatin" that limits the rotational freedom of K and water. This is how a cell structures its water from bulk water to structured water to make a semiconductor. Structure water means to Ling that it lines up in an antiparallel fashion end to but and end to put. This puts the more electronegative side containing oxygen to the next two hydrogen atoms. Ling erred a bit because structured water can't be a dipole because dipole have neutral changes and we now know that the EZ has a net negative charge from Pollack's experiments. This allows water to become a superconductive cable in a cell to transfer energy and it allows water to gain a three dimensional form of a liquid crystalline structure of matter to perform quantum magic in tight spaces like microtubules or around mitochondria in the MINOS........so if you are dehydrated and then switch fuels you have only done step one correctly. Go back and look at the CT protocol closely........what does it say to due before CT? It says drink cold 32 oz of water before you begin!!! Why? what good is using cold to increase your current of flow if you dont have the water to make the superconducting cables to begin with? Got it?

ATP function is not its use of a high energy substrate as modern cell theory teaches.......ATP is designed to unfold proteins fully to open their carbonyl and imino side chain groups to intracellular water. to make them more hydrophilic. This allows water at the interface or surface to form an EZ that acts like a batter for light. ATP has zero to do with energy to live. (not totally accurate but the point is being made) ATP's main job in a cell is to opens proteins to bind water which transmits the electrons and photons and solitons you learned about in EE6. This is where the energy in a cell comes from. This is why people who go ketotic lose sleep efficiency because they are all dehydrated. If your proteins are missing electrons they are hydrophobic and they can't form an EZ. This is why your hormone labs are often useless. You need to know if the hormone is filled with electrons or lacking them to know what energy balance really is. And your observation of poor sleep with ketosis proves my idea that ATP is not a high energy anything.......if it was, ketosis should make you a zombie and in fact, it does not. Water is the key ingredient for sleep......and the Ocotober webinar is going to simply stun you to that reality.........in 3 D........your jaw will be left open.

Once the first water layer is laid down at a surface in this fashion other water layers layer on top more EZ thickness can be made. When water is not in EZ form the hydrogen bonding network effects its structure because water is a natural dipole, and dipole's within the Earth's magnetic field act differently than they do in a cell. Tap water and cell water are not equal players. Non native EMF affects this step in 2 ways, it dehydrates your cells, and it alters the hydrogen bonding arrangement due to the fact that water is a small magnet. When water layers around a protein it can becomes a reverse micelle and self organize or be fortified by a large EZ to exclude protons. these things alternate between day and night. This is why DNA and RNA only code for protein in all life forms and why it is highly conserved from simple life to complex life....... the key nucleation point (origin point) for this water crystal is built into the carbonyl and imino side chains of the proteins coded for. Water is a primordial thing found on Earth therefore it did not need to be coded for on DNA. It was used by DNA because it is the perfect magnetic dipole that is capable of allowing from proton tunneling that enzymes need to work. The reason potassium is critical and not Magnesium or sodium inside a cell ties to beta and gamma carboxyl groups also in proteins where potassium binds too. This bond allows K+ to ADSORB to this site and donate electrons to the water gel. Each molecule of ATP in a quantum cell controls 8800 water molecules and 20 potassium ions to make this liquid crystalline semiconductor inside every cell of your body. Potassium acts like the glue to keep your back bone intracellular semiconducting plates together. This is why when any life form dies.......potassium and water are released from a cell and salt linkage form between muscles because no more energy is present within the water supercables. Notice this is where Magnesium is bound up in salt linkages and shows you why it is not a major cation in energy transfers......it must have water to act. Mg2+ most important role is to make ATP in the mitochondria as a co factor........but as I said earlier.......without water........ATP or Magnesium are worthless and this is why one can still sleep like shit when you are in deep ketosis. This is also why people who are energy deficient, obese or diabetics have low potassium, high BUN/creat ratios and urinate a lot of water.......with their magnesium in it for the ride..............When you understand QED all the diseases make sense. DETAILS MATTER IN QUANTUM BIOLOGY

### freesia — 2013-10-09T01:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97316>

Funny you should ask about sleep because I think I'm adusting to the season shift and last week I woke in the night a couple of times! But this is unusual. It used to be a regular thing.

The best ways I can describe my sleep improvements in the past two years is:
\*I have no trouble _ever_ with falling asleep.
\*If I do wake in the night I'm not crushed the next day. By crushed I mean feeling like I'm physically and mentally pushing through concrete, with carbs suddenly and seductively appearing on the radar as a quick-fix "solution".
\*Sleep does actually feel restorative. _Antioxidative_ and _cooling_ is how best to describe how night time feels. I wake at sunrise feeling good. Magic.

Iced water and CT - more like 16 oz works for me, or 2 big glasses. I sip a glass while I'm in the bath...I clink the ice cubes and imagine I'm at a resort.  I did drink more initially, but as the time in the bath got longer I cut back on the volume to prevent needing to get in and out for toilet breaks!

Not sure what you mean about Yahoo? Do you mean groups??

### Inger — 2013-10-09T07:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sleep-webinar.8347/post-97322>

Wow, I slept so well tonight. I really have learned paleo treats are not good for me!

Yesterday I had zero nuts or carbs out of season, just cucumber and dirty carrots from own garden, wild lingonberries, stinging nettles and mint from the garden as a smoothie with water, seaweed smoothie, raw reindeer, raw lambs liver and meat from own lambs, organic Norwegian salmon, raw. For added fat 1 raw eggyolk, EVOO and coconut oil. Finished dinner by 5 PM. Took care to drink lots of own well water that had been out in the rain in glass jugs for hours. Had celtic sea salt in quite many glasses of it too.... because I craved it.
Huge difference in sleep to the night before, just amazing! I grounded as much as the day before, and did as much CT too (2 short swims in the river) so it was only food, the timing of my last meal and water intake that was different.

Now I really get it clearly. Inflammation -> dehydration-> bad sleep.......

Maybe to get healthy really is that easy

DH always had amazing sleep abilities... and he is very healthy too. He is an animal..lol I now understand why!

# Thread: Anyone familiar with DISH or osteoarthritis with calcification/bone spurs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-10-06T05:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/post-96985>

I feel like anything I know about osteoarthritis is preventative....I am out of my league here.

My mother in law is the throes of this stuff diagnosed with DISH Diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis (DISH) , also known as Forestier-Rotes-Querol disease, and I want to help her but don't even know where to start and/or if it is too late.

I would really appreciate any knowledge y'all could share on this subject as there is very little on the internet aside from pain killers and surgery.

I just can't tell a woman in pain to go to the lengths and expense we all go to if it is too late to benefit her.

Thanks so much.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-06T20:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-familiar-with-dish-or-osteoarthritis-with-calcification-bone-spurs.8349/post-97064>
